DAAG.name: Houari Boumediene International
DAAG.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Houari Boumediene International
Ground Handling: SWISSPORT - NIL
Terminal: 4
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 H24 YES YES YES NIL YES
DAAG.atc:
WARNING Traffic separation between VFR and IFR flight is not provided. Not all traffic may
be displayed on TCAS.
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Low transition altitude.
DAAG.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.RWY 05/23 Centerline and touchdown markings are faded and difficult to see,
especially during hours of darkness or adverse weather conditions.
2.RWY 09 ILS localizer deviations are possible when the ILS critical areas are
not protected. Be alert for localizer course deviations.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• STARs not published, expect radar vectors.
• Do not accept visual approach clearance until landing RWY is correctly identified.
RWY 23
• Localizer deviation to the right has been reported with autopilot engaged.
RWY 05 and 27
• NIL ALS.
DAAG.ground_maneuvering:
TWYs
• Flight Crew should exercise extreme caution at the intersection area of TWYs A2, A3, A4, H1
and H2 due to complex configuration of TWYs.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Refuelling Procedures. Due to fuel gauge position and as well as standard fuelling pipe length,
the fuelling vehicle parks very close to the aircraft. Safety cones must be placed one meter
away from engine and the area must be manned throughout the whole process.
• Parking stand identification boards are faded and difficult to read.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
DAAG.departure_procedures:
Refuelling with Pax Onboard
• Fire trucks must be present at the aircraft.
Planning
• RWY Preference for departure: RWY05 and RWY27.
• SID is normally cancelled after take-off. Ensure all the radar vectors provide adequate terrain
clearance.
DAAG.miscellaneous:
• In case of a bird strike, ATC must be promptly advised. The PIC is required to complete the
prescribed documentation provided by ATC. The completed documentation is required to
facilitate the release of the aircraft for subsequent operations. Failure to submit the documents
will result in operational delays.

FNLU.name: Luanda - 4th of February
FNLU.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Luanda - 4th of February
Ground Handling: GHASSIST
Terminal: TBA
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 H24 NIL YES YES NIL NO
CAUTION Birds and wildlife activity on and in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
Note: RWY 07/25 – NOT AUTHORIZED for take-off and landing.
FNLU.company_policy:
• The inbound Flight Crew are required to remain onboard until the aircraft is formally handed
over to the outbound Flight Crew or to an authorized ground engineer during the ground
handling procedures with passengers on board.
FNLU.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in vicinity of aerodrome.
2.Luanda has a busy TMA with high volume of VFR traffic, including helicopters.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponders may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
• SIDs and STARs are not published.
• No Radar services. Procedural separation with the use of DME and radials to separate arrivals
and departing traffic.
Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• Use standard and simple R/T phraseology, speak slowly and clearly to avoid any confusion.
• ATC may communicate in French with other carriers. As a result, situational awareness of the
surrounding traffic may be hampered.
• Communication may be difficult to establish at FIR entry points.
• The following additional HF Frequencies are available within the Luanda FIR: 2851, 2854, 5565,
6559, 8888, 11291, 13294, 13315.
FNLU.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Exercise vigilance when conducting VOR approach, it has been reported as
offset while in the charts and AIP it is depicted as aligned.
2.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake converging RWY
for landing RWY.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Delays for descent may be expected for inbound traffic.
RWY 05
• NIL ALS and PAPI.
• VOR DME approach is offset.
RWY 23
• PAPI has been reported as dim.
• VOR DME and NDB approaches are offset.
FNLU.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION 1.Aircraft Landing/taking off on RWY 05/23 will be required to cross RWY 07/25
to enter/exit the Apron. Request clearance to cross RWY 07/25 and exercise
vigilance of aircraft movements on RWY 07/25 before crossing.
2.People have been observed crossing the RWY.
3.FOD has been reported in TWY and Apron areas.
TWYs
• Entry/exit the apron via TWY B only.
• Pot holes and patches have been reported in section of TWY A between TWY A1 and TWY B,
exercise vigilance.
• TWYs F and G are not named on Jeppesen Charts. Refer below graphic.
TWYs
Apron/Parking Stands
• Follow me vehicle available on request.
• Uneven surface and fuel/oil spillage spots on the Apron.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• Hand signals are used for chocks placement and for engine start by the handling agent.
FNLU.departure_procedures:
Startup and Pushback
• Request ATC clearance 20 minutes prior to taxi.
• Start clearance should include requested flight level, endurance, fuel and persons on board.
• ATC clearance is usually given during taxi-out.
• Engine start clearance, to obtain from pushback crew.
Planning
• Take-off from RWY 23, expect to intercept a radial to a distance/height and then proceed on the
flight plan route.
RWY 23
• Non-standard holding point marking, exercise vigilance and follow ATC instruction.
• Below graphic for reference only.
RWY 23 Holding Point
FNLU.miscellaneous:
Cargo Operations
Aerodrome is prone to high number of mosquitoes entering the cockpit:
• Keep the L1 door closed during the transit time.
• Keep the cockpit area isolated with the curtain.
• Use the provided repellent after all doors have been closed.

HDAM.name: Ambouli International
HDAM.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Ambouli International
Ground Handling: AIR DJIBOUTI
Terminal: TBA
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
8 H24 YES NO NO YES YES
CAUTION Wildlife activity on and in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
HDAM.company_policy:
• Operating Captain shall have minimum six (6) months experience on type/variant (after
FLC/Command Release Check).
• ILS RWY 27 is the preferred approach up to the maximum tail wind limitation specified in the
aircraft FCOM (Performance permitting).
• RWY 09: All approaches to be flown by Captains or Captains under training only.
• Uplift of potable water is not permitted from Djibouti.
HDAM.atc:
WARNING Traffic separation between VFR and IFR flight is not provided. Not all traffic may
be displayed on TCAS.
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Djibouti aerodrome is located 8 NM North of the border with Somalia.
3.Military aircraft, Drones and Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) activity in the
vicinity of the aerodrome.
4.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
5.Low Transition Altitude.
General
• Djibouti airspace below FL240 is a non-radar environment and controlled by Djibouti Tower.
Above FL240 is controlled by Addis Ababa Control.
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponders may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
HDAM.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWY for
landing RWY.
RWY 09
• NIL ALS.
• PAPI angle is steeper than normal.
• RNAV (GNSS) Z approach descent angle not coincident with PAPI.
RWY 27
• RNAV (GNSS) and VOR approaches descent angle are not coincident with PAPI.
• Flight Crew to identify the correct RWY 27 DTHR as the old RWY Threshold/touchdown zone
markings are visible on the final approach. See below pictorial for reference only.
RWY 27 Threshold/Touchdown Zone Markings
Local Procedures
• Flight Crew to contact Djibouti TWR 10 minutes prior entering Djibouti airspace.
• During sand storms, Flight Crews must request approach and RWY lights ON.
HDAM.ground_maneuvering:
RWYs
• RWY 27 has a hump between threshold and displaced threshold.
TWYs
• TWYs South of RWY 09/27:
‐ Available for military traffic and can be used only with ATC approval.
‐ Flight Crew can be instructed to change frequency from 122.1 (TWR) to 124.9 (Southern
GND), when cleared to vacate the RWY to the South.

4.Low transition altitude.

HECA.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWYs for

RWY 05C

RWY 23L
• VOR approach is offset.
HECA.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION 1.RWY 05C/23C and RWY 05R/23L are on elevated ground resulting in TWYs
with significant upslope/downslope. When landing, plan RWY exit so as to use
minimum braking. Use proper braking technique and consider single engine
taxi to avoid brakes overheat. If installed, use brake fans as required.
2.During the taxi phase, apply even braking and monitor the brakes temperature.
3.Do not mistake service road located between J1 and J2 for TWYs J1 and J2
while taxiing on TWY G or TWY J.
TWYs
• All TWYs have significant upslope towards the South.
• Poor lighting has been reported.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Poor lighting in the Apron area stands 15 to 18 has been reported.
HECA.departure_procedures:
General

EOBT.
• RWY 23R holding point location can be easily missed from the first officers seating position.
HECA.miscellaneous:
General
• Commanders are required to conduct transit checks if required.
• This is an AFP sector and the OFP will be generated automatically.

HAAB.general:

Ground Handling: International Cargo and Aviation Services
Terminal: 2
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 H24 YES YES YES YES NO
CAUTION 1.High Density Altitude Aerodrome Operations.
2.Weather balloons are launched twice daily in the aerodrome (03:00 to 06:00
and 10:00 to 13:00).
HAAB.company_policy:
• All take-offs and landings shall be performed by Captains or a Captain under training only.
• Operating Captain shall have minimum six (6) months experience on type/variant (after
FLC/Command Release Check).
• Maximum tailwind limit of 10Kt.
Note: The above restrictions are not applicable to Emergencies or Medical diversions.
HAAB.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Low transition altitude.
Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• ATC requires the total number of persons on board during the first contact with Tower during the
approach and during the first contact with Ground Control for departure.
Nav-aids
• Remote monitoring of NAV AIDs by ATC is not available.
RWY Preference
• Expect to received frequent RWY change instructions from ATC.
• RWY 07R/25L is main RWY for landing and takeoff.
HAAB.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Both RWY ends have steep drop-offs.
2.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel RWY for
landing RWY.
3.RWY centerline markings are faded and difficult to see, especially during hours
of darkness or adverse weather conditions.
4.RWYs and TWYs are poorly lit.
General
• Aircraft positioning during approach is limited due to CBs build up late afternoons and evening,
combined with high terrain. This could result in arrival delays.
RWY 25R
• VOR approach is offset.
HAAB.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION Animals have been observed in all maneuvering areas at the aerodrome. Maintain
a continuous watch and report as necessary to ATC.
General
• Significant elevation variation throughout the aerodrome.
• Due to the tall grass within the vicinity of TWY, it is difficult to determine the TWY signs.
RWYs
• RWY has a pronounced up-slope towards midpoint.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Apron
‐ Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the
proximity of other aircraft, equipment and personnel, especially:
▪when wide-body aircraft are parked on stands S1 or N1 to N10.
▪for code E: when turning left from TWY B towards Cargo Apron.
If any doubt exists, request Marshaller/Wing walkers.
‐ Main Apron. Southern section of the apron, between parking stands S7 and S13, have been
reported as having upslope towards final parking position. Flight Crew should exercise proper
energy management.
‐ Apron surface is reported as slippery when wet, exercise extreme vigilance.
‐ Parking Stands: Stop bars are not painted for any aircraft type. The parking position is
coordinated by the marshaller to ensure adequate clearance between fuelling truck and
aircraft engine.
HAAB.departure_procedures:
Refuelling with Pax onboard
• Flight Crews must inform ATC before start refuelling with pax on board.

WARNING Traffic separation between VFR and IFR flight is not provided. Not all traffic may

• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.

before being released by Lagos and vice versa.
Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)

period.
RWY Preference
• RWY 21 is the preferential RWY for take-off and landing.
DGAA.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Turbulence and strong crosswinds may be encountered on final approach.
2.Power lines cross the final approach of RWY 21.
3.RWY centerline markings are faded and difficult to see, especially during hours
of darkness or adverse weather conditions.
4.RWYs and TWYs are poorly lit.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Expect handover to approach radar at 60 NM. Either radar vectors or procedural service is
provided.
NAV-AIDS
• Remote monitoring of Nav-aids by ATC is not available.
RWY 03
• VOR Y approach descent angle not coincident with PAPI .
RWY 21
• Expect radar vectors to KEDOS regardless of approach flown. KEDOS is located 10 NM on final
approach to RWY 21 and is the IF for RNAV approach RWY 21.
• Unusual surface profile, initially upslope followed by a marked downslope.
DGAA.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION FOD have been reported on RWY and TWYs.
General
• TWYs and apron to the East of RWY 03/21 – NOT AUTHORIZED.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Follow me vehicle available on request.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• Stop bars are not painted for any aircraft type.
• Taxi lights must be switched off before final turn to parking stand.
DGAA.departure_procedures:
Refuelling with Pax On-board
• Fire truck must be present at the aircraft.
Startup and Push-back
• ATC requires total of persons on board and endurance on request to start.
DGAA.miscellaneous:
General
Note: Flight Crew are requested to share the MiFi device password with the riding engineer to
access the internet for sending the logs to base.
• A Customs Declaration Form is required to be filled-up inbound to Accra giving specific
information about goods to declare (i.e. laptops, phones, wrist-watch etc. instead of just
personal effects).
• For maintenance duties, a riding engineer may travel on-board the aircraft as a passenger on
sector ACC-ABJ-ACC and will be included in the GD as operating crew due to traffic rights
restrictions.
Note: DOW/DOI will be adjusted.
• If no maintenance engineer is available in ABJ, Commanders are required to conduct the transit
check.

CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations on and in the vicinity of the
aerodrome.
3.Low transition altitude.
Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• Deviations from standard phraseology have been reported in the past.
Radar Vectoring
• Radar service available, outside UTA/TMA radar monitoring available.
RWY Preference
• RWY 06 preferred RWY in use.
Local Procedures
• Payload and total people on board are required by ATC upon arrival and departure.
HKJK.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Turbulence and strong crosswinds may be encountered on final approach.
2.RWY centerline markings are faded and difficult to see, especially during hours
of darkness or adverse weather conditions.
3.RWY and TWY lights have been reported as dim.
General
• RWY edge lighting is positioned 7.5m from the RWY edge.
Stars/Radar Vectoring
• Expect radar vectors to final approach.
HKJK.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION FODs have been reported in Apron 2.
TWYs
• Several TWYs have upslope, especially on TWY K and E towards the RWY, on TWY J and
H towards TWY G, southbound on TWY G abeam cargo stand F3. Exercise proper energy
management to prevent the aircraft from stopping.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• Marshaller service available, contact apron control.
HKJK.departure_procedures:
Startup and Pushback
• ATC clearance, request 10 minutes prior to EOBT.
• Startup clearance: Contact TWR.
• To taxi from Apron 1 to holding point at TWY H and J: Contact Apron Control.
• To taxi from Apron 2 to holding point at TWY K: Contact TWR immediately after pushback is
complete and the aircraft is ready for taxiing.
• Refer to Jeppesen chart 10-9 for details.
HKJK.miscellaneous:
Cargo Operations
Aerodrome is prone to high number of mosquitoes entering the cockpit:
• Keep the L1 door closed during the transit time.
• Keep the cockpit area isolated with the curtain.
• Use the provided repellent after all doors have been closed.

General

RWY Preference

Local Procedures

aircraft movements on RWY 16/34 before crossing.

• RWY surface is bumpy.

Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• Apron surface has been reported as rough.

• All parking stands are power-in/out. The aircraft will be marshalled to park nose out.
• Taxi slowly to the parking stand since Marshaller may request to stop suddenly.

• After take-off, expect radar vectors to join the airway.

Note: All Crew (Flight Deck and Cabin), while boarding and de-boarding, must wear a high
visibility reflective jacket that will be supplied by the station staff.

Additionally, an electronic ASR must be filed as per normal procedure.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.

FCBB.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Kinshasa (FZAA/FIH) aerodrome is located 14 NM to the Southeast of
Brazzaville aerodrome and the ATC have special procedures to coordinate the
traffic operating in both aerodromes.
3.Brazzaville has a busy TMA with high volume of VFR traffic.
4.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
5.NAVAIDs are not monitored by ATC, therefore NAVAIDs may be inoperative
without NOTAM notification or ATC knowing the actual status.
6.LOC identification may not be available.
7.Low transition altitude.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponders may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
HF
• Communication may be difficult to establish at FIR entry points.
• IATA IFBP (In Flight Broadcast Procedure) apply in Brazzaville FIR.
FCBB.arrival_procedures:

T (old RWY marked with X on each RWY threshold) for landing RWY.

during hours of darkness or adverse weather conditions.
3.RWY lights and PAPI have been reported as very dim

• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due to the proximity

Marshaller/Wingwalker.

• Start clearance should include requested flight level, endurance, fuel and persons on board.
• ATC clearance is usually given during taxi-out.

Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 H24 YES YES YES NIL NO
CAUTION 1.High Density Altitude Aerodrome Operations.
2.Birds in vicinity of aerodrome and maneuvering area. Birds strike most common
in April and December.
Note: All Crew (Flight Deck and Cabin) while boarding and deboarding must wear a high
visibility reflective jacket that will be supplied by the station staff.
HRYR.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Low Transition Altitude.
General

Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• Controllers English accent may be difficult to understand. Use standard and simple R/T
phraseology, speak slowly and clearly to avoid any confusion.
HRYR.arrival_procedures:
General
• As a result of weather avoidance, aircraft can be left high on approach. Flight Crew need to pay
attention to descent profile, TAS and GND speed due elevation.
Nav AIDs
• Check for availability prior to commencing approach.
RWY 10
• NIL ALS.
RWY 28

• RA readings on final approach can be misleading.

TWYs

Apron/Parking Stands

Marshaller/Wingwalker.

Startup and Pushback

• Expect pushback to the holding/exit point.
Planning

due to terrain.

FSIA.name: Seychelles International
FSIA.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Seychelles International.
Ground Handling: SEYCHELLES OPS – 121.700.
Terminal: TBA
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 H24 YES YES YES NIL NO
Note: RWY 13/31 closed every Monday 18:15 to Tuesday 02:45 for routine maintenance. RWY
can be re-opened within 30 minutes for emergencies.
FSIA.company_policy:
• All take-offs and landings shall be performed by Captain or Captain under Training only.
• Operating Captain shall have a minimum of six (6) months experience on type/variant (after
FLC/Command Release Check).
• Aerodrome is available as ETOPS for flights operating to/from Seychelles only.
• RNP approach for RWY 13: In order to comply with company stabilization criteria, align aircraft
with runway centreline as soon as MDA is reached and runway is in sight.
• Reactive and Predictive wind shear detection function must be operative before departure from
DOHA.
• GPWS and TAWS Functions must be operative before departure from DOHA.
FSIA.atc:
WARNING Traffic separation between VFR and IFR flight is not provided. Not all traffic may
be displayed on TCAS.
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (Drones) activity in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
General
• No Radar environment.
Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• SATPHONE telephone numbers: (248) 4384193 / 4384000 or INMARSAT 466401 are available
for Seychelles ATC as a backup for HF/VHF communications difficulties.
FSIA.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.RWY centerline markings are faded and difficult to see, especially during hours
of darkness or adverse weather conditions.
2.RWY and TWYs have been reported as poorly lit.
Note: Flight Crew should monitor ETA to mitigate early arrivals into Seychelles to avoid arrival
delays due to departing traffic.
General
• Due to lack of surrounding lighting in vicinity of the aerodrome, a “black hole” effect may be
experienced during night operations.
RWY 13/31
CAUTION Transverse white stripe identifying the end of the displaced threshold is not
available(see graphic).
RWY 13/31 Aiming Point and missing DTHR transverse
RWY 13
• RNP approach is offset and descent angle is not coincident with PAPI.
• RNP X. Missed approach requires higher than standard climb gradient. If unable to comply,
advise ATC as soon as possible.
• The following procedure should be applied when RWY 13 is in use, but Flight Crew require the
approach to RWY 31:
1.Request Expected Approach Time (EAT) from ATC.
2.Expect to continue on the STAR for RWY 13 until the IAF.
3.Depending on traffic, ATC may:
• Request holding at the IAF (MALON) or
• Issue a clearance to proceed and hold at the IAF (PETER) of RWY 31 for an ILS Z RWY
31 approach or
• Issue a clearance to proceed to the IAF (JERAD) for an RNP Y RWY 31 approach.
4.Expect to remain at or above the relevant MSA.
RWY 31
CAUTION 1.Glide Slope for ILS 31 fluctuates in the last 300ft.
2.When visual intercept the PAPI Glide Slope. Do not follow the approach Glide
Slope/Glide Path below the DA/MDA.
• RNP approach is offset.
• The approach is over water and with Northwest winds, there can be funneling effect due to the
two hills and other high terrain adjacent to the threshold, creating downdraft and to a lesser
extent, turbulence.
FSIA.ground_maneuvering:
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• When entering apron via TWY B, no taxi lines to parking stand 6.
FSIA.departure_procedures:
Pre-Flight Inspection (Walk Around)
• Birds have been observed nesting in the Air Conditioning Pack bay ventilation intake area,
engine cowlings and exhausts.
• Bugs found in the pitot tube.
• During extended ground time (more than 8 hrs) in Seychelles and in order to reduce such
events, maintenance will apply the following:
‐ Install a blanking cover (with red “REMOVE BEFORE FLIGHT” streamer) to cover the Air
Conditioning PACK bay ventilation NACA inlet.
‐ Make an entry in the technical log book.
‐ Place a “Do not operate tag” in the cockpit near to pack(s) switches (to be removed before
flight).
• Flight crew when performing the external walk around are required to ensure the following:
‐ Installed tag and covers are removed before operating the Packs.
‐ Pitot covers are removed.
Startup and Push-back
• Contact TWR 10 minutes prior startup for ATC clearance.
• ATC requires passengers on board (POB) and fuel endurance when requesting push-back.
Planning
• No Takeoff alternates are available.
FSIA.miscellaneous:
SPRAYING PROCEDURES – INBOUND
• Cabin Crew will liaise with Flight Crew to complete spraying of the aircraft prior to push-back
from Doha (after all doors are closed).
• On arrival in Seychelles, flight deck windows or exterior doors of the aircraft may not be opened
without prior authorization from Airport Health officials.
Note: The main cabin door may only be opened to permit entry of the Public Health Officer to
conduct a pre-disembarkation inspection.
DENGUE FEVER
• Dengue Fever is present in Seychelles and carried by mosquitos, the disease results in very
severe health complications.
• Captains are to brief the crew to comply with the below:
1.Avoid going out at night.
2.Avoid sitting under trees for a long time.
3.Apply mosquito repellent at all times.
4.Do not leave windows open in your room.
5.Avoid visiting the mountain.
6.In case of any symptoms of severe muscle pain or skin irritation, immediately contact the
nearest doctor and advise QR Station Manager as mentioned on the crew information.

FACT.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspaces in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponders may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
FACT.arrival_procedures:
RWY 01
• VOR Z approach is offset and descent angle not coincident with PAPI.
FACT.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION RWY 16 and RWY 19 thresholds are closely located.
TWYs
• TWYs H, T, Z and to the East of RWY 01/19 – NOT AUTHORIZED.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Parking Stand Allocation - Inbound: Contact Cape Town Apron to confirm parking stand.
• Parking Stand A3 VDGS has been reported as difficult to see due to low brightness.
• Apron A: Stands have upslope towards final parking position. Flight Crew should exercise
proper energy management to prevent aircraft from stopping.
FACT.departure_procedures:
General
• ATC flight plan is valid for 1 hour after ETD. Thereafter, a new ATC flight plan must be
submitted unless original flight plan ETD was modified.

FAOR.name: O. R. Tambo International
FAOR.general:
Name of Aerodrome: O. R. Tambo International.
Ground Handling:
• Pax/Cargo: MENZIES AVIATION – 129.975.
Terminal: A.
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 H24 NIL YES YES NIL NO
CAUTION 1.High Density Altitude Aerodrome Operations.
2.Weather balloons launch to the Northwest of the aerodrome. Exercise vigilance
during landing and take-off.
FAOR.company_policy:
• Approach and landing shall be performed by Captain or Captain under training only.
• Operating Captain shall have a minimum of six (6) months experience on type/variant (after
FLC/Command Release Check).
FAOR.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations on and in the vicinity of the
aerodrome.
3.Low transition altitude.
General
• ATC control is limited to 11 NM North of the aerodrome due to military controlled airspace.
FAOR.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Johannesburg-Rand (FAGM) aerodrome is located 6 NM to the Southwest
and Brakpan-Benoni (FABB) located 6 NM to the southeast Johannesburg
(FAOR/JNB) International.
2.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWYs for
landing RWY.
RWY 03R
• Preferred RWY for arrivals.
• Northwest winds on ground may produce tailwind on approach.
• Radar vectors often reduce the track distance, leaving the aircraft on high and fast profile.
• RWY has pronounced hump and may give optical illusion for landing flare.
RWY 03L
• Advised via ATC or ATIS when in use for arriving traffic.
RWY 21R
• VOR approaches descent angle not coincident with PAPI.
Planning
• Expect clearance for ILS Z approaches.
FAOR.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION 1.When cleared to cross CAT II holding points on TWY A or TWY B towards
holding point on RWY 03L, do not confuse the green centerline lights as a
clearance to enter the RWY. If in doubt request clarification from ATC.
2.Animals (Dogs) observed on the ground maneuvering areas.
TWYs
• Judgemental steering required on:
‐ TWY Exit RS – Southbound turn to TWY Y or vice versa.
‐ TWY Exit RR – Northbound turn to TWY Y or vice versa
• TWY A has a significant up-slope from North to South.
• TWY E has up-slope from TWY A towards the apron. Flight crew should exercise proper energy
management to prevent aircraft from stopping.
• Due to reduced wingtip clearance, do not taxi behind an aircraft holding short of the RWY at an
intersection TWY. Stop the aircraft and advise ATC.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• When Visual Docking Guidance System fails or is unserviceable, Flight Crew must request Wing
Walker from Apron Control, even if marshalling services are provided.
• Parking stands D3A and D4A are indicated by dashed lead-in lines at 45° angle.
FAOR.departure_procedures:
General
• ATC flight plan is valid for 1 hour after ETD. Thereafter, a new ATC flight plan must be
submitted unless original flight plan ETD was modified.
• Preferred RWY for departure: RWY 03L.
Startup and Pushback
• Flow Control:
‐ Flown control procedures are in force daily. A CTOT shall be issued based on EOBT.
‐ Any change to EOBT, +/- 15 minutes should be advised to ATC.
• Recommendations to avoid potential delays:
‐ Contact Clearance Delivery in advance of ETD to obtain departure clearance.
‐ Pushback: Contact Apron Control in advance.
FAOR.miscellaneous:
General
Note: Flight Crew are requested to share the MiFi device password with the riding engineer to
access the internet for sending the logs to base.
• For maintenance duties, a riding engineer may travel on-board the aircraft as a passenger on
sector JNB-DUR-JNB and will be included in the GD as operating crew due to traffic rights
restrictions.
Note: DOW/DOI will be adjusted.
• If no maintenance engineer is available in DUR, Commanders are required to conduct transit
checks.

HSSK.name: Khartoum International
HSSK.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Khartoum International
Ground Handling:
• PAX - MAKS Aviation
• Cargo - NASPORT Air Handling Company
Terminal: Apron 2 or 3
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 H24 NIL YES YES NIL NO
CAUTION Birds and animal activity on and in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
Note: Before departing to Khartoum with APU INOP, confirm with MOCC/IOCC that Khartoum
station can provide the ground support equipment for the aircraft type.
HSSK.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Low transition altitude.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponders may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
• ATC services have been reported as poor (generally) for both aerodrome and en-route.
• Hajj Season - Flight Crew awareness should be heightened during Hajj season due to
significant increase in traffic within Khartoum FIR during this period.
• Radar services are available within 50 NM of the aerodrome.
Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• Communication "black- spots" have been reported within the Khartoum FIR.
HSSK.arrival_procedures:
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Arrival from the North, ATC may offer direct routing to RWY 36 "IAF".
Nav-aids
• Due to navigation and landing aids often being subject to unserviceability without prior NOTAM
information from the Airport Authorities, Flight Crew should request the RNAV approach instead
of an ILS for RWY 18.
• VORs have been reported as unreliable with signal fluctuations.
• PAPIs have been reported as unreliable.
• RWY 18:
‐ Regular false LOC captures have been reported.
‐ Glide Path signal fluctuating.
• RWY 36:
‐ Approach lights have been reported as dim.
HSSK.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION 1.Many incidents during ground maneuvering have taken place at Khartoum
aerodrome due to:
• Badly maintained surfaces with poor/faded markings and signs.
• Incorrectly parked aircraft leading to insufficient wing-tip clearance, resulting
in ground collision.
• Flight Crew should exercise vigilance during all ground maneuvering and if in
doubt, stop aircraft and request ground assistance.
2.Rough surface and potholes not properly marked have been reported on apron.
3.FODs have been reported in TWY and apron areas.
RWY
• Only turn pads located at each RWY ends are authorized.
• Backtrack may be required at both RWY ends.
TWYs
• All fleet: TWYs E and F – NOT AUTHORIZED.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Due lack of State published accurate information, discrepancies on ground markings and
Jeppesen Airport Layout charts can be found. Parking stand numbers marked on ground does
not match with Jeppesen 10–9 chart. Flight Crew to rely on ATC information provided and
request marshaller as required.
• All fleet: Apron 4 – NOT AUTHORIZED.
• Follow-me vehicle is mandatory prior to entering apron and if unable to follow the taxi out
yellow line due to the poor surface. Coordinate with Tower in advance and comply with their
instructions to avoid delay.
• No stop bar markings/signs per aircraft type are available. Follow Marshaller’s instructions.
HSSK.departure_procedures:
Planning
• ATC may give an initial clearance to 3,000 ft, which is below acceleration altitude as per the
Jeppesen Chart 10-4.
HSSK.miscellaneous:
Crew staying overnight at Sudan, passports will be held by immigration and returned at time of
departure.

HSPN.name: Port Sudan
HSPN.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Port Sudan
Ground Handling: NIL
Terminal: N/A
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
7 H24 YES See note YES NIL NO
WARNING Due to the dynamic threat situation in the vicinity of Post Sudan airport, Flight
Crew should contact the Dispatch Office for current intelligence updates/briefs
prior to departure.
CAUTION Birds in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
Note: Circling Approach – NOT AUTHORIZED West of RWY due to High Terrain.
HSPN.company_policy:
• Port Sudan can be nominated as destination alternate only when the weather forecast at
destination Khartoum is 5,000m (Visibility)/1,000ft (Ceiling) or greater.
• Full approach procedures must be flown.
HSPN.atc:
CAUTION 1.Port Sudan is located within a restricted airspace.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Low transition altitude.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponder may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
• No Radar environment. Procedural separation with the use of DME and radials to separate
arriving and departing traffic.
• The peak period is from 13:00 to 17:00.
RWY preference
• RWY 34 is the preferred RWY for take-off and landing.
HSPN.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION Do not mistake Old aerodrome used for military exercises, located 8 NM to the
North and with similar RWY alignment for Port Sudan.
RWY 16/34
• Approach lights may be inoperative temporarily due to frequent power cuts.
HSPN.ground_maneuvering:
TWYs
• All TWYs have a down-slope from the RWY to the apron.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Apron is small with limited parking stands.
• Apron layout and parking stands are not depicted on the Jeppesen aerodrome layout Charts.
• Taxiing slowly to the parking stand is required since Marshaller may request to stop suddenly.
• Aircraft are normally parked parallel to the terminal building and according to the wind direction
(head wind).
• All parking stands are power-in/out and careful maneuvering when taxiing on the apron is
required as wing tip clearance is minimal if other aircraft are parked.
HSPN.departure_procedures:
General
• ATC flight plan is filed manually by local ground handling.
Refueling with pax onboard
• Two stairs need to be available and fire truck must be present at the aircraft.
Pushback and Startup procedure
• Call 10 minutes before start-up.
• ATC requires fuel (endurance) and total passengers on-board on the first contact.
Planning
• After take-off on RWY 34, turn to the right before 5 NM to avoid the old aerodrome.
HSPN.miscellaneous:
General
• The ground handling is available during the time of the operation (normally from 12:00 to 17:00
UTC). Outside that time, 30 minutes prior notice is required to have the ground personnel
available.
• Only one first aid ambulance is available at the aerodrome. Hospitals are available in the city.

RFF RETI

9 H24 NIL YES YES NIL YES

Note: 1.RWY 14/32 – NOT AUTHORIZED.

HTDA.atc:
WARNING Traffic separation between VFR and IFR flight is not provided. Not all traffic may
be displayed on TCAS.
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Busy TMA with high volume of VFR traffic.
3.Helicopters, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
4.Nav-aid signal may fluctuate due power outages.
5.Low transition altitude.
Note: APP Radar Services: Every last Monday of the month, 06:00 to 09:00 not available for
maintenance.
Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• Radio reception has been reported poor.
• Controllers English accent may be difficult to understand. Use standard and simple R/T
phraseology, speak slowly and clearly to avoid any confusion.
• Possible loss of VHF coverage at Nairobi/Dar es Salaam FIR boundary.
• Do not rely on ATIS for RWY in use, confirm with TWR.
HTDA.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Aircraft position and height must be continuously monitored throughout the
approach.
2.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWY for
landing RWY.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• STARs are published, but radar vectoring is used.
• DAR primary approach radar coverage is 120 NM. Therefore, Flight Crew are to Squawk code
2000 when entering Tanzanian airspace from a region with no radar coverage unless assigned
a different code.
RWY 05
• Radio altimeter indicates terrain slopes up to 500ft to the Southwest.
• VOR Z and NDB approaches descent angle not coincident with PAPI.
RWY 23
• VOR approaches descent angle not coincident with PAPI.
HTDA.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION While taxiing on TWY P, request clearance to cross RWY 14/32 and exercise
vigilance of aircraft movements on RWY 14/32 before crossing.
RWYs
• There is a pronounced dip in middle of RWY.
TWYs
• TWYs are poorly marked.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects while taxiing on TWY P due to
the proximity of other aircraft, equipment and personnel.
• TWY Q - NOT AUTHORIZED.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Cargo Apron – NOT AUTHORIZED.
• Terminal III: All parking stands have upslope towards final parking position. Flight Crews should
exercise proper energy management to avoid aircraft from stopping.
HTDA.departure_procedures:
Startup and Pushback
• Request clearance for any maneuver only when the aircraft is ready to comply with the
clearance without delay.
• ATC clearance is usually given during taxi-out.
Routing DAR - JRO
• The route is planned via waypoint "MUMTU" followed by DCT segments to enter terminal area.

HTKJ.company_policy: • All landings at night shall be performed by Captains or a Captain under training only.

WARNING Traffic separation between VFR and IFR flight is not provided. Not all traffic may

CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Busy TMA with high volume of VFR traffic, including helicopters and general

3.Navaids may become unserviceable during the rainy season and

RWY 09

• GPWS warning may be triggered due to steep rising terrain.
HTKJ.arrival_procedures:
General
• Due to lack of surrounding lighting in vicinity of the aerodrome, a “black hole” effect may be
experienced during night operations.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Ensure all descent clearances provide adequate terrain clearance.
RWY 09
• ATC may give late descent clearance (due to VFR traffic) for the straight-in approach to RWY
09.
HTKJ.ground_maneuvering:
RWYs
• There is a pronounced dip in middle of RWY.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Follow-me vehicle not available.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• Apron surface has been reported as rough on TWY R.
• Before entering the apron, await signal from Marshaller.
HTKJ.departure_procedures:
Refuelling with Pax Onboard
• Approved by local authority, fire truck is on standby and steps (front and rear) attached to
aircraft.
Startup and Push-back
• Request clearance for any maneuver only when the aircraft is ready to comply with the
clearance without delay.
• ATC clearance is usually given during taxi out.
Planning
• Adherence to the SID altitude constraints is required due to terrain.
• ATC may clear a direct turn to waypoint UTATA after FL 120.
• Routing JRO - DAR: The route is planned via waypoint "MUMTU" followed by DCT segments to
enter terminal area.

HTZA.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Abeid Amani Karume International
Ground Handling: NIL
Terminal: N/A
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 H24 NIL NO YES NIL LMT
Note: 1.From July to September is the charter flights season, the number of flights operating
dramatically increases from 10:00 to 15:00. Additionally, Kilimanjaro departure time
and the arrival time at Zanzibar coincides with local traffic movements as per the
current schedule except Wednesday which will be worst.
HTZA.company_policy: • Full approach procedures must be flown.
HTZA.atc:
WARNING Traffic separation between VFR and IFR flight is not provided. Not all traffic may
be displayed on TCAS.
CAUTION 1.NAVAIDS are not monitored by ATC, therefore NAVAIDs maybe inoperative
without NOTAM notification or ATC knowing the actual status.
2.Busy TMA with high volume of VFR traffic.
3.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
4.Low transition altitude.
General
• SIDs and STARs are not published.
• Procedural separation with the use of DME and radials to separate arrivals and departing traffic.
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponders may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
RWY Preference
• To minimize the 180° turns on the turn pad of RWY 18, Flight Crews are to follow the preferred
RWY sequence indicated below unless limited by aircraft performance or other restrictions:
‐ Preferred RWY for departures - RWY 36.
‐ Preferred RWY for landings - RWY 18.
HTZA.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION Note the low level for missed approach and especially when the aircraft is light.
General
• Expect to be transferred to approach at FL060 or below.
• ATC requires passengers on board (POB) and aircraft registration on first contact.
• Flight Crew should Contact Zanzibar Tower in advance to obtain weather and RWY in use.
RWYs
• PAPIs are difficult to see due to tall grass.
HTZA.ground_maneuvering:
RWY
• Turn pads are not lit.
• RWY 18 Turn Pad:
‐ Do not follow the Turn Pad markings.
‐ Use the turn technique as detailed in FCOM/FCTM.
TWYs
• Due to the tall grass within the vicinity of TWY, it might be difficult to determine the TWY signs.
• TWY E has a down slope from the apron to the RWY.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• Aircraft is guided by Marshaller to the final parking position. Wing walkers must be in position
prior to parking.
HTZA.departure_procedures:
General
• ATC flight plan is filed manually by local ground handling.
Refuelling with Pax On-board
• Fire truck must be present at the aircraft.
Startup and Pushback
• When ready for pushback, inform Ground Crew to coordinate with ATC.
• Once ATC clearance for pushback is issued, the aircraft will be pushed back to the startup
position facing East on the taxi lane under marshaller instructions.
• Request Engine Startup after receiving marshaller confirmation that the aircraft is correctly
aligned on the taxi lane.
• Wing walkers must be in position prior to pushback.
• For pushback, ensure head-set operator has clear understanding of the pushback clearance
and reads back accordingly.
Initial Clearance
• Zanzibar tower provides clearance to climb to inter-medial flight level.
• Dar Es Salaam Control will authorize turn on course to the exit point.

WARNING Traffic separation between VFR and IFR flight is not provided. Not all traffic may
be displayed on TCAS.
CAUTION 1.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
2.Low transition altitude.

restrictions.

transit check.

• Departure after midnight (Local Time)
CYUL.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Busy ATC environment due to several nearby aerodromes.
CYUL.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWYs for
landing RWY.
Note: 1.For CAT II/III operations, the maximum crosswind component is 15Kt.
2.For parking gate information, contact GHA prior to arrival and refrain from contacting
Apron Control.
3.Approach ban. Based on the “FAF” or if no “FAF” published, point where final
approach course is intercepted.
4.APU needs to be turned on prior to reaching the gate. Once the engines are shutdown,
the bridge connected, ground staff will plug-in the Ground Power Unit.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Expect to fly procedures as published. Montreal ATC tries to accommodate CDA as much as
possible.
• Arriving from the East, landing RWY 24R expect to cross MAIRE intersection at or below
12,000ft (32 NM track miles to threshold).
General
• Expect to be transferred directly from TWR to Apron control after landing RWY 24R and exiting
at B2, B6 or RWY end.
CYUL.ground_maneuvering:
Note: Ramp operation is generally stopped when TS or lightning is the vicinity of the
aerodrome.
Winter operations
• TWYs and Apron may be covered with black ice. Exercise vigilance while taxiing.
• RWY 06R/24L. NOTAM published when slippery condition exist.
CYUL.departure_procedures:
Startup and Pushback
• TWR
‐ Read back clearance must include call sign, assigned SID and Transponder code.
• PDC Procedure
‐ Request PDC no sooner than 60 minutes prior to ETD and no later than 15 minutes after
ETD.
‐ Acknowledge PDC within 5 minutes otherwise, it will be cancelled.
‐ Revert to voice in the event of PDC cancelled or failure.
• Pushback (Local procedure)
‐ Includes more than one personnel.
‐ The employee who is disconnecting the tow bar removes the safety pin and shows it to the
Flight Crew.
‐ Marshaller is always in front of the aircraft holding his position until everyone is clear.
‐ Before taxiing follow the instructions of the marshaller.

• Judgmental steering is required.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Parking stand designators are displayed on ground only, aircraft is guided by marshalling.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
MMQT.departure_procedures:
General
• ATC flight plan must be filed locally.
Startup and Push-back
• Slots are valid for 15 minutes only. If unable to meet slot tolerance, GND will coordinate with
Flight Crew for revised slot.
• Request clearance 30 minutes before start-up to coordinate the flight levels.
• Advise ATC on first contact if full RWY is required for takeoff.
Planning
• Due to combination of aerodrome elevation and temperature, take-off performance is impacted.

General

• Ensure all descent clearance provides adequate terrain clearance.
• Expect RWY assignment from Anchorage approach on initial contact.
RWY Preference

• RWY 07L is the alternate RWY.

RWY 07L and RWY 07R

GROUND MANEUVERING
Winter Operations

Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
De-icing
• De-icing is done on parking stand.
RWY preference
• RWY 33 is normal RWY for take-off.
• RWY 07L/R: high terrain and mountain wave may impact the climb out.
• RWY 07R: Performance calculation in the OPT is based on take-off from TWY J.
SIDs
• Expect radar vectors for departure to assigned route or fix.
• Expect further clearance to filed altitude/level within 10 minutes after departure.
• Ensure all departure clearance provides adequate terrain clearance.

KATL.name: Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International
KATL.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International
Ground Handling:
• Pax: GAT AIRLINE GROUND SUPPORT. Contact is via Ramp Control Freq.
• Cargo: SWISSPORT. Contact is via Ramp Control Freq.
• De-icing: PRIME FLIGHT AVIATION
Terminal:
• PAX and Cargo only operation with Pax aircraft: Concourse F
• Cargo: South Cargo Ramp
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 (E) H24 NIL NO YES NIL YES
Note: 1.Aerodrome is located in a noise sensitive area.
2.Preferred Noise Abatement Departure Procedure is NADP 2.
COMPANY POLICY
NIL
AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL
CAUTION 1.TCAS alert may be triggered due to extensive VFR traffic operation around the
aerodrome.
2.Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (drones) have been reported. Advise ATC when
applicable.
3.Laser targeting has been reported. Inform ATC when applicable.
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION 1.Turbulence and strong crosswinds may be encountered on final approach.
2.Carefully review the charted DME distance at the threshold for the planned
approach. If the DME is not collocated with the ILS, consider using the
FMC/FMGC Fix Page and RWY end waypoint to determine an accurate
distance to the threshold during the approach.
3.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWYs for
landing RWY .
Note: For CAT II/III operations, the maximum crosswind component is 15Kt.
General
• During periods of bad weather, arrival delays build up quickly.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Expect radar vectoring.
• Do not accept visual approach until landing RWY is correctly identified.
GROUND MANEUVERING
CAUTION All RWYs have the same QDM, but designated differently to avoid confusion.
TWYs
• Judgemental steering required for the following turns:
‐ Left Turn from TWY M12 to TWY M.
‐ Left Turn from TWY E3 to TWY E.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• Passenger Operations:
‐ Expect to be parked on stands F5 or F7.
‐ Parking stand F5 has upslope towards final parking position. Flight Crews should exercise
proper energy management to avoid aircraft from stopping.
• Cargo Operations:
‐ Expect to be parked on stand 5, 6 or 7.
‐ Flight Crews must transmit blind on 122.95 their position and intentions before entering
uncontrolled South Cargo ramp.
‐ Taxi line markings South of RWY 09R to the South Cargo Ramp and onto the parking stands
are difficult to see especially during hours of darkness or adverse weather conditions. If in
doubt, request ground assistance.
‐ Look for the Marshaller upon entering the apron for guidance to the parking stand.
• Visual Docking Guidance System:
CAUTION The system is manually operated. Flight Crew must exercise vigilance as
instructions for full stop or alignment are subject to the ground operator
handling.
‐ The Visual Docking Guidance System provides lateral and forward movement guidance.
However, no distance to parking spot is provided
‐ Lateral guidance.
The system is equipped with vertical bar lights located in the middle part of the upper hood:
▪A single green bar indicates that the aircraft nose is correctly aligned.
▪A red bar to either side of the green bar indicates the aircraft nose is drifted in that
direction. The red bar disappears once the aircraft nose is corrected.
Lateral guidance
‐ Forward movement guidance.
The system is equipped with a set of three lights with fixed colours, which will be displayed
once the aircraft is aligned towards the parking position:
▪Green light indicates “Continue forward”.
▪Yellow light indicates “Prepare to stop”.
▪Red light indicates “Stop” or “Gate unavailable”.
Forward guidance
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
Planning
• ATC may expect/request intersection take-off from M2 for RWY 09L. ATC must be advised as
early as possible if full length take-off is required due to performance.

AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL

aerodrome. Naval ships equipped with transponders may trigger TCAS alerts
as well.
2.Busy TMA and ATC environment.
3.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
General
• Expect single RWY operations when weather falls below VFR minima (commonly RWY 04R).
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION 1.Carefully review the charted DME distance at the threshold for the planned
approach. If the DME is not collocated with the ILS, consider using the
FMC/FMGC Fix Page and RWY end waypoint to determine an accurate
distance to the threshold during the approach.
2.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel RWYs
and TWY for landing RWY.
Note: For CAT II/III operations, the maximum crosswind component is 15Kt.
General
• Expect OOSHN RNAV STAR.
• Prior to landing, Flight Crew must ensure receipt of assigned parking stand.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Do not accept visual approach until the landing RWY is correctly identified.
RWY 04L and RWY 27
• NIL ALS.
RWY 09
• NIL ALS, IAP and PAPI.
RWY 22R
• NIL ALS and IAP.
GROUND MANEUVERING
Winter operations
• TWYs and Apron may be covered with black ice. Exercise vigilance while taxiing.
TWYs
• Judgemental oversteering required.
• TWY J, TWY A (between TWY N and TWY C) and TWY E (between TWY A and Terminal A) –
NOT AUTHORIZED.
Apron/Parking stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
General
• Significant deviation in magnetic variation is reported on Stand E5.
Startup and Pushback clearance
• Contact Clearance Delivery Frequency.
‐ Once clearance delivery is issued, contact same frequency when ready for pushback.
‐ After “ready to pushback” is notified, aircraft will be asked to monitor Ground Control for
pushback clearance (Ground Control issues all pushback clearances). Usually all pushback
clearances are issued within 30 seconds after requested.
• Crossing an active RWY on the way to the departure RWY is common.
• Ground Controller will provide a frequency change to monitor the appropriate Tower frequency
prior to reaching the first hold short point before crossing an active RWY.
Planning
• Determine if able to comply with SID required climb gradient. If unable to comply, advise ATC to
confirm if adherence with the SID climb gradient is required or possibility of alternative RWY.
• Due to performance limitations, the max take-off weight (MTOW) is possible only in RWY 15R.
Performance must be checked for impact on the carriage of payload.
• RWY 33L full length is not available due to the lack of turn pad at the RWY end. Take-off
performance computation should be based on Intersection Charley (i.e. RWY 33L/C).
• Preferred SIDs: LBSTA SIX DEP and CELTK FIVE DEP.
De-icing/Anti-icing Procedures
• Expect de-icing to be conducted at gate. Aircraft may be towed few metres back for allowing
de-icing trucks maneuvering.

KORD.name: Chicago - O'Hare International
KORD.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Chicago - O'Hare International
Ground Handling: Pax: SWISSPORT – 131.525 (in case of no response contact Ramp Tower for
gate assignment).
Terminal:
• Pax: Terminal 5 (Contact Ramp Freq. for parking stand information)
• Cargo: North East Cargo Ramp
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 (E) H24 NIL NO YES NIL YES
CAUTION Large flocks of Canadian Geese have been observed in the aerodrome vicinity
during Spring and Fall.
Note: 1.Aerodrome is located in a noise sensitive area.
2.Preferred Noise Abatement Departure Procedure is NADP 1.
KORD.atc:
CAUTION 1.Busy aerodrome and ATC environment due to several domestic aerodromes
nearby.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAV) activity in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
RWY Assignment
• Last minute RWY changes are a common occurrence.
• If RWY assigned is not acceptable, coordinate with ATC well in advance, as late notification
may result in arrival or departure delay.
Penalty Boxes and Hold Pads
• Both arrival and departure delay holding positions are the Penalty Box and the various hold
pads located around the aerodrome.
• In general, the Penalty Box is used for arriving aircraft waiting for a parking stand, but exact
locations vary with RWY take-off and landing configurations. Hold pads are also used in some
cases for secondary de-ice procedures. All RWYs except RWY 04L/22R have a hold pad.
KORD.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWYs for
RWY.
2.Unstable approaches are common, caused by ATC keeping aircraft high and
fast. Conservative aircraft configuration planning required.
3.Carefully review the charted DME distance at the threshold for the planned
approach. If the DME is not collocated with the ILS, consider using the
FMC/FMGC Fix Page and RWY end waypoint to determine an accurate
distance to the threshold during the approach.
Note: For CAT II/III operations, the maximum crosswind component is 15Kt.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• STARs are typically flown until 40 NM from the aerodrome, at which point radar vectors to
the RWY are used. Expect to fly full STAR during peak traffic or periods of LVP. STARs have
numerous FL published, advice ATC if unable to comply.
• During uncongested periods/night time, “DCTs to ORD VOR” and discretionary descents is
normally offered.
• Do not accept visual approach until the landing RWY is correctly identified.
Speed Control
• During peak traffic expect to be slowed for spacing. Chicago has very tight spacing, expect
multiple speed changes on arrival. At times, you may be slowed at one altitude then in the
descent cleared to increase speed. Provide prompt speed compliance and speed control to
assigned fix.
‐ It is extremely important to maintain assigned airspeeds without deviation, unless otherwise
directed by ATC.
‐ If unable to comply with assigned speed, advise ATC immediately of the speed you can
accept.
Arrival Flow Rates
• Arrival flow rates are significantly reduced when winds are from the East and combined with
ceiling less than 1,000ft.
Separation
• Reduced separation of 2.5 NM between arrivals is normal practice.
Approach hand over to TWR
• When instructed to contact the tower at the FAF or marker, stay with approach control and
switch over at the marker, unless otherwise instructed.
RWY 09L/27R and RWY 10R/28L
• NIL PAPI.
KORD.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION 1.Multiple Intersecting RWYs combined with complex TWYs and hold
instructions, extra vigilance is required at all times.
2.Be alert to RWY crossing clearances.
TWYs
• Judgemental oversteering is required for all turns.
Northeast Cargo Ramp
• Do not start engines before reaching the engine startup positions E1 or E2.
KORD.departure_procedures:
Night Operations (22:00 to 07:00 Local Time)
• Chicago has a voluntary “fly quiet program” in place between 22:00 to 07:00 Local Time:
‐ Preferential RWY for departure: RWY 28.
‐ Departure flight tracks/crossing altitude requirements advised by ATC.
‐ Noise abatement signs are located on approaching TWYs to threshold of RWY 28.
Passenger Operations
• After push-back, contact Metering stating current ATIS and aircraft position. Metering will ask if
able to comply with minimum altitude/distance restriction on departure.
• Aircraft is handed over to Ground Control for taxi clearance.
Departure Climb Crossing Restrictions
• Be vigilant of the crossing restriction (5.5 and 8.5 DME from GCO VOR) as shown on SID chart.
If unable to comply advise ATC. These restrictions are imposed to protect separation from other
aerodrome in the area, also act as noise abatement procedures.
Takeoff All Engine Performance Guidance
• To compute All Engine Performance, use distance from Brake Release point provided below.
Altitude can be checked using the graphical option to ensure compliance.
Ident Distance To D5.5/ GCO (nm) Distance To D8.5/ GCO (nm)
04L 6.3 9.3
04R 6.9 9.9
09C 6.9 9.9
09L 6.5 9.5
09R 6.8 9.8
10C 6.6 9.6
10L 6.7 9.7
10R 6.2 9.2
22L 4.9 7.9
22R Landing Only Landing Only
27C 6.0 9.0
27L 5.9 8.9
27R 5.5 8.5
28C 5.7 8.7
28L 5.1 8.1
28R 6.2 9.2
Note: Utilizing NADP 1 ensures compliance with SID crossing altitudes.

• Full approach procedures must be flown.
• Operation is not authorized if icing conditions are reported/observed at the aerodrome.
PACD.atc:
WARNING Cold Bay is an uncontrolled aerodrome located within class E airspace.
CAUTION Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
Traffic Information Broadcasts by Aircraft Procedure
• Below FL180 the airspace is uncontrolled, Class G.
• On entering Class G airspace the below procedures must be observed.
‐ Arrival
▪Establish communication with Cold Bay Radio on VHF.
▪Provide ETA, POB, aircraft registration and aircraft type.
▪Request current traffic information, both arriving and departing.
▪Maintain a continuous listening watch and broadcast the aircraft’s position and intended
movements prior to maneuvering the aircraft as applicable.
▪Broadcast acknowledgements of any messages received.
▪Broadcast any other message considered necessary in the interests of safety.
▪Flight Crew shall make the following position reports within the vicinity of the aerodrome,
joining downwind, base, final and clearing the RWY.
‐ Departure
▪Communications shall be maintained at all times with Cold Bay Radio on VHF frequency.
▪Prior to departure maintain a listening watch for at least 5 minutes on Cold Bay Radio
frequency.
▪Broadcast to Cold Bay Radio the aircraft position and intentions before entering the RWY
for takeoff, before commencing takeoff roll, when the aircraft is airborne (ATD), when it
leaves the traffic circuit and when it leaves the Class G airspace.

request ground assistance.
RWY

Apron/Parking stands

of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.

RFF RETI
hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 (E) H24 NIL NO YES NIL YES
Note: 1.Aerodrome is located in a noise sensitive area.
2.Preferred Noise Abatement Departure Procedure is NADP 1.
KDFW.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspaces in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Busy TMA and ATC environment due to several nearby aerodromes .
3.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operation at the aerodrome.
4.Parachute and Glider activity in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
General
• TCAS alerts may be received during peak times: from 14:45 to 17:15 (15:45 to 18:15) and from
20:00 to 00:00 (21:00 to 01:00).
RWY Assignment
• Simultaneous approaches to RWYs 17C/R, 18L/R and 35L/C are often authorized.
• Expect simultaneous take-off on the parallel RWYs with both aircraft maintaining the same
heading for a long distance.
• If allocated RWY is not acceptable, advise ATC as soon as possible to prevent last minute
delays.
KDFW.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Carefully review the charted DME distance at the threshold for the planned
approach. If the DME is not collocated with the ILS, consider using the
FMC/FMGC Fix Page and RWY end waypoint to determine an accurate
distance to the threshold during the approach.
2.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel RWYs for
landing RWY.
Note: For CAT II/III operations, the maximum crosswind component is 15Kt.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Expect radar vectoring.
• Do not accept visual approach until the landing RWY is correctly identified.
RWY 13L
• NIL ALS and IAP.
RWY 31L
• NIL ALS.
RWY preference
• RWYs 18R/36L and 17L/35R are preferred RWY for landing, then 17C/35C, 13R or 31R.
• Simultaneous converging ILS approaches to RWY 18L/R – 17C/R – 13R and RWY 31R – 35L/C
– 36L/R may be used. Flight Crews to ensure the briefing is according to the ATC assigned
approach and that the correct approach charts and FMS NAVDB approach are selected.
Planning
• Expect delay and extensive vectoring during significant convective weather activity.
• During peak times, expect to be slowed down for spacing. Dallas Fort Worth has a very tight
spacing. Expect multiple speed changes on arrival. ATC may request slowing down at one
altitude and increased speed when clearing for descent. Provide prompt speed compliance and
speed control to assigned fix.
• It is extremely important to maintain assigned airspeeds without deviation, unless otherwise
advised by ATC. If unable to comply with assigned speed, advise ATC immediately with the
acceptable speed.
• Expect to be assigned 190Kt or less within 5 NM of intercepting the final approach course to
prevent overshooting the final approach course.
• Landing RWY will be advised within 35 NM to 40 NM from the aerodrome.
• In Missed Approaches, ATC may provide radar vectors instead of the published procedures.
KDFW.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION LAHSO signs are lighted and marked with pulsing white lights that may cause
distraction. Exercise vigilance.
General
• RWY Status Lights are operational, Refer to 6.3.3.4.1.5.22 Runway Status Lights for details on
the system.
• Exercise vigilance in all ground manoeuvring operations due to multiple RWYs in use and
complex taxi routings.
• Ramp operation is generally stopped when TS or lightning is in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
• Follow-me vehicle available on request.
TWYs
• TWY edges are not lighted but light reflectors are installed in all TWYs, except on the curves
that are equipped with lighting.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Exercise vigilance due to numerous markings and lines painted on ground for the parking
stands.
• Solid lead-in lines available for narrow-body and broken lead-in lines for wide-body aircraft.
KDFW.departure_procedures:
Startup and Pushback
• Contact Ramp Tower 10 minutes before startup.
De-/Anti-icing
• De-/anti-icing treatment will be performed by Worldwide Flight Services (WFS) at the
SOUTHWEST HOLD PAD (near RWY 36R threshold).
• Upon arrival at the airport, the Commander shall inform QR ground staff of the requirement for
de/anti-icing and of any special requirement (i.e. APU INOP).
• QR staff will relay the request to Worldwide Flight Services (WFS).
• WFS Frequency: 129.4 (to be used to request de/anti-icing treatment prior to departure).
• After de-/anti-icing treatment is coordinated with WFS, request pushback with DFW Ramp
Tower.
• Taxi to Apron Entrance/Exit Point to exit ramp and monitor Ground frequency.
• Advise Ground frequency that you will be de-icing at the SOUTHWEST HOLD PAD.
• After receiving taxi instructions, contact “Ice Man” (Frequency 130.2) and advise that you are
taxing to the De-icing Pad.
• "Ice Man" will assign the parking position where the de/anti-icing personnel will meet and guide
the aircraft.
• The aircraft will be marshalled to the designated parking position.
• Once the aircraft has come to a complete stop, set the parking brakes and shut down the
engines.
Note: De-/anti-icing with engines running will only be performed on aircraft with APU INOP
and requires prior notification and approval.
• The De-icing Supervisor will contact the Flight Crew via interphone to:
‐ Verify the required treatment (De-ice only or De-ice/Anti-ice).
‐ Request the Flight Crew to configure for treatment.
• Treatment will only start after the Flight Crew has confirmed “Aircraft is configured/ Cleared to
spray”.
• When the treatment is completed, the De-icing Supervisor will provide the de-/anti-icing code
and confirm that the area is clear for engine start.
• When ready to taxi, contact Ground frequency for clearance to depart the SOUTHWEST HOLD
PAD.
Taxi to Eastern RWYs
• When taxiing on TWY Y: Monitor East TWR frequency and hold short of TWY JY if no further
instructions are received.
• When taxiing on TWY A: Monitor East TWR frequency and hold short of TWY JA if no further
instructions are received.
Planning
• Preference RWY for departures are 18L/36R and 17R/35L.
• When RNAV 2 SIDs are planned, ATC will issue take-off clearance including the first waypoint
only, i.e. “QATARI 123, RNAV to NAVYE, RWY 18L CLEAR TO TAKE-OFF”. Flight Crews must
follow the complete SID as published.

KIAH.general:
Name of Aerodrome: George Bush Intercontinental.
Ground Handling:
• Pax: SWISSPORT – 130.600
• Cargo: PINNACLE LOGISTICS – 131.375
Terminal:
• Pax: Terminal D West
• Cargo: East Cargo - South Ramp Area (East of RWY 26L THR).
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 (E) H24 NIL NO YES NIL YES
Note: 1.Aerodrome is located in a noise sensitive area.
2.Preferred Noise Abatement Departure Procedure is NADP 2.
COMPANY POLICY
NIL
AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL
CAUTION 1.TCAS alert may be triggered due to extensive VFR traffic operation around the
aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operation at the aerodrome.
General
• Peak times:
‐ Arrival: 18:30 to 19:30 (17:30 to 18:30) and 22:30 to 23:30 (21:30 to 22:30).
‐ Departure: 22:30 to 23:30 (21:30 to 22:30) and 01:00 to 02:00 (00:00 to 01:00).
Local Procedure
• SWAP (Severe Weather Avoidance Procedure) used to reduce Enroute capacity. Re-routes and
revised flows are used to avoid severe weather.
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION 1.Carefully review the charted DME distance at the threshold for the planned
approach. If the DME is not collocated with the ILS, consider using the
FMC/FMGC Fix Page and RWY end waypoint to determine an accurate
distance to the threshold during the approach.
2.When approaching RWY 26L during low visibility, do not mistake East cargo
apron with RWY 26L ALS.
3.Ensure correct RWY identification. Do not mistake parallel TWYs and parallel
RWY for landing RWY.
Note: For CAT II/III operations, the maximum crosswind component is 15Kt.
General
• Extended holding and arrival delays to be expected at any time due to traffic and changing
weather.
• Flight Crew to plan and brief for potential RWY change.
• Flight Crew may request RWY 08R/26L if possible as this RWY is closer to the parking terminal.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Expect radar vectoring.
RWY Operations
• RWY 26L, RWY 27 and RWY 26R (CAT III) up to 10Kt tailwind.
• ATC advises RWY in use on first contact.
RWY 08L/26R
• NIL PAPI.
RWY 15L/33R
• RWY 15L: NIL ALS and IAP.
• RWY 33R: NIL PAPI.
RWY 33L
• NIL ALS and IAP.
Local Procedure
• Triple simultaneous parallel approaches (RWY 26L/R and RWY 27) in use during busy periods.
• If RWY 15L/R or RWY 33L/R used for arrivals, flow rate is reduced considerably.
• Inbound traffic may be rerouted to Dallas Forth Worth as weather fronts build up rapidly to the
East and West.
GROUND MANEUVERING
General
• Hot Spots: RWY 26L TWY NP and TWY NE, RWY 15L/R at TWY WL.
CODE E/F FLEET
Apron/Parking Stands
• Taxi with minimum thrust.
• Passenger Operations
‐ Expected parking stand is D5. Other stands may be offered.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
Startup and Pushback clearance
• PDC: Request 20 minutes prior to EOBT.
• Pushback Clearance (passenger operations):
‐ Contact “Ramp Control North” for pushback to “hand-off point” (12 or 19).
‐ Ramp control will advise either “tail West” or “tail East”.
‐ Pushback clearance ends at “hand-off point”.
‐ Wait for further taxi instructions.
• After pushback
‐ After receiving clearance to pushback by “Ramp Control”, Flight Crew will be advise to
monitor “Ground Metering” frequency.
‐ “Ground Metering” will contact the aircraft for taxi readiness and latest ATIS. Flight Crew will
be advise to monitor “Ground Control” frequency.
‐ “Ground Control” will contact the aircraft and issue taxi instructions.
Planning
• RWY 33L: Recommended to calculate take-off performance for payload impact.
• Preferential RWYs: RWY 15L (primary), RWY 15R (secondary) and RWY 09 (traffic permitting).
• RWY15L/R: Exercise vigilance around TWY NB and TWY WV due to many joining TWYs.
• Typical departure profile for RWY15L/R:
‐ Expect right turn to North/Northeasterly heading, with a restriction to maintain 4,000ft.
‐ After clearing downwind arriving traffic, a clearance to 15,000ft is initiated.

cleared to increase speed. Provide prompt speed compliance and speed control to assigned fix.

accept.

Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 (E) H24 NIL NO YES NIL YES
Note: 1.Aerodrome is located in a noise sensitive area.
2.Preferred Noise Abatement Departure Procedure: NADP2.
3.Miami has capacity restrictions and SLOTS are enforced for arrival/departues. Flight
Crews are reminded to comply with the scheduled arrival/departure times to avoid
incurring significant fines or penalties.
4.Passengers Operations: Flight Crews must contact GHA to provide arrival and
departure times and fuel on board.
COMPANY POLICY
NIL
AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspaces in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Busy ATC environment due to several nearby aerodromes.
3.TCAS alert may be triggered due to extensive VFR traffic operation around the
aerodrome.
4.General Aviation operations at and in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
General
• Traffic circuit is RIGHT HAND for RWY 09 and RWY 26L .
• Radar Traffic Information Services (RTIS) is provided within 25 NM, subject to controller
discretion. This is advisory service of radar targets which may be in close proximity to aircraft
position or its intended flight path.
Preferred RWYs
• Arrival:
‐ RWYs 09, 12, 08L preferred during daytime.
‐ RWYs 09, 08R, 26L preferred during night time.
• Departures:
‐ RWYs 08R, 27 and 08L preferred during daytime.
‐ RWYs 08R, 09, 27, 26L preferred during night time.
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION 1.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel RWYs,
TWYs and intersecting RWYs for landing RWY.
2.Carefully review the charted DME distance at the threshold for the planned
approach. If the DME is not collocated with the ILS, consider using the
FMC/FMGC Fix Page and runway end waypoint to determine an accurate
distance to the threshold during the approach.
Note: For CAT II/III operations, the maximum crosswind component is 15Kt.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• STARs are published, but radar vectors to final approach are common practice.
• Do not accept visual approach until the landing RWY is correctly identified.
Local Procedures
• ACARS ARR MVT MSG should be sent only when aircraft arrives on stand.
GROUND MANEUVERING
CAUTION 1.Miami Dade Aviation Department (MDAD) controls all apron movements,
refer to Jeppesen Chart 10-9 for details. Miami GROUND does not instruct
frequency changes, contact MDAD as required prior entering the apron.
2.RWYs 08R, 12 and also RWYs 27, 30 share a common area for thresholds.
General
• Ramp operations are generally stopped when lightning. Ground Control will advise aircraft
holding position until ramp is re-opened.
Apron/Parking Stands
Cargo Operations
• Expect to be parked on stand W-51, Other parking stands may be offered.
CODE E FLEET
Apron/Parking Stands
• Expect parking stands E23 and E24. Others parking stands may be offered.
• Parking stand E10:
‐ B77L: Due close proximity of left engine to the jet bridge, the aircraft will be stopped with nose
northbound to be further towed in to the gate. Engines must be shut down prior turning to the
gate.
• Parking stand E11, follow the yellow dashed lead-in lines.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
Note: When pushing back from the parking position, coordinate with tow Crew NOT to
inadvertently enter TWY P.
Startup and Pushback
• Engine start is permitted once pushback is completed.
• Permission for engine start-up must be authorized by Miami Dade Aviation Department (MDAD).
• Power setting must be maintained at idle until aircraft is located at power out spot or aligned on
centreline of the assigned active TWY.

Noise Abatement Procedures - RWY Usage (Weather permitting)
• Westerly Operations (Daytime)
‐ Westerly operations is the normal traffic pattern during daytime for arrival and departures due
to prevailing Westerly wind.
‐ Arrivals and departures are usually assigned RWY 26L and RWY 26R.
‐ If weather condition require a tailwind component of 7Kt of more in dry RWY conditions or
more than 3Kt in wet RWY conditions, aircraft operations are reversed to Easterly operations
with both arrivals and departures on RWY 08L and RWY 08R.
• ‘Contra-Flow’ Operations (22:00 - 07:00 Local time)
‐ Arrivals are usually assigned RWY 26L and RWY 26R.
‐ Departures are assigned on RWY 08L and RWY 08R.
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION 1.Turbulence and strong crosswinds may be encountered on final approach.
