Note: 1.Aerodrome is located in a noise sensitive area.
2.Preferred Noise Abatement Departure Procedure is NADP 2.
3.San Francisco has capacity restrictions and SLOTS are enforced for arrival/departure.
Flight Crews are reminded to comply with the scheduled arrival/departure times to
avoid incurring significant fines or penalties.
COMPANY POLICY
NIL
AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL
CAUTION 1.TCAS alert may be triggered due to extensive VFR traffic operation around the
aerodrome. Naval ships equipped with transponders may trigger TCAS alerts
as well.
2.Busy TMA and ATC environment due to high volume of VFR traffic including
helicopters and General Aviation and several aerodromes nearby.
RWY Preference
• RWY 28L/R: Preferred RWY for landing.
Note: 1.In case of performance limitation, RWY 28L/R or RWY 10L departure can be
requested to ATC. Expect delay for departure.
2.In case of strong westerly winds, RWY 28L/R will be preferred for take-off.
3.In case of a storm system moving through the Bay Area, the winds will switch
directions and RWY 19L/19R will be preferred for landing and RWY 10L/R for
departure.
Late Night Preferential Runway Use
• The preferred Arrival Runways are RWY 28L/R.
• The preferred departing runways from 01:00 LT to 06:00 LT are RWY 10L/R, RWY 01L/R or
RWY 28L/R with an immediate right turn when safety, weather and traffic permits.
• This reduces aircraft noise in residential communities surrounding the airport as over
water-departure procedures directs aircraft over the bay.
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION 1.Do not mistake Oakland (KOAK/OAK) located 10 NM to the Northeast for San
Francisco (KSFO/SFO).
2.Turbulence and strong crosswinds may be encountered on final approach.
3.Carefully review the charted DME distance at the threshold for the planned
approach. If the DME is not collocated with the ILS, consider using the
FMC/FMGC Fix Page and RWY end waypoint to determine an accurate
distance to the threshold during the approach.
4.RWY 19L glideslope deviations are possible when critical areas are not
protected due to aircraft operating near the glide slope antenna. Be alert for
momentary localizer course deviations due to large aircraft operating in vicinity
of localizer antenna.
5.Ensure correct RWY identification. Do not mistake parallel TWYs and parallel
RWY for landing RWY.
Note: For CAT II/III operations, the maximum crosswind component is 15Kt.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Expect radar vectoring.
• ATC may offer significant shortcut which may result in the aircraft being left high in relation to
remaining track miles.
• Do not accept visual approach until the landing RWY is correctly identified.
RWYs 01L and 01R
• NIL IAP.

Apron/Parking stands

of aircraft parking stands even if VDGS available. If Marshaller and / or Wing Walkers not
available, contact SWISSPORT before turning into the final parking position.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
Planning
• Adherence to SIDs/vectors is required.
• Do not request direct route immediately after departure.

COMPANY POLICY
NIL
AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.TCAS alert may be triggered due to extensive VFR traffic operation around the
aerodrome.
3.Busy ATC environment due to several nearby aerodromes.
4.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
Peak times traffic flow
• Departures and Arrivals: 17:00 to 23:00 Local Time.
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION 1.Carefully review the charted DME distance at the threshold for the planned
approach. If the DME is not collocated with the ILS, consider using the
FMC/FMGC Fix Page and runway end waypoint to determine an accurate
distance to the threshold during the approach.
2.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWYs for
landing RWY.
Note: For CAT II/III operations, the maximum crosswind component is 15Kt.
General
• Bad weather produces extensive delays and diversions.
• Arrivals, planned via “Lancaster (LRP)” VOR, Numerous crossing altitudes published. Expect
radar vectors to final approach. Last minute RWY changes is common.
• RWY 01R: When “R6608” active, results in arrivals being slowed down due spacing and
vectoring .
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Do not accept visual approach until the landing RWY is correctly identified.
Simultaneous Converging Instrument Approaches
• Simultaneous converging ILS approaches to RWY 12 and RWY 19L/C/R may be used. Ensure
that the approach assigned by ATC is what is briefed and that correct approach chart and FMS
NAVDB approach are selected.
• Flight Crew must be notified by controller or via ATIS when procedure is in use.
Expected Arrival Flight Profile
NAV AID / Waypoint Expected FL
SPARTA (SAX) FL240 to FL280
Lancaster (LRP) 10,000ft - 17000ft
MULRR 10,000ft
RWY Configuration Preference
Takeoff RWY Landing RWY
South Configuration 19L/R 12, 19L/R
North Configuration 30/01L 01L/R
RWY 19L/R
• The go-around will need to be carefully planned when RWY 12 is also in use as any go-around
may require a turn away from RWY 12.
RWY 30
• NIL ALS and IAP.
GROUND MANEUVERING
Note: APU INOP during winter. Confirm with MOCC/IOC that station can provide the ground
equipment to support the Aircraft with APU inoperative before dispatching.
General
• The double yellow lines marking the edge of the Midfield Terminal Ramp may be easily
mistaken by taxi lanes, especially when turning towards the North side of the ramp from TWY
Y7 and when in low visibility.
• Extreme vigilance with Jet Blast Hazard is required when cleared to Taxi lane C from Taxi lane
D.
• Arriving aircraft: There is insufficient wing tip clearance for A350 or B777 when taxiing abeam
RWY 19L holding pad and a B747 or B777 is parked.
• Departing aircraft: There is insufficient wing tip clearance for A350 or B777 to access RWY 01C
from TWY Y11 if a B777 or B747 are parked at the hold-short line on TWY Y9.
TWYs
• After landing on RWY 19C, left turn from TWY Y7 to TWY Y – NOT AUTHORIZED.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Do not enter the non-movement area on TWY D without Ramp Control frequency authorization.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
Cargo Operations
• Expect to be parked in the New Cargo Ramp (Apron 6):
‐ No markings are available for parking stands at New Cargo Ramp – Apron 6.
‐ Request “Follow me” vehicle and wing walkers through the Ground Handler to enter the apron
and guidance to final parking position.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
De-icing/Anti-icing
Note: 1.Contact Swissport Iceman on Air to Ground Freq 131.975 before calling Ground for
Pushback Clearance for queue status.
2.Prior coordination with Swissport Iceman" is required for de-icing with Engines On.
This will only be performed if the APU is inoperative.
• Pushback procedures:
‐ Contact the Ramp TWR for pushback and start clearance.
‐ After pushback, request taxi to Apron W for de-icing.
‐ Expect enter/exit Apron W via TWY W3 and W4.
‐ The area between W3 and W4 is designated as a non-movement area.
• Arrival to Apron W:
‐ Hold short of TWY on W3 (unless told otherwise by ATC) and contact "Swissport Iceman" on
Freq. 131.975 (air-to-ground Freq) for parking position.
‐ Upon entering the apron W, follow marshaller signals to parking location.
‐ Set parking brake and configure aircraft for de-icing.
‐ Shutdown engines.
• Fluid application procedures:
‐ Iceman will confirm the parking brake is set and the aircraft is configured for de-icing.
‐ Iceman will ask Flight Crew for the requested method (one-step: type I only or two- step: type
I and then type IV) to be used and areas to be sprayed (i.e. wings and tail only).
‐ Iceman will notify the Flight Crew when de-icing/anti-icing is completed.
‐ Post de-icing/anti-Icing check - Critical surfaces will be inspected and the following
information given over the air-to-ground frequency:
▪Fluid brand name(s) / type(s) / concentration(s).
▪Start time in local time of the final fluid application.
▪Employee number of employee completing final inspection.
▪Aircraft is clean and free of contamination.
Note: • If an engineer or mechanic conducts the post de-icing/anti-icing inspection,
headset communication may be utilized.
• Do not move the aircraft until cleared by ICEMAN.
• Departure procedures:
‐ Iceman will confirm when the area is clear for engine start.
‐ Monitor “Swissport Iceman” on 131.975 until clear of apron W.
‐ Contact ground and request taxi clearance.
Planning
• Expect extensive vectoring after RWY heading.

altitudes.

SBGR.name: Guarulhos - Governador André Franco Montoro International
SBGR.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Guarulhos - Governador André Franco Montoro International.
Ground Handling: SWISSPORT
Terminal: 3.
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
10 H24 YES NO NO NIL YES
COMPANY POLICY
NIL
AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Busy ATC environment.
3.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
4.Low transition altitude.
General
• Joint civil and military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft TCAS may not be compatible with civil aircraft.
• Arrival delays are common.
• Busy landing periods: 07:30 to 10:30 (08:30 to 11:30) and 19:00 to 23:59 (20:00 to 00:59).
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION 1.Do not mistake Sao Paulo Guarulhos aerodrome with the following
aerodromes:
• Sao Paulo Campo De Marte, located 10 NM to the Southwest.
• Sao Paulo Congonhas, located 16 NM to the Southwest.
2.False Localizer and/or Glideslope capture has been reported on base leg.
3.Turbulence and strong crosswinds may be encountered on final approach.
4.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWYs for
RWY.
General
• Sao Paulo uses non ICAO phraseology.
• ATC, reported as being slow at issuing descent clearance. Prompting ATC may be required.
• If cleared to waypoint or Nav-aid not part of the planned route, verify routing after that point.
• Traffic sequencing: Enroute holding, speed reduction/radial intercepts maybe used.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Ensure all descent clearance provide adequate terrain clearance.
• “Cleared via arrival XXX” is an authorization to fly the arrival route at published FL/ altitudes.
“Cleared arrival XXX” is an authorization to fly the arrival routes XXX issued by ATC. Noted the
word “VIA” makes a difference, so when ATC issue, example “via USABA arrival” they will not
issue further descent clearance. Flight Crew must confirm with ATC that they are “cleared to
descend on the arrival” including any cleared FL or altitudes.
RWY Preference
• During periods of heavy landing traffic, side steps to RWY 10L may be used.
RWY 10R
• During busy period, arrival from North/Northeast to RWY 10R, expect long downwind radar legs,
at FL070/80 North of aerodrome, turning base clearance will be left turn, down 4,100ft. During
less busy periods, ATC may offer shortcuts resulting in reduced track miles and aircraft being
left high on profile.
GROUND MANEUVERING
General
• FOD have been reported. Exercise vigilance when maneuvering on the apron and comply with
the following:
‐ While docking into the stand, pay close attention to any lose items that could cause FOD to
the aircraft, use idle power unless higher than idle is absolutely required.
‐ During walk around, pay close attention to the aircraft surrounding area with special attention
to presence of FOD under the engine nacelle, tires and expected pushback track. Make sure
the Engineer is informed to remove identified objects to prevent FOD.
CODE E FLEET
TWYs
• Left turn from TWY FF to TWY B – NOT AUTHORIZED.
• Right turn from TWY DD to TWY B – NOT AUTHOHRIZED.
• Judgmental oversteering required:
‐ To/From TWY A.
‐ From TWY B to TWYs O and L, and vice versa.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Entering apron, follow solid taxilanes.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
General
• ATS flight plans are valid for EOBT + 35 minutes. If ATS flight plan has not been amended
beyond EOBT + 35 minutes, then a new ATS flight plan is required, taking 45 minutes to
become active. Flight Crew should take necessary action to ensure ATS flight plan remains
active during a delay period. Coordinate with local GHA or the outstation staff.
Refuelling with PAX onboard
• To enhance turnaround times, refuelling is to be commenced as soon as the aircraft arrives at
the parking stand. To expedite the process, brief Cabin Crew accordingly beforehand. Ensure
communication with Ground Staff is established before/during the refuel process.
Startup and Pushback
• Request ATC clearance 15 minutes before departure and include destination and requested FL.
Planning
• Cabin must be secured for take-off prior to reaching the holding point.
• If cabin is not secured prior reaching the holding point, advice ground control.
• When cleared to line-up or take-off, pilots shall ensure, considering safety, that they are able to
proceed expeditiously.
• When take-off clearance is received, aircraft must commence the take-off roll immediately.
MISCELLANEOUS
NIL

‐ Expect most international flights to divert from Sao Paulo to Rio De Janeiro, resulting
in traffic congestion and higher than planned fuel burn.

SCEL.name: Arturo Merino Benitez International
SCEL.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Arturo Merino Benitez International
Ground Handling: AGUNSA – 131.85
Terminal:
• Cargo - Cargo Apron
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 H24 YES NO YES NIL YES
WARNING Mountain waves and icing conditions may be encountered when crossing the
Cordillera de Los Andes, more probable during the winter season in the East side
of the Cordillera. Speed changes with no changes in altitude may be experienced,
resulting in Over Speed warnings.
Note: 1.Aerodrome is located in a noise sensitive area.
2.Applicable Noise Abatement Departure Procedure is NADP 1.
3.As per local regulations the aircraft must be fumigated before arrival to Santiago, the
process must be done at the last point of departure, even if it is a technical stop.
4.As per local regulations the consumption of alcohol is not allowed within 24 hours
before the departure for all crew members.
SCEL.company_policy:
• The speed limitation policy of 250 KIAS below 10,000ft AGL or the applicable MSA, whichever
is higher, is not applicable for approaches and departures in Santiago, which has an MSA of
19,000ft based on the AMB VOR.
When conforming to a normal flight profile as per published SIDs and STARs, the 19,000ft MSA
altitude will likely be crossed outside the published 25 NM MSA range. Therefore Flight Crews
are reminded to manage their speed effectively.
It is recommended to be at 250 KIAS when within 25 NM of the AMB VOR or 12,000ft AMSL,
whichever occurs first. This will assist in providing a smoother transition during descent and
climb phases of flight into Santiago.
PROCEDURE FOR CROSSING THE CORDILLERA DE LOS ANDES
All flights inbound to Santiago from the East:
• Before departure:
‐ At least 30 minutes before departure, Flight Crew must contact Flight Watch to confirm if any
new or updated turbulence SIGMET related has been issued and that the planned route is
still valid.
• After departure:
‐ At least 1 hour before crossing DOZ VOR, Flight Crew to contact Flight Watch to confirm
if any new or updated turbulence related SIGMET has been issued and may maintain the
planned route or not.
‐ At least 60 NM East of DOZ VOR, Flight Crew to request Mendoza Center for Turbulence
over the Cordillera Report and assess if re-routing or change in Flight Level is required.
For East bound departures:
• If a turbulence related SIGMET is available in the briefing package, Flight Crew to review and
confirm that the planned route avoids the affected area. If required, contact Flight Watch for
clarification.
• At least 30 minutes before departure, request ATC/TOWER for updated Turbulence Over the
Cordillera report. If a new or updated report is available, contact Flight Watch for an update to
the planned route or confirmation that the flight can continue on the planned route.
Use of seat belts sign when crossing the Cordillera de Los Andes
• Due to the turbulence associated with the Mountain Wave, it is required for passengers and
crew members to remain seated during the time of crossing the Cordillera de Los Andes.
• Departures
‐ From take-off to a pre-defined distance after crossing the Cordillera.
• Arrivals
‐ From a pre-defined distance before crossing the Cordillera until landing in Santiago.
• It is recommended to announce Crossing the Cordillera at least 10 minutes before turning the
Seat Belts Sign ON.
• The use of the Seat Belts Sign to restrict the passengers and crew movements can be initiated
or extended as required in compliance with Qatar Airways standard operating procedures at any
segment in the flight.
• The below tables provide the reference for the use of the Seat Belts Sign when crossing the
Cordillera.
‐ From May to August (when severe turbulence is more probable):
Departures from Santiago on SID
Via Waypoint Maintain the seat belts sign ON, from take-off until
ALBAL 30 NM to the East of SRA (San Rafael)
NEBEG or ALBAL 55 NM to the East of ESITO
ANKON 30 NM to the East of SRA (San Rafael)
GUVOL 30 NM to the East of SANU (San Juan)
Arrivals to Santiago from the East on STAR
Maintain the seat belts sign ON, from the
Via Waypoint
indicated location until landing in Santiago
UMKAL 55 NM to the East of MDZ (Mendoza)
ANKON 30 NM to the East of SRA (San Rafael)
ASIMO 30 NM to the East of SANU (San Juan)
‐ From September to April:
Departures from Santiago on SID
Via Waypoint Maintain the seat belts sign ON, from take-off until
ALBAL At least until overflying SRA (San Rafael)
NEBEG or ALBAL 30 NM to the East of ESITO
ANKON At least until overflying SRA (San Rafael)
GUVOL At least until overflying SANU (San Juan)
Arrivals to Santiago from the East on STAR
Maintain the seat belts sign ON, from the
Via Waypoint
indicated location until landing in Santiago
UMKAL 25 NM to the East of MDZ (Mendoza)
ANKON When overflying SRA (San Rafael)
ASIMO When overflying SANU (San Juan)

‐ Slower acceleration than normal should be expected.

CAUTION 1.High Density Altitude Aerodrome Operations.
2.Several volcanos located within 50 NM of Quito with frequent volcanic activity.
Information is published via NOTAMs.
SEQM.company_policy:
• All take-offs and landings shall be performed by Captains or a Captain under training only.
• Full approach procedures must be flown.
• Due to landing distance or brake cooling time calculation, if operationally required, the
Commander shall consider the use of full reversers upon landing. Special consideration shall be
given to RWY conditions other than DRY.
• To prevent the possibility of Tire Speed Limit exceedance and to support payload determination,
the following should be used for performance calculations:
‐ In the case of winds between 0 to 5Kt headwinds, use 0Kt wind.
‐ When head wind is higher than 5Kt, use 5Kt (head wind).
‐ In case the wind conditions differ from the above after the doors are closed or after pushback,
Flight Crew shall check the wind component to comply with the aircraft performance
limitations for take-off.
• EGPWS must be operational before departure to Quito.
• EGPWS must be operational for night operations to/from Quito. This limitation may be waived
by VPFO/CFOO. The choice to dispatch/continue the flight, once approved by the VPFO/CFOO
or their designee, rests solely with the commander.
• Seat belts SIGN ON not later than top of descent. All galley equipment fully secured and
checked on Cargo aircraft at this time.

encountered on final approach. Both RWY ends have steep drop-offs, possible
downdraughts/turbulences on finals.

• The wind direction/speed change due to the terrain, generating tail wind in different segments of
the approach.
STARs/ Radar Vectoring
• Expect radar vectors.
• Ensure all descent clearance provide adequate terrain clearance.
• Speed is restricted to 250 KIAS below FL180. Flight Crew must not accept any optional high
speed below FL180.
• Guayaquil center transfers the aircraft to Approach at 50nm out of QIT VOR and at FL260.
RWY 18
• ILS or LOC
‐ After crossing ‘QIT’ VOR, the use of speed brake may be necessary for height loss and
speed control; however Company stabilization policy must be complied with.
‐ Flight Crew must be aware that VNAV path may intercept the LOC above the GS.
• In case of single engine missed approach, refer to the Departure Procedures for additional
information about the EOSID.
• RADIO CALLOUT
‐ Whilst descending on the ILS, due to the undulating nature of the terrain three 1,000ft
calls can be expected. Flight Crew must be aware to make the 1,000ft stable call based on
barometric altitude.
RWY 36
• Arrivals from Brazil or Argentina, Flight Crew can coordinate with Guayaquil Control the routing
via ENVIG DCT QNV DCT QIT then ILS Z RWY 36.
• Approaching via CONDORCOCHA (QIT); note the crossing altitude and speed restriction after
CONDORCOHA in which it is recommended to configure the aircraft early.

OAKB.name: Kabul International
OAKB.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Kabul International
Ground Handling: GAAC Holding
Terminal: TBA
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
8 Refer to Note 1 YES NO YES NIL NO
WARNING Due to the dynamic threat situation in the vicinity of Kabul airport, Flight Crew
should contact the IOC or Dispatch Office for current intelligence updates/briefs
prior to departure.
CAUTION 1.High Density Altitude Aerodrome Operations.
2.Persons and wildlife have been observed in the maneuvering areas.
Note: 1. Operating Hours: Refer to NOTAM for latest update.
2. Customs and Immigration Operating Hours: 00:00 to 17:30.
3. Aerodrome has limited ATC services.
4. Aerodrome lighting system not reliable.
5. METAR and TAF NOT available.
6. Before departure from Doha, confirm from IOC, Kabul landing permission is received.
7. DOH-KBL-DOH route should be planned via Pakistan.
8. Additional holding fuel must be considered before departure to Kabul.
9. Taking photographs on ground is strictly prohibited.
10.Security search may be conducted by dog units on arrival and pre-departure.
11.An engineer will travel on-board the aircraft on sector operating to KBL and will be
included on the GD.
OAKB.company_policy:
• Operations to Kabul should be planned during DAYLIGHT only.
• Operation is not authorized if icing conditions are reported/observed at the aerodrome.
• Flight Crew shall make all possible attempts to obtain the latest aerodrome weather conditions
from Kabul Tower or IOC prior to commencing descend. If icing conditions are reported at the
aerodrome a diversion plan should be coordinated with IOC.
• If RWY condition assessment report is not available/reliable, departure from a contaminated
RWY or in active icing conditions is not authorized.
• All takeoffs and landings shall be performed by Captain only.
• If operationally required due to landing distance or Brake Cooling Time Calculation, the
commander shall consider the use of full reverses upon landing, special consideration shall be
given to RWY conditions other than DRY.
• APU, EGPWS, and TCAS must be operational before departure to Kabul.
• Uplift of potable water from Kabul is NOT permitted.
• Tankering to Kabul shall only be considered if there are no performance limitations. If
performance limited, plan for two alternates. In the event re-fueling is required, ensure fuel is
available prior to dispatch.
• Dispatch Criteria (Winter Season):
‐ Dispatch is required to check all available resources for possible icing conditions or
precipitation.
‐ RWY considered as wet for performance calculation.
• Minimums: Ceiling 2,500ft AGL and visibility 5,000m.
OAKB.atc:
WARNING 1.Kabul FIR is uncontrolled. Flight Crew must apply Traffic Information
Broadcasts (TIBA) procedure on 125.2 during overflight to Kabul FIR.
2.Uncontrolled traffic activity has been reported around the aerodrome. Exercise
extra vigilance and report to TWR.
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Heavy military, Drones, UAV and Kites activity in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponders may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
• Kabul airspace:
‐ CTR: Class D.
‐ CTA: Class G (TIBA procedures apply).
Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• Some VHF blocking can occur around the aerodrome.
• Lost communication procedures procedure- ICAO procedure supplemented by the following
excerpt (as depicted in Jeppesen):
‐ Arrival Procedure
▪In the event of two-way communications failure, squawk 7600 and continue to monitor
guard frequencies, refer to Jeppesen Text Manual ‘EMERGENCY’ Afghanistan-1.
‐ Departure Procedure
▪In the event of lost communications on departure, contact Kabul TWR. If no reply, squawk
7600 and continue to monitor guard frequencies, execute the published departure
procedure to LOBRE, hold south of LOBRE on the Kabul R-195 for 15 minutes, one zero
mile legs, right turns, maintain 14000ft. Climb to FL170 then proceed direct WEBRO and
execute the ILS RWY 29 and attempt to contact TWR.
Preferential RWY
• RWY 29 is preferred RWY for landing and take-off up to 10kt tailwind component.
Nav-Aids
• Radio communication and performance of Nav-Aids can deteriorate at short notice.
• Nav-Aids are not continuously monitored by TWR.
OAKB.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Check the current status of Nav-Aids with Kabul TWR prior landing.
2.Turbulence and strong crosswinds may be encountered on final approach.
3.ILS critical area not protected. Ground movements may affect ILS signals for
RWY 29.
4.RWY centerline markings are faded and difficult to see, especially during hours
of darkness or adverse weather conditions.
5.ATC may offer direct routes or shortcut, exercise vigilance to check the Max
Landing Weight if accepted.
Terrain – Kabul Area Overview
General
• Communication with Kabul TWR can be expected when within 100 NM from Kabul aerodrome.
• PAPI has been reported as visible only at 2 NM. Confirm with TWR the status of PAPIs.
• Maximum speed below FL200 is 250 Kt.
• No radar coverage.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• STARs are not published.
Local Procedure
• TWR does NOT provide landing clearance.
• Expect TWR to advise: ‘RWY XX IN USE, WIND CONDITION XXX/XX AND LAND AT OWN
DISCRETION’.
RWY 11
• Guided Visual procedure is authorized. Refer to 19-0 “Attention All Users Page” for additional
details.
RWY 29
• RNAV (GPS) Approaches - NOT AUTHORIZED.
• Early aircraft configuration is recommended.
• Early glide slope capture leading to climb and altitude burst were reported.
• Expect to maintain cruise level until waypoint WEBRO and then descend on the holding pattern
for ILS approach.
• During the final approach, Flight Crew should ensure that they are established on Localizer
before leaving platform height to avoid terrain alert triggering on the final approach.
• It is recommended to configure the aircraft early. ATC generally keeps the aircraft above the
charted altitude. During the final approach, Flight Crew should ensure that they are established
on Localizer before leaving platform height unless ATC provides vectors to avoid terrain left of
the final approach.
• Terrain profile dips on final approach near threshold RWY 29.
• The below pictorial shows the Close proximity with terrain (Approximate position: ‘MYLOE’ at
10,600 ft).
Close Proximity Terrain
Vertical Profile for Approach RWY 29
• Below Vertical Profile table is based on IAKW ILS/DME, to be used when performing ILS
Approach RWY 29 to confirm ILS accuracy.
RECOMMENDED ALTITUDES RWY 29
FPA 3.5
IAKW DME ALTITUDE
10.3 9750
9.0 9310
8.0 8940
7.0 8570
6.0 8200

1.0 6340
Note: In case of IAKW DME U/S, the above table can be used for vertical guidance when
referenced to RWY 29 THR as distance difference from ILS DME is 0.1NM.
OAKB.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION 1.FODs have been reported in TWYs and Aprons.
2.RWYs and TWYs are poorly lit.
RWY
• RWY surface has been reported rough and bumpy.
• 180° turn on the RWY - NOT AUTHORIZED.
• Taxi slowly to vacate RWY due to presence of FODs.
TWYs
• No stop bars lights and TWY signage.
• Judgmental oversteering required.
Apron/Parking stands
• 'Follow-me’ vehicle is available on request.
• Confirm towbar is available before parking nose-in.
• Apron has uneven surface in certain areas.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
OAKB.departure_procedures:
Planning
• Refueling with Pax Onboard - NOT AUTHORIZED.
• Intersection takeoff - NOT AUTHORIZED.
• Flight Crew shall broadcast the intended RWY, departure track and cruising level on the TIBA
Freq. 120.6 not less than 10 minutes before takeoff.
• One airborne, do not change the departure frequency unless instructed by ATC.
• Expect communication loss with Kabul over LAJAK, crew to contact Pakistan ATC.
• Expect FL270 in Kabul FIR until crossing LAJAK, coordinate with Pakistan ATC for higher FL.
Startup and Push-back
• Requests start-up and push-back clearance from tower when fully ready.
• NIL Ground Support during engine start, ensure all areas are clear before closing doors and
starting the engines.
SIDs
• ATC may not use the SID names and therefore may describe the SID e.g. runway heading to
8,000ft, right turn to KBL.
• Climb via SID and comply with the SID climb gradient.
• If unable to comply with climb gradient, inform ATC.
• Exercise vigilant during the climb, observe the TCAS.
Climb Out Considerations
• Flight Crew should make every effort to obtain the best climb out performance until reaching
cruise altitude.
• The below points should be considered (subject to any operational restrictions) and briefed
before departure:
‐ Amend the all engine acceleration and Thrust reduction height as per below:
▪ACCEL HT: 5,000ft.
▪THR REDUCTION: 5,000ft.
‐ Use of maximum TO thrust rating available for the actual TOW and atmospheric conditions.
(Including the use of WINDSHEAR RTG).
‐ Use of maximum CLB Thrust.
‐ Maintain Flaps-up Maneuvering Speed until passing MSA.
Note: Maintain at least 15 knots above minimum maneuver speed when climbing through
FL200 to prevent the EICAS caution message, “AIRSPEED LOW” from occurring.
‐ When clear of obstacles and above MSA, Flight Crew may request SID track miles shortening
or direct routing.
• Monitor pitch attitude, airspeed, and climb rate in order to make sure you are obtaining the best
climb performance.

AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
Note: During periods of bad weather, i.e. TS, Beijing Capital may stop accepting traffic. Nearby
alternates will fill up quickly. Flight Crew should monitor weather, liase with ATC on
alternate options.
Flight below Transition Level/Transition Altitude (TL/TA)
• Three (3) Transition Altitudes in use, depending on QNH.
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION 1.Flight Crew should be prepared for rushed approaches as frequent RWY/STAR
changes are very common.
2.Do not mistake the aerodrome boundary and highway lights for RWY lights.
3.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWYs for
landing RWY.
Descent
• Early descents are common along with long low level radar vectoring.
• ATC controller may not advise STAR in use on first contact.
• When landing on southerly direction RWY, i.e. RWY 19 or RWY 18L, delayed descent clearance
due safety altitudes may occur, resulting in reduced track miles and aircraft being left high on
profile.
Approaches
• Some missed approaches require higher than standard climb gradient. If unable to comply,
advise ATC as soon as possible.
RWY 18R/36L
• RWY width is 50m and can create an illusion of being low during the approach and landing flare.
• Long taxi-in times to be expected. Note the crossing RWY rules.
GROUND MANEUVERING
CAUTION Ensure correct understanding of taxi instructions and familiarize with the routing
and location of TWYs.
TWYs
• Judgemental steering required.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
General
• Aircraft delayed by more than 30 minutes, the flight plan must be modified to show revised
EOBT.
Planning
• Last minute changes in departure RWY is common.
• Initial planned FLs are commonly unavailable due congestion.
De-icing
• Inform the GHA the requirement for de/anti-icing treatment at least 1 hour before the STD.
• The request for de/anti-icing must include the type of procedure (one or two-step) and the
aircraft surfaces requiring treatment (wings, fuselage, horizontal stabilizer, vertical stabilizer).

• Three (3) Transition Altitudes in use, depending on QNH.
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION 1.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel RWY and
TWY for landing RWY.
2.RWY 01L/19R has the same QDM as RWYs 17L/35R and 17R/35L but
designated differently to avoid confusion.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Ensure all descent clearances provide adequate terrain clearance.
GROUND MANEUVERING
CAUTION Ensure correct understanding of taxi instructions and familiarize with the routing
and location of TWYs.
Apron/Parking stands
• Expect to be parked on stand 183. Other parking stands may be offered.
• Apron blue color taxi lines have been reported difficult to see during hours of darkness or during
adverse weather conditions when joining the normal taxi lines for taxi out.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWYs/apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due to the
proximity of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and
request marshaller/wingwalker.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
Planning
• Last minute changes in departure RWY is common.
• Initial planned FLs are commonly unavailable due congestion.
SIDs
• Several SIDs have similar designator with different tracks. Flight Crew shall verify that the
cleared SID is inserted into the FMS and briefed accordingly.
De-icing
• Inform the GHA the requirement for de/anti-icing treatment at least 1 hour before the STD.
• De/Anti-icing fluid information:
‐ Type II Fluid Brand: Newave Aerochemical FCY-2.
‐ Mixing Ratio: 100/0.
Note: The post treatment report (Anti-icing code) will not contain the fluid information.

AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Busy ATC environment.
3.ATC Controller may speak rapidly and use non-ICAO standards phraseology
(eg. continue South on TWY F and call ground 121.85). If unsure about
clearance, clarify.
Flight below Transition Level/Transition Altitude (TL/TA)
• Three (3) Transition Altitudes in use, depending on QNH.
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION TCAS alert may occur due to possibility of simultaneous approaches.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Expect radar vectoring.
• Entering Guangzhou approach area, radar vectoring/sequence to “middle approach segment” or
to time RWY is in sight.
• During simultaneous approaches, assigned STARs may contain additional track miles.
• Do not accept visual approach clearance until landing RWY is correctly identified.
Local Procedure
• Contact APP on assigned frequency before entering into the Approach Control Area.
• Traffic circuits of RWY 02R/20L shall be made to the East, and traffic circuits of RWY 02L/ 20R
shall be made to the West.
• Traffic circuit altitude: 1,650ft to 1,970ft (500m to 600m).
GROUND MANEUVERING
CAUTION Ensure correct understanding of taxi instructions and familiarize with the routing
and location of TWYs.
TWYs
• TWY P3 and TWY P4 holding point is located at the entry of the TWY, before the RWY warning
lights. Flight Crews are reminded NOT to cross the holding point without ATC clearance to avoid
RWY incursion, below graphic for reference only.
TWY Holding Point
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWYs/apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due to the
proximity of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and
request Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• Follow-me vehicle service available via Ground Control.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
General
• Aircraft delayed by more than 30 minutes, the ATC flight plan must be modified to show revised
EOBT.
Planning
• Necessary weather avoidance immediately after take-off should be coordinated prior to the
issue of take-off clearance wherever possible and should, if practicable be requested to the East
due to parallel operations on RWY 01/19.

VHHH.name: Hong Kong International
VHHH.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Hong Kong International
Ground Handling:
• PAX: Hong Kong Airport Services Limited (HAS) / 131.6 (Call sign: HAS Dispatch).
• Cargo: Hong Kong Air Cargo Terminal (HACTL)
Terminal: TBA
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
10 H24 YES NO YES NIL YES
CAUTION Birds in vicinity of aerodrome.
Note: 1.Refer to the current NOTAMs for RWY closure timings due to the periodic
maintenance.
2.Hong Kong has capacity restrictions and SLOTS are enforced for arrival/departure.
Flight Crews are reminded to comply with the scheduled arrival/departure times to
avoid incurring significant fines or penalties.
COMPANY POLICY
NIL
AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL
CAUTION 1.Hong Kong is a busy and challenging aerodrome with very limited airspace.
Flight Crew must understand and adhere strictly to ATC clearances issued.
Strict adherence and compliance with SIDs and STARs in term of routing and
altitude restrictions is MANDATORY unless instructed otherwise by ATC.
2.Flight Crew are to ensure cleared SIDs and STARs are inserted in the FMS
along with the appropriate transitions and vias.
3.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
4.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
5.Laser targeting has been reported. Inform ATC when applicable.
General
• Weather Avoidance
‐ Obtain ATC permission prior to making any deviation from prescribed departure track.
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
WARNING 1.An ATC clearance to proceed via a STAR on arrival into Hong Kong does not
imply a clearance for descent. Flight Crew are not to descend unless issued a
specific clearance to do so.
2.All cleared altitudes must be confirmed with the relevant chart. Descend below
charted altitude is strictly forbidden unless explicitly cleared by the ATC and
confirmed by the Flight Crew.
3.False Localizer capture and Localizer overshoot have been reported, Flight
Crew to monitor raw data in order to confirm proper ILS capture and allow for
early intervention if required.
CAUTION Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWYs for
landing RWY.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Call Hong Kong radar at least 5 minutes before the FIR boundary.
• Do not descend below the flight level assigned by Guangzhou ACC, before confirming with
Hong Kong radar on handover.
• Expect lower flight level at waypoint “SIERA” and note speed restrictions. Descent on an
assigned STAR without a specific clearance to do so is prohibited.
• During off-peak times, track shortening can be offered.
RWY 25R
• RNAV TRANSITION to ILS procedure has multiple altitude restrictions. Flight Crew shall use
proper aircraft energy management to comply with altitude constraints.
CODE E FLEET
Speed Control
• Adherence to published and ATC assigned speeds is required.
GROUND MANEUVERING
RWY
• RWY 07C: Section of the RWY with arrow markings between TWYs B2 or B3 and TWYs A1 or
F1 shall not be used for landing and take-off.
• RWY 25C: Section of the RWY with arrow markings between TWYs A12 or F8 and TWYs B10
or B11 shall not be used for landing and take-off.
• All RWY surfaces have been reported as rough.
Apron/Parking Stands
• APU Usage
‐ The use of the APU shall be reduced to the absolute minimum required.
‐ APU may only be used for 5 minutes after arrival and 25 minutes prior to push-back.
‐ APU may be used at remote parking stands during turnaround.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
General
• Delays in excess of 30 minutes from filed EOBT, ATC flight plans must be amended due
coordination with adjacent ATS units.
Startup and Push-back
• For engine start up, one engine will be started during pushback and second engine will be
started only after pushback is completed.
Clearance Crosscheck
• ATC may only clear Flight Crew for the SID and not the required Terminal transition route. Flight
Crew are to confirm the Terminal Transition route that joins the SID to the Airway.
• Terminal Transition route charts are available in the SID charts.
• Flight Crew must exercise the highest levels of vigilance when accepting and reading back
clearances.
Planning
• Adherence to SIDs or vectors is required.
• Do not request direct route immediately after departure.
• Aircraft outbound from Hong Kong are required to reach cruising level at or before FIR
boundary.
• Ships may be present in the takeoff paths of RWY 25L/R in the Anchorage and/or Fairway
maritime facilities as shown in the graphic below.
• For takeoff performance - FSA/OPT provide options to select the appropriate obstacle set for
each takeoff RWY. Different ship heights are added as Special Procedures (#SPn) to FSA/OPT
for crew selection.
• ATC will advise the ship/vessel heights. Flight Crews must select the FSA/OPT #SPn
accordingly.
• If the ATC reports a ship/vessel higher than the height included in the selected #SPn, the
takeoff shall be delayed until the ship/vessel height is reported to be equal or lower than height
mentioned in #SPn.
Overview Vessels
MISCELLANEOUS
Cargo operations - Security Procedure
• Until Security Authorities provide cargo Flight Crew with baggage check in service, Flight Crew
are advised to:
‐ Refrain from carrying sharp objects (i.e. shaving razors, nail clipper, etc.) in either hand bags
or suite cases as both will have to be carried on-board by the individual Flight Crew.
‐ If any such items are discovered in individual Flight Crew baggage and it is confiscated by
security during baggage screening, Flight Crew should not object and proceed to the aircraft
without arguing with the security staff to avoid unnecessary delays.
‐ If required, during the stay in Hong Kong, Flight Crew could request personal hygiene items
such as shaving razors from the hotel staff.

RWY Preference

ZHCC.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing.
• Do not mistake parallel road located to the North of aerodrome for RWY
12L/30R.
• Do not mistake parallel TWYs for landing RWY.
2.RWY markings are difficult to see during hours of darkness and/or adverse
weather conditions.
3.RWY lights and PAPI have been reported as very dim.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Ensure all descent clearances provide adequate terrain clearance.
ZHCC.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION Consider aircraft wingspan, TWYs/apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due
to the proximity of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists,
stop aircraft and request Marshaller/Wingwalker. Ensure correct understanding of
taxi instructions and familiarize with the routing and location of TWYs.
TWYs
• Judgemental steering is required for the following turns:
‐ Right turn from TWY H4 to TWY H and vice versa.
‐ Left turn from TWY H8 to TWY H and vice versa.
‐ Right turn from TWY D7 to TWY D and vice versa.
‐ Left turn from TWY D6 to TWY D and vice versa.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Expected Parking Stands:
‐ North Cargo Apron: 601 to 616.
‐ Terminal 1: 28 to 35 and aircraft is guided by ‘Follow-me’ car for Entry/Exit.
‐ Terminal 2: 901 to 908 or 267 to 270.
ZHCC.departure_procedures:
Pushback
• During peak hours, tow trucks will be positioned according to aircraft readiness and ATC aircraft
release sequence. If tow truck is not in position for push back at time of departure, ground staff
may request to turn beacon light ON to indicate readiness.
RWY 12L/30R Departure Procedure: Holding position on TWY C
• RWY 12L/30R departure, exercise extra vigilance regarding the Holding Point on TWY C
located to the north side of RWY 12L/30R. This is indicated by a “Pattern B Holding Line” set at
this position. CAT I / II (12L) and CAT I (30R) signs are set beside the RWY number sign at the
holding position.
RWY 12L CAT I/II sign
RWY 30R CAT I sign
• When ATC instructs to taxi to CAT I/II holding point on RWY 12L/30R via TWY A, C8, C13, C18,
C, aircraft is expected to taxi to the “Pattern B Holding Line” and hold short of it, to avoid any
interference on ILS signal.
Pattern B Holding Line
RWY 12L/30R Departure Taxi Routes
• RWY 12L departure:
‐ ATC will instruct to taxi to holding point of RWY12L on TWY C4, it usually means they intend
to use TWY C4 for RWY crossing rather than intersection departure. The aircraft should
expect to cross RWY via TWY C4 to taxi to TWY D1 for full length departure.
• RWY 30R departure:
‐ ATC will instruct to taxi to holding point of RWY 30R on TWY C22, it usually means they
intend to use TWY C22 for RWY crossing rather than intersection departure. The aircraft
should expect to cross RWY via TWY C22 to taxi to TWY D12 for full length departure.
Taxi Routes

confirm with ground personnel if towing to final parking position is required.

VIAR.name: Sri Guru Ram Dass Jee International Airport
VIAR.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Sri Guru Ram Dass Jee International Airport.
Ground Handling: Air India Airport Services - NIL.
Terminal: N/A
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
8 H24 NIL NO YES NIL YES
CAUTION 1.Birds in vicinity of aerodrome.
2.Wildlife activity (dog) in and around the aerodrome.
Note: RFF upgrade to CAT 9 on request.
VIAR.atc:
CAUTION 1.Route deviations - Arrival/departure/go-around/weather deviations require prior
coordination with ATC due to the proximity of Amritsar aerodrome to Pakistan
border (located 13 NM).
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Multiple reports of GPS interference in the vicinity of the aerodrome. Flight
Crew must monitor aircraft navigation performance. Refer to the relevant
FCOM Supplementary Procedure and FOTs.
4.Low transition altitude.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponders may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
• NIL STARs/SIDs published, expect radar vector on final approach.
Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• Multiple frequencies to take care of at the same time.
Local Procedure
• Minimum RWY occupancy must be followed. Flight Crew should inform ATC if unable.
• Local Airspace Restrictions. Departure and Go-around:
‐ RWY 16: Right Turns – NOT AUTHORIZED.
‐ RWY 34: Left Turns – NOT AUTHORIZED.
VIAR.arrival_procedures:
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Arrival via Lahore FIR:
‐ Expect to cross waypoint RABAN (Pakistan/ India boundary) at FL110 or above, followed by
entering VOR hold to commence descent.
‐ Contact Alpha Control (Indian Air force) on frequency 119.7 and Delhi Control on frequency
124.55 to report position for RABAN during descent.
RWY 16
• Contact Amritsar TWR 10 minutes prior to waypoint RABAN.
• Approach Lights (Horizontal bar) reported as being extremely bright.
RWY 34
• Normal RWY in use.
• Circuit pattern is a nonstandard Right Hand pattern.
• If simultaneous departure is in place during aircraft arrival, expect right turn over AAR to
proceed on radial 135 AAR and then self position for ILS 34.
VIAR.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION 1.Avoid excessive breaking when landing on RWY 34 to exit into TWY E. It is
recommended to use the turning pad as required and in liaison with ATC.
2.RWY crossing point for military traffic located between TWY E and the fire
station.
3.Animals have been observed in all maneuvering areas at the aerodrome.
Maintain a continuous watch and report as necessary to ATC.
TWYs
• Parallel TWY (West of RWY) – NOT AUTHORIZED.
• TWY F centreline and markings/signs have been reported as faded and difficult to see,
especially during hours of darkness.
VIAR.departure_procedures:
Planning
• RWY 34: Due to the proximity of Amritsar aerodrome to Pakistan border, it is recommended not
to exceed 5 NM before setting course to exit point RABAN.
• FL crossing restriction at waypoint RABAN (India/Pakistan boundary) required by Lahore ATC,
results climbing within the VOR hold.
• Contact Alpha control (India Airforce) and Lahore ATC before crossing waypoint RABAN to
obtain appropriate clearance.

550m at estimated time of use.

VOCL.company_policy:
• Taxi, take-off and landing by Captain or Captain under training only.
• Surface Conditions: Operations is not permitted when RWY surface is contaminated.
VOCL.atc:
CAUTION Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
General
• SIDs/STARs are not published. Procedural separation with the use of DME and radials to
separate arriving and departing aircraft.
VOCL.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.False GS captures have been reported.
2.Both RWY ends have steep drop-offs.
3.Turbulence and strong crosswinds may be encountered on final approach.
General
• During times of holding, aircraft will not be cleared for the approach until preceding aircraft has
landed. Full ILS procedure to RWY 28 approximate 10 minutes duration.
• Due to lack of surrounding lighting in vicinity of the aerodrome, a “black hole” effect may be
experienced during night operations.
RWY 10/28
• ILS G/S has been reported as unreliable beyond 10 NM from the RWY and when below 500ft
during approach.
• RWY 10/28 has significant upslope. The below pictorial is for reference only.
RWY 10/28 slope
RWY 28 - Lead-in-lighting system:
• 4 Light units, each consisting of a group of 3 sequential flashing white lights in a linear
configuration installed on the final APP.
• Distance from THR RWY 28;
‐ 6,193m (3.3 NM),
‐ 4,721m (2.5 NM),
‐ 3,121m (1.7 NM) - offset by 16.5m towards right while approaching of extended RWY
centreline,
‐ 1,826M (1 NM).
VOCL.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION 1.After heavy rain, areas of standing water may exist on the RWY. Check with the
TWR the latest RWY surface conditions and braking action.
2.Animals have been observed in all maneuvering areas at the aerodrome.
Maintain a continuous watch and report as necessary to ATC.
General
• Aerodrome lighting is very poor.
RWY
• RWY surface has been reported as rough and uneven with possibility of broken asphalt in some
places.
Apron/Parking Stands
• TWY C may be used as an overflow for parking aircraft when the apron is congested.
• Apron flood lights may impair visibility.
• When assigned parking stand is occupied isolation bay is used for holding aircraft.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWYs/apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due to the
proximity of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and
request marshaller/wingwalker.

VOCI.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponder may not be compatible with civil aircraft TCAS.
VOCI.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWY for
landing RWY.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Expect radar vectors to final approach.
VOCI.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION While parking at a stand served with aerobridge, do not use Marshaller guidance
for parking in case the electronic guidance system fails or is unserviceable. Stop
the aircraft, notify ATC and request for tow-in.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• In case of VDGS inoperative or in case of wrong aircraft indication on VDGS stop the aircraft,
notify ATC and request for tow-in to the Parking position.
• Exercise vigilance during rainy condition due to light reflections on wet surfaces.
Note: Isolation bay is suitable for CODE C and E fleets.
VOCI.departure_procedures:
ATS Flight Plan
• Delays of 30 minutes or more, ATS flight plan must be amended to reflect the revised departure
time.
Engine Start-up Procedures
• Engine start-up is permitted only after pushback is completed.
• If APU is inoperative, one engine start-up (idle thrust) is permitted at the parking stand.
Maintenance Engineer must coordinate with the Apron/Airport Authorities to get the
authorization. Captain after coordination with Maintenance Engineer shall make the request to
ATC for push-back with start-up positively informing “Apron coordinated.

VOGO.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Dabolim
Ground Handling: Air India Airport Services.
Terminal: TBA
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
8 H24 YES NO YES NIL NO
CAUTION Wildlife activity on and in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
Note: 1.Aerodrome not available for scheduled flight from Monday to Friday 03:00 to 07:00
and 10:00 to 11:00 due to military operations. Prior coordination mandatory for non -
scheduled operation during these periods.
2.Peak hours for civil operations: 07:30 to 10:30.
3.RWY 08/26 closed for periodic maintenance every year from 01 to 07 May from 02:00
to 07:00.
4.For delayed flights ex Doha, check with Dispatch/IOCC before departing due to limited
parking stands and military restrictions.
5.Announcement about Prohibition of Photography:
• Flight Crew is reminded to adhere to the requirement of making announcement
regarding prohibition of aerial and ground photography while overflying and during
arrival and departure from Naval air station, Dabolim (Goa), Vizag, and Port Blair.
• Photography of these sensitive defense areas is against the interest of national
security. It is therefore requested that QR complies with the requirement of making
the announcement regarding prohibition of aerial photography whilst operating from
above mentioned aerodromes.
• All observed cases of non compliance must be reported to concerned security and
law enforcement agencies under intimation to this HQRS for suitable necessary
action.

CAUTION 1.Restricted airspaces in the vicinity of the aerodrome.

RFF RETI

10 H24 NIL NO YES NIL YES

CODE E FLEET
MAX Pavement Weight in Tons for RWY

B789 210
COMPANY POLICY
• VOR A RWY 16L/R approach – NOT AUTHORIZED.
• Only Captain and Captain under training are authorized to conduct LDA approaches to RWY 22
and RWY 23.
AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Busy aerodrome and ATC environment.
General
• Prompt response to ATC instructions or clearance for take-off and RWY crossing.
Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• Refer to Area Briefing Language/Radio Transmission (R/T) for “Read Back Procedure”.
Use of transponder
• Arrival: Turn off the Mode S transponder immediately on arriving at the stand/spot.
• Departure: Turn on Mode S transponder before pushback.
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
Note: 1.Visual aids depicted on (visual prescribed tracks) approach charts RWY 22 and RWY
23 are difficult to identify from air during night.
2.Rushed approaches may occur due to frequent RWY/STAR changes.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Do not accept visual approach clearance until landing RWY is correctly identified.
Planning
• Radar vectoring may give short cut to route. High descent rates may be instructed.
• ATC may assign specific arrival routings and approaches due to strict noise abatement
procedures. When directed or requested by ATC, it is acceptable to fly the LDA RWY 23 or LDA
RWY 22 approaches, instead of the ILS or RNP. All other restrictions in OM Part A and OM Part
C remain applicable.
Approach – Airbus Fleet 14:00 to 21:00
RWY usage Southerly Configuration RWY usage Northerly Configuration
RWY 23 34R
IAP LDA Y (Note 1) ILS
Note 1:
• LDA Y is the standard approach used in a Southerly RWY configuration. (Summer Months).
• If conditions permit, ILS RWY 34R can be requested, advise ATC as early as possible, but
expect holding up to 40 minutes due traffic sequencing.
• Night arrival are planned via waypoint KAIHO, keeping aircraft over Tokyo Bay for noise
reasons.
• ILS Y RWY 23 is only used when weather conditions dictate its use.
Approach – Boeing Fleet 14:00 to 21:00
RWY usage Southerly Configuration RWY usage Northerly Configuration
RWY 23 34R
IAP LDA Y (Note 1) / RNP(AR) (Note 2) ILS
Note 1:
• LDA Y is the standard approach used in a Southerly RWY configuration. (Summer months).
• If conditions permit, ILS RWY 34R can be requested, advise ATC as early as possible, but
expect holding up to 40 minutes due traffic sequencing.
• Night arrival are planned via waypoint KAIHO, keeping aircraft over Tokyo Bay for noise
reasons.
• ILS Y RWY 23 is only used when weather conditions dictate its use.
• B787: Integrated Approach Navigation (IAN) function cannot be used for LDA.
• B777/B787: Suggested approach mode for LDA Y RWY 23 is LOC/VNAV. Do not start final turn
too early as to avoid undershoot.
Note 2: B787:
• Flight Crew who intend to conduct RNP (AR) APCH shall request RNP (AR) APCH to ATC.
• ATC will issue the clearance using the following phraseology: "Cleared for RNP (AR) RWY23
Approach”.
• Use of AP is recommended.
• Refer to FCOM: Supplementary Procedure / Automatic Flight / Instrument Approach - RNP
(AR).
• Refer to QRH: RNP (AR) Approach Review.
RWY 04
• NIL IAP and ALS.
RWY 34L
• VOR approach is offset.
Missed Approach
• Contact ATC immediately on commencing a missed approach due possible traffic conflict with
landing traffic RWY 22 and/or departure traffic RWY 16L.
GROUND MANEUVERING
TWY
• TWY C1 has upslope towards RWY34R. Flight Crews should exercise proper energy
management to avoid aircraft from stopping.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
CAUTION Wake turbulence threat for aircraft departing on RWY 05 (at or about V1) from
aircraft landing on RWY 34R.
SIDs
• Confirm that ATC acknowledges read back of SID and Transition assigned, before proceeding.
• Expect OPPAR Departure.
• Strict adherence to SID routings are mandatory. Flight Crews are reminded to review the SID
designators based on departure timings as depicted on the Jeppesen 10-3 charts.
MISCELLANEOUS
NIL

RJAA.company_policy:
• The following restrictions are applicable when using TWY K between E2 and B8 and vice versa.
‐ Taxi by Captain only.
‐ Max ground speed: 10Kt.
‐ LVP conditions – NOT AUTHORIZED.
RJAA.atc:
CAUTION 1.Busy aerodrome and ATC environment due to several nearby aerodromes.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• Refer to Area Briefing Language/Radio Transmission (R/T) for “Read Back Procedure”.
ATC Procedures
• Spacing between arrival/departures maybe minimal. Expect minimum RWY occupancy
consistent with safe operations.
RJAA.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Do not mistake Tateyama (RJTE) located 5 NM Southwest for Tokyo Narita
aerodrome.
2.Low Level wind shear and moderate turbulence may be encountered when
wind is from Northwest or Southwest with 15Kt strength or greater.
RWY 16L/R
• To minimize collateral damage from items falling from an aircraft on the approach, the landing
gear shall be extended before IKF 5.5 DME (16R) and ITM 5.5 DME (16L), unless safety
reasons preclude this.
RWY 34L/R
• Gear down procedure. ATIS will include reminder for this procedure.
RWY 34R
• Obstruction on Approach. There are trees penetrating above the approach surface at the area
about 400m before the RWY 34R threshold.
RWY 34R: Pilot view and obstructions
Obstructions - Vertical view
RJAA.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION TWY routings are complicated. Include this as a threat in the briefing and maintain
vigilance at all times.
TWYs
• Taxi routing: Standard taxi routing published and identified by route number unless otherwise
advised by ATC.
• TWY K:
‐ Inbound to apron between B8 and E1 GWY (Gateway). Steep downslope. Exercise vigilance
during slippery conditions.
‐ The following pictorial views are for Flight Crew orientation purposes only and should not be
used for operational use.
Overview of TWY K location
TWY K showing wing tip tracking and obstacles located
on either side of TWY K. Direction RWY to Apron
TWY K showing wing tip tracking and obstacles located
on either side of TWY K. Direction Apron to RWY
Pictorial view of TWY K showing obstacles
RJAA.departure_procedures:
Planning
• Use assigned RWY for departure due to slot restrictions. If payload/performance restricted,
local Ground Staff must be advised no later than STD – 40 minutes to permit coordination for a
possible departure RWY improvement.
• Once take-off clearance has been received, aircraft should commence take-off roll without
delay. If unable to do so, notify TWR.
RWY 16L/R
• Altitude restriction on departure to provide separation from arriving traffic.
RWY 34L/R
• Expect radar vectors followed by DCT routing.
MISCELLANEOUS

VNKT.company_policy:
• All take-offs and landings shall be performed by Captain only.
• Full approach procedures must be flown.
• VOR and DME must be operative for take-off and landing.
• Seat belts should be selected ON not later than top of descent.
• Tailwind component for take-off, for VOR and LOC approaches are limited to 10Kt.
• Full flaps landing shall be used unless required by aircraft procedure.
• Weather Deviation on Arrival or Departure:
‐ For weather avoidance on departure or arrival, coordinate the route that is intended to be
used with ATC.
‐ Weather deviations once established on approach - NOT AUTHORIZED.
• EGPWS must be operational before departure to Kathmandu.
• Stabilization criteria for all approaches is as per OM Part A.
• During Go-Around, no DIRECT TO waypoint.
VNKT.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Paragliding activity in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
4.Multiple reports of GPS interference in the vicinity of the aerodrome. Flight
Crew must monitor aircraft navigation performance. Refer to the relevant
FCOM Supplementary Procedure and FOTs.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponders may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
• Airway G598 passes through restricted area VE(R) – 75A. Contact Gorakhpur TWR before
entering on 131.0.
VNKT.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION The RWY has a pronounced hump that gives the illusion of a short RWY.
General
• Holding: If holding is required to lose altitude or for traffic, it is recommended to hold at RATAN.
RWY 02
• PAPI is unreliable beyond 2 NM from RWY threshold.
• PAPI has been reported as difficult to distinguish during hours of darkness.
• VOR approach descend angle not coincident with PAPI and steeper than normal.
RWY 20
• NIL ALS.
Preferred Approach Criteria
• Preferred approach criteria subject to weather conditions:
1.RNP AR approaches (Applicable fleets).
2.LOC RWY 02.
3.VOR RWY 02.

CAUTION First 1,000m of RWY 02 is rough and bumpy.

• Right turn from RWY 20 to TWY B - NOT AUTHORIZED.
• Potholes and rough surface have been reported in section of TWY F between TWY A and TWY
B, difficult to identify during hours of darkness or reduced visibility.
• Holding positions markings for RWY 20.
‐ Holding position marking at each TWY not depicted in the Jeppesen Charts.
Holding Position
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
VNKT.departure_procedures:
Note: De-icing service is not available (Tankering fuel can result in ice forming on the wings
due cold soak).
General
• Request the ADC and FIC numbers from Ground immediately when on-board to avoid delays.
If the communication between Ground and Kolkata is not available, Flight Crew should call
Delhi/Kolkata on HF (refer to Jeppesen for frequencies) or Contact IOC.

AUTHORIZED.
Acceleration Altitude
• Disregard “EO ACC ALT” from FSA. Use Acceleration Altitude as indicated on customized
Jeppesen Charts 10-7B and 10-7C (RWY 20 only).
Navigation accuracy
• High Navigation accuracy required before departure.
Takeoff Alternates
• Refer to 6.4.3.3.3.1.2 Dhaka (VGHS/DAC) - CATEGORY B
• Refer to 6.4.7.3.14.1.1 Kolkata (VECC/CCU) - CATEGORY A

Note: 1.In-flight announcements must refer to the new aerodrome name "Bacha Khan
International Airport" as required.
2.Peshawar has capacity restrictions and SLOTS are enforced for arrival/departure.
Comply with the scheduled arrival/departure times to avoid incurring significant fines or
penalties.
3.Non-scheduled and Cargo flights are not permitted during the period 02:00 to 09:00.
OPPS.company_policy:
• VOR A and NDB B approach – NOT AUTHORIZED.
• Full approach procedures must be flown.
OPPS.atc:
WARNING 1.Flight Crews must continuously monitor VFR traffic and be aware that not all
traffic may be shown on TCAS.
2.The aerodrome is located 20 NM to the East of the border with Afghanistan.
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military traffic operates on separate frequency.
• Military traffic normally gets priority, resulting in potential delay.
• Monday to Saturday: military activity from early morning until 14:00 Local time.
• ATC standard has been reported as poor.
Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• Excessive ATC clearance may cause high workloads.
OPPS.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.ATC may clear the aircraft to Flight Levels below transition level as they have
no information on actual QNH.
2.RWY is poorly lit.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Non-radar environment. Procedural instrument approaches are used.
• Aircraft are expected to call when each segment of approach procedure is complete, i.e. “VOR
outbound”, “Turning Inbound” .
RWY 17
• RWY can be hard to identify due to surrounding wooded area.
• When VOR approach is flown, RWY will be in sight only when aircraft is established on a 3°
approach profile on final.
RWY 35
• Preferred RWY for landing.
• Do not short cut the approach entering the inbound leg, as visual contact with RWY may be lost
due to surrounding wooded area.
• During hours of darkness, aircraft lights must be off and landing lights may be switched ON on
short finals.
• Railway track crosses RWY near threshold.
OPPS.ground_maneuvering:
RWY 17/35
• RWY 35 has a pronounced downslope from threshold towards RWY 17 threshold.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Follow-me vehicle/Marshaller available on request via TWR.
• Apron is poorly marked.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
OPPS.departure_procedures:
Refuelling with Passengers Onboard
• Report to TWR before commencing refuelling.
Startup and Pushback
• Call 5 minutes before ready to start. Startup approval valid for 5 minutes only.
• Expect ATC clearance together with startup approval.
Planning
• Take-off power can only be applied 152m (500ft) after threshold. Use appropriate #SP selection
in FSA/OPT to apply this displacement.
• Arrester gear: Performance data is available in FSA/OPT and shall be used when the arrester
gear is up.

4.Laser targeting has been reported. Inform ATC when applicable.
General
• Traffic peak time: 07:00 to 19:00 local time.
RWY Preference
• January to June: RWY 06 is generally in use.
• July to December: RWY 24 is generally in use.
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION Do not mistake Sangley Point AFB, located 5 NM to the Southwest, for Manila.
GROUND MANEUVERING
CAUTION 1.Simultaneous RWY operations may be in use. Request clearance to cross
RWY 13/31. Exercise vigilance on aircraft movements on RWY 13/31 before
crossing.
2.Hot Spot: Flight Crew should exercise vigilance at the intersection area of RWY
13/31 and RWY 06/24, due to the complex configuration of TWYs that are
poorly lit, with non-standard signages/markings.
3.Degraded braking action when RWY wet due to rubber deposits.
General
• Potholes, soft spots and uneven surface (re-carpeted potholes) have been reported on Aprons,
RWY and TWYs, especially at the following locations:
‐ Intersection of TWY G9 and TWY C5.
‐ TWY C1 leading to RWY 24. Additional thrust may be required to start taxi from H1 to RWY
24. To avoid high thrust and chances of FOD when starting to taxi for RWY 24, check with
ATC if it is possible to hold on TWY C abeam TWY M1 instead of H1.
‐ Refer to latest state NOTAMs regarding details about works in progress and status of
surfaces.
• TWY C2 at intersection with TWY E2 towards Northeast has upslope. Flight Crew to exercise
proper energy management to avoid aircraft from stopping.
RWY
• RWY 06/24 has a marked downhill slope from threshold RWY 24 towards Southwest.
TWYs
• Taxi lines are difficult to see, especially after heavy rains.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Ramp operations (Boarding, re-fueling, loading, etc.) are terminated when TS and lightning
activity are in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
‐ ATC may refer to YELLOW or RED alerts are in place (depending on the proximity of the
phenomena in relation to aerodrome area).
‐ ATC will inform when conditions are suitable for resuming ramp operations, expect delays.
• Parking stands 111, 113 and 115 have upslope towards final parking position. Flight Crew
should exercise proper energy management to prevent aircraft from stopping.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
Startup and Pushback
• Request ATC clearance 15 minutes before EOBT.
Planning
• Expect clearance to climb 7,000ft on RWY heading and radar vector to ‘SIRCA’.

CODE E/F FLEET
Apron/Parking Stands
Passenger Operations
• Parking stands on Terminal 1 Apron have upslope towards final parking position. Flight Crews
should exercise proper energy management to avoid aircraft from stopping.
• A350/B777: Expect to be parked on stands D32 or D34. Other parking stands may be offered.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, taxiway/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the
proximity of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and
request Marshaller/Wingwalker.
Cargo Operations
• Parking stand 603: A depression in the surface towards final parking position has been reported,
Flight Crew should exercise proper energy management to prevent aircraft from stopping.
WSSS.departure_procedures:
Planning
• SIDs - Climb Gradients
‐ For takeoff performance - FSA/OPT provide options to select the appropriate obstacle set for
takeoff RWY. Different ships’ height are added as Special procedures (#SPn) to FSA/OPT, of
which Flight Crew may select one.
‐ ATC shall advise the ship/vessel height. Flight Crews on receipt of this information shall
select the FSA/OPT #SPn accordingly.
‐ If the ATC reports a ship/vessel higher than height mentioned in the selected #SPn, then
takeoff should be delayed until the obstacle height is lower than height mentioned in #SPn.
• Departing aircraft are to maintain initial climb altitude as indicated on SID chart and are not to
climb to the expected cruising level given during departure clearance.
WSSS.miscellaneous:
Alcohol Testing
• CAAS (Civil Aviation Authority Singapore) and Certis Cisco shall be conducting random alcohol
testing on select Crew members, the approved tester card will be shown to the Crew member
before testing and compliance is mandatory.

VTSP.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION RWY centerline and TWYs markings are faded and difficult to see, especially
during hours of darkness or adverse weather conditions.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Expect to be cleared to MANYU waypoint.
RWY 09
• Due to lack of surrounding lighting in vicinity of the aerodrome, a “black hole” effect may be
experienced during night operations.
• VOR approaches are offset.
• NIL ALS.
• Flashing lights (located on each side of threshold) indicate RWY threshold.

• Terrain close to the approach area.
• Expect RWY 27 for arrival up to 10Kt tail wind.
VTSP.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION FOD have been reported on aprons and TWYs.
RWY 27
• Significant downhill slope.
• Nose-wheel skidding maybe induced due combination of rubber deposits and wet RWY when
conducting left hand turn from TWY G onto RWY 27.
• Turn pad: the turning taxi-line is not adequate to follow. Judgmental steering is required.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
follow-me or Marshaller/Wingwalker.
APU Usage
• APU must be shutdown during turnaround.
• GPU and PCA (Pre–Conditioned Air) is provided upon arrival.
• It is permitted to operate APU for 5 minutes after arrival on stand.
• APU start: Permitted 10 minutes prior to pushback.
• If ‘PWR U/S’ sign is positioned at parking stand, APU start is permitted or mobile service
available (15 minutes notification required).
• APU is permitted on remote parking stands.
VTSP.departure_procedures:
Startup and Pushback
• Contact “Phuket Clearance” 5 minutes prior to startup for ATC clearance.
• Contact “Ground Control” for pushback and start-up, after ATC clearance has been received.

Crew must familiarize themselves with the Standard Taxi Routes, TWY
Restrictions and Pushback Procedures published in the Jeppesen Airport charts
before operating to Istanbul (LTFM/IST).
General
• Exercise vigilance while taxiing due to workers, equipment and vehicles crossing.
TWYs
• Numerous TWYs have upslope/downslope, Flight Crew should exercise proper energy
management during LMT-RETI application.
Apron/Parking Stands

“L” parking position and one shared by the Main and the right “R” positions, this is located offset
to the centreline of the main parking stand. Flight Crews are to still able to follow the guidance
after ensuring that the displayed information is applicable to the type of aircraft, if in doubt stop
the aircraft and request for assistance.

Visual Docking Guidance System (VDGS)

approach.

without further instructions. Clearance to land is given after Flight Crews have reported Landing
Gear down.

EBBR.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation and and Military operations at the
aerodrome.
3.Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (Drones) activity in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
4.Low transition altitude.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponder may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
EBBR.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION STARs and Approach procedures have strict speed limitations that shall be
maintained. If unable to comply, inform ATC on initial contact.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Do not accept visual approach clearance until landing RWY is correctly identified.
RWY 07L
• VOR approach is offset.
RWY 07R
• NIL ALS.
EBBR.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION RWY 19 and RWY 25R share a common area near the threshold. Ensure that you
are lined up on the correct RWY before commencing the takeoff.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Ramp operations (i.e. boarding, re-fueling, loading) are suspended when lightning is in the
vicinity of the aerodrome. Refer to ATIS for updates.
EBBR.departure_procedures:
CAUTION Strict adherence to SID routing is required, any deviation from SIDs tracks without
ATC consent may lead to significant fines and penalties. Flight Crews shall ensure
that the assigned/requested SID is appropriate for the planned/actual departure
time.
Planning
• RWY 07R: Full RWY length is NOT AVAILABLE due to jet blast requirements. Take-off
positions are depicted in the Aerodrome Layout (i.e. LINE-UP POSN H ).
Noise Abatement Procedures
• Brussels is surrounded by noise sensitive areas with automated noise measuring equipment
used to monitor noise abatement procedures adherence.
• All Brussels SIDs are noise abatement compliant and have closely monitored lateral and
vertical paths, any deviation will result in an investigation and/or find from the Brussels airport
authorities. Strict adherence is mandatory.
• Flight Crews shall observe High Climb gradients and speed/altitude restrictions in turns.
• Special procedures for specific RWYs during night time are detailed in the Jeppesen Airport
Briefing pages.
EBBR.miscellaneous:
Entry Requirements
• All Crew Members must carry and present the Crew Member Certificate (CMC) and the valid
passport on arrival.
