LQSA.name: Sarajevo
LQSA.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Sarajevo
Ground Handling: TBA
Terminal: TBA
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
7 Refer NOTAMs YES NO YES NIL YES
Note: 1.RFF CAT 8 and 9 available O/R with 24 hours PN.
2.Delayed flight outside of the operating hours requires a valid slot from Aerodrome
Authority. Request must be submitted before 17:00 LT.
3.Sarajevo has capacity restrictions and SLOTS are enforced for arrival/departure. Flight
Crew are reminded to comply with the scheduled arrival/departure times to avoid
incurring significant fines or penalties.
LQSA.company_policy:
• All take-offs and landings shall be performed by Captain or Captain under training only.
• Operating Captain and First Officer shall have minimum six (6) months experience on
type/variant (after FLC/Command Release Check, as applicable).
• EGPWS must be operative prior departure to Sarajevo.
• Landing on RWY 29 – NOT AUTHORIZED.
• RWY 11: Take-off – NOT AUTHORIZED.
• Intersection take-offs – NOT AUTHORIZED.
• Sarajevo aerodrome can be planned as Destination only.
LQSA.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
General
• Peak Periods: From 12:00 to 15:00 (11:00 to 14:00).
LQSA.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Turbulence and strong crosswinds may be encountered on final approach.
2.RWY centerline and TWYs markings are faded and difficult to see, especially
during hours of darkness or adverse weather conditions.
General
• Wind direction and speed maybe different during the approach than reported by ATC on the
RWY.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• STARs published, but radar vectors are common practice.
• Delays for descent may be expected due to traffic.
• Flight Crew need to confirm the approach procedure required on first contact with Sarajevo
Approach.
RWY 11 Landing
LQSA.ground_maneuvering:
General
• Winter Operations: RWY/TWYs/Apron marking may not be visible due snow/ice.
RWY 11
• Exercise caution when required to perform 180° turn after landing on RWY 11 to prevent
damage of RWY surface. When instructed to make a 180° turn, crew shall use the TURN PAD
and leave RWY via TWY B.
• Below graphic for reference only.
Apron/Parking Stands
• “Follow me” vehicle is compulsory after vacating the RWY to the final parking stand.
• Parking stands 3 and 4 are equipped with aerobridge and Visual Guidance System.
• Aircraft is guided by Marshaller to parking stand.
LQSA.departure_procedures:
Refueling with Pax On-board
• Local regulations state that Flight Crew must ensure communication with the ground engineer is
established via headset at all times.
• Fire truck must be present at the aircraft.
Startup and Pushback
• Contact Tower 5 minutes before departure to coordinate the clearance.
• ATC clearance and transponder code is provided during Taxi.
De-icing
• De-icing is conducted on apron before pushback with engines OFF.
SIDs
• All SIDs are minimum noise routings, which must be flown as accurate as possible.
• High terrain located on extended C/L, resulting in initial turns at low altitude.

LFSB.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted and uncontrolled airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Extensive light aircraft activity controlled in French on grass strip 200m West of

4.Low transition altitude.
General
• The aerodrome is in France but is jointly administered by French and Swiss.
Radar Vectoring
• Flight Crews are requested to operate within controlled airspace as established in OM Part A
– Navigation Procedures. In the event that ATC offers to vector the aircraft outside controlled
airspace, do not accept such request.
LFSB.arrival_procedures:
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Ensure all descent clearance provide adequate terrain clearance.
RWY15
• ATC may vector the aircraft East of the aerodrome before turning North.
• ATC may use the phase “expect left vector to RWY15” to indicate that a left-hand pattern is
being used.
• Track miles may be less than expected as a very short left-hand downwind or practically a
continuous left turn on to intercept heading may be given.
• Final turn to intercept LLZ often occurs D10 BLM.
RWY 33
• ATC can vector the aircraft to the South for long finals.
• NIL ALS.
• RWY difficult to see at night.
LFSB.ground_maneuvering:
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
LFSB.departure_procedures:
DCL Procedures
• Due ATC system limitations, any change in DCL may not be updated in the local ATC at the
aerodrome of origin, even if it is acknowledged by EUROCONTROL. If any difference is noticed
within the clearance and the planned route, Flight Crews are required to contact ATC before
departure for correct processing and synchronization.
Planning
• All SIDs are minimum noise routings, which must be flown as accurate as possible. Minimum
crossing altitudes must be observed.
• Do not request direct route immediately after departure.
• Due surrounding terrain and SIDs climb gradients, careful planning is required in case of engine
failure during climb out and should be briefed during preflight briefing.

3.This aerodrome brief in conjunction with the Jeppesen Airport Briefing pages provide
detailed operational information based on AIP and DGAC website extracts. This fulfills
the requirements stated in the Jeppesen Airport Briefing pages 10–1P series.
LFMN.company_policy:
• All landings shall be performed by Captains or Captain under training only.
• Operating Captain shall have minimum six (6) months experience on type/variant (after
FLC/Command Release Check).
LFMN.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.

vicinity of the aerodrome.
4.Low transition altitude.
LFMN.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel RWY and
wide parallel TWY U for landing RWY.
A320/A330/A350/B787: Guided Visual Approaches (GVA)
• GVA approaches are available for all RWYs, and recommended when weather permits.
• When cleared by ATC for the RNP followed by the VPT approach, Flight Crew must select, from
the FMC/FMS, the applicable GVA for that RWY.
• The FMC/FMS selected GVA will capture all waypoints included in the RNAV initial approach,
RNP and VPT charts for that RWY.
• Refer to Jeppesen Chart 19-XX series.
RWY 04L/R
• PAPI must be operative for night operation.
RWY 22L/R
• Do not extend down wind due to terrain.
• RWY 22R, sequence flashing lights (300m length).
• Final Approach course is offset, PAPIs are visible once aligned with the RWY.
LFMN.ground_maneuvering:
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop the aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
LFMN.departure_procedures:
DCL Procedures
• Due ATC system limitations, any change in DCL may not be updated in the local ATC at the
aerodrome of origin, even if it is acknowledged by EUROCONTROL. If any difference is noticed
within the clearance and the planned route, Flight Crews are required to contact ATC before
departure for correct processing and synchronization.
Holding Points
• When ILS 04L is in use, expect taxi instructions via holding point C1 for RWY 04L.
SIDs
• Adherence to SIDs is mandatory.
• Do not request direct route immediately after departure.
• High terrain located on extended C/L.
• If RWY 22L/R is expected for departure, expect specific altitude constraint to provide separation
between departure/arrival traffic. Strictly follow initial flight paths and published altitudes.

LFPG.name: Charles-De-Gaulle
LFPG.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Charles-De-Gaulle.
Ground Handling:
• PAX: CDG PCA – 131.480
• Cargo: WORLDWIDE FLIGHT SERVICES (WFS) – 131.650
Terminal: 1
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI

10 H24 NIL YES YES NIL YES
Note: QR does not hold night time slots/quota in Paris Charles De Gaulle for scheduled flights.
Therefore, landing before 04:30 (03:30) is NOT AUTHORIZED.
LFPG.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Be aware of traffic around Le Bourget (LFPB/LBG) airport which lies about 5
NM West of Charles De Gaulle.
3.Helicopter, VFR and General aviation operations at the aerodrome.
4.Low transition altitude.
LFPG.arrival_procedures:
General
• Expect reduced track miles and delay in descent clearance.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• RNAV STAR procedures which include the words “BY ATC”, Flight Crew should prepare in
advance as these STARs maybe assigned by ATC at the last minute.
• Do not accept visual approach clearance until landing RWY is correctly identified.
Local Procedure
• When handed over from Paris ACC to De Gaulle approach, on first contact, report to ATC the
latest speed instructed by the preceding sector (Paris ACC).
LFPG.ground_maneuvering:
RWYs
• RWY Status Lights (RWSL). Refer to Jeppesen charts 20-9 series for details about the RWSL
installed.
TWYs
• Exercise vigilance while taxiing as TWY ground markings differ from TWY designators used in
ATC clearances. (i.e. Cleared to BD10, ground marking is TU5).
• After a routing error DO NOT try to catch up at the next turn without an ATC clearance.
• Never take a turn when there is no marking/lighting on the ground.
• Respect the NO RIGHT/LEFT TURN signs.
• Refer to the TWY Turn Restrictions published in Jeppesen charts 20-9 series.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Parking stands in Terminal 2 can also be allocated (Terminals 2C and 2E parking stands):
‐ Parking stand C14: Markings have been reported as difficult to identify during hours of
darkness and when surface is wet.
‐ Parking stand E22: A significant depression has been reported towards the final parking
position. Flight Crews should exercise proper energy management.
• Parking stand K71 in Terminal S3 has been reported as having upslope. Flight Crews should
exercise proper energy management.
LFPG.departure_procedures:
De-icing
• Iceman may use aircraft registration instead of call-sign.
DCL Procedures
• Due ATC system limitations, any change in DCL may not be updated in the local ATC at the
aerodrome of origin, even if it is acknowledged by EUROCONTROL. If any difference is noticed
within the clearance and the planned route, Flight Crews are required to contact ATC before
departure for correct processing and synchronization.
Speed Restrictions - Departure
• ATC will by controller initiative only, request speed increase above 250 KIAS below FL100,
provided that:
‐ The aircraft will be cleared of noise abatement procedures (passing FL060 typically).
‐ This ATC speed instruction will comply with any possible speed restriction in force on the
allocated SID (example: the restriction “280 KIAS max” will be kept on southbound SID).
• Flight Crew must not increase speed under his own discretion to a speed higher than 250 KIAS
below FL100.
Local Procedure
• Departure frequency is not given by the tower after take-off. Flight Crew must prepare it before
take-off and wait for frequency change instruction issued by tower.
LFPG.miscellaneous:
Cargo Operations
Access to the aircraft for Cargo Flights will be thru RAMP ACCESS.

LGMK.name: Mikonos
LGMK.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Mikonos
Ground Handling: SWISSPORT
Terminal: Main Terminal
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
7 Refer to Note YES NO Day time only NIL NO
WARNING RWY 16/34 is 30m wide. Before departure Flight Crew to ensure allocated
aircraft is authorized for narrow RWY operations. Refer to FCOM
‘PRO-SPO-60-Operations on Narrow RWY’.
CAUTION 1.Careful performance calculation for take-off and landing is mandatory.
2.Landings to Mikonos out of abnormal situations shall only be considered in
case of time limiting scenarios (example: inextinguishable fire, dual engine
stall/damage etc.)
Note: 1.Mikonos has capacity restrictions and slots are enforced during the Summer Season
due to limited parking.
2.Operating hours for scheduled operations, refer to NOTAM for timings. For
non-scheduled operations, 24 hours PNR.
3.Fueling services:
‐ Summer: Available during hours of scheduled operations.
‐ Winter: Available during hours of scheduled operations with prior notice.
LGMK.company_policy: • All landings and take-offs shall be performed by Captains or Captains under training only.
LGMK.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Low transition altitude.
General
• Procedural separation with the use of DME and radials to separate arrivals and departing traffic.
LGMK.arrival_procedures:
WARNING 1.Due to lack of PAPI on RWY 16 in combination with visual illusion created
by the 30m width of the RWY and the possible wind changes, Flight Crew
carefully, fly the final stages of the approach and cross the threshold of RWY
16 at 50ft. If this is not possible, a Go-Around is mandatory.
2.Touchdown zone is 1,950ft, defined by 4 touch down zone markings. Due to
the reduced RWY length, touch down must be performed strictly within the
touch down zone. If in doubt or if this cannot be achieved, a Go-Around is
mandatory.
CAUTION 1.Turbulence and strong crosswinds are common and downdraught associated
with strong Westerly winds can be experienced during approach when the
aircraft crosses RWY 34 threshold and departure.
2.Rapid change in wind direction, up to 30° may occur.
3.Gusty surface winds up to 40kt are common.
4.The uneven terrain approaching RWY 34 leads to a rapid decrease in radio
altimeter height crossing the threshold and the narrow RWY can present
numerous illusions. The final stages of the approach are to be carefully flown
and flare anticipated.
5.TCAS alert may occur due transponder equipped ships located at Mikonos
harbor, 1 NM West of the aerodrome.
General
• It is often difficult to get the appropriate descent from Athens, resulting in a descent in the
Mikonos hold.
• Expect clearance below 5,000ft when overhead MKN VOR.
• Expect approach via position PIDAX for RNP RWY 34 or VOR Y RWY 34. Expect late descent
from 6,000ft by ATC.
• If arrival is during peak time, expect holding over the VOR followed by full procedure for RNP
RWY 34 or VOR Z RWY 34.
• Holding time:
‐ RWY 34 in use: 10 minutes.
‐ RWY 16 in use: 20 minutes.
RWY 16
• Approach is challenging during turbulent conditions when strong Southerly winds.
• RWY width is 30m and can create an illusion of being high during the approach landing flare.
• NIL ALS and PAPI.
• Guided visual approach procedures published.
• Obstacles are not lighted during the night.
• Due to terrain and visual illusions the approach needs to be performed accurately.
• During the final turn wind direction and speed must be continuously monitored.
Final approach to RWY 16
RWY 34
• RWY width is 30m and can create an illusion of being high during the approach landing flare.
• NIL ALS.
• VOR and LCTR Z approaches are offset.
• Guided visual approach procedure published.
Final approach to RWY 34
LGMK.ground_maneuvering:
RWY
• There is pronounced hump on RWY 34 at touchdown point.
• Backtrack is required after landing and for take-off at both RWY ends.
RWY 34 - Steep drop offs at the end of the RWY
TWYs
• Old TWY markings are still visible.
• Judgemental oversteering required.
• TWYs have upslope from RWY toward the Apron. Exercise proper energy management.
Apron/Parking Stands
• The parking stands have two stop bars numbered 1 and 2. Number 2 is for A320.
Parking Stands – Stop Bars
• Apron is small and usually congested, uneven surface in certain areas.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop the aircraft and request
marshaller/wingwalker.
• Initial turning to the parking stands is under Flight Crew discretion, then the aircraft is guided by
Marshaller to the final position.
• Confirm the current wind does not exceed limitation for operation before cargo compartments
and passenger doors are opened.
LGMK.departure_procedures:
Start-up and Pushback
• Due to short taxi from the apron to the RWY and ramp congestion, the Passenger Safety
Demonstration must be conducted before requesting pushback clearance.
• Request clearance for any maneuver only when the aircraft is ready to comply with the
clearance without delay.
• ATC clearance is usually given before pushback.
RWY 34
• Expect either PIDAX SID or a visual departure with a right turn to position PIDAX.

LGSR.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Santorini
Ground Handling: SWISSPORT - NIL
Terminal: TBA
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
7 Refer to Note YES YES Day time only NIL NO
WARNING RWY 15/33 is 30m wide. Before departure Flight Crew to ensure allocated
aircraft is authorized for narrow RWY operations. Refer to FCOM
‘PRO-SPO-60-Operations on Narrow RWY’.
CAUTION Wildlife and birds in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
Note: 1.Santorini has capacity restrictions and SLOTS are enforced for arrival/departure. Flight
Crews are reminded to comply with the scheduled arrival/departure times to avoid
incurring significant fines or penalties.
2.Operating hours for scheduled operations, refer to NOTAM for timings.
LGSR.company_policy:
• Take-off and landing shall be performed by Captains or Captains under training only.
• VOR B Approach - NOT AUTHORIZED.
LGSR.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Do not deviate west of the RWY extended centerline due to rapidly rising
terrain.
3.Santorini has a busy TMA with high volume of VFR traffic.
4.Helicopters, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
5.Low transition altitude.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponder may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
• Procedural separation with the use of DME and radials to separate arrivals and departing traffic.
LGSR.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Turbulence and strong crosswinds are common. Downdraught associated with
strong westerly winds can be experienced during approach.
2.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWY A
(former RWY) for landing RWY.
General
• Preferred RWY is RWY 33:
‐ VOR A With circling minima is authorized.
• If RWY 15 is in use:
‐ ATC preferred approach is VOR RWY 15.
‐ If RNP RWY 15 is conducted, plan STAR BINKI1D.
• In VMC conditions, ATC preferred approach is visual approach.
• Do not accept visual approach until landing RWY is correctly identified.
Wind Limitations
• When reported wind direction is between 180º and 300º, the maximum allowed wind intensity is
20Kt including gust.
• Exercise vigilance when the reported gust factor is between 5Kt and 10Kt.
• If the TAF indicates that the wind intensity will exceed 20Kt, flight must dispatched with FOB to
cover two destination alternates in the OFP.
• The Flight Crew must confirm that the wind values remains within the limitations at the following
stages:
‐ At initial contact with Santorini TWR.
‐ At the initial Approach Fix.
‐ At 1,000ft AAL.
‐ At 500ft AAL.
RWY 15
• VOR approach descent angle is not coincident with PAPI.
• NDB approach is offset.
• NIL ALS.
RWY 33
• VOR A approach is offset.
• Guided visual approach procedure available.
LGSR.ground_maneuvering:
Note: Arrester cables installed on the RWY.
TWYs
• Judgmental steering required.
• TWYs have upslope from RWY toward the Apron.
• Military Area West of TWY A (between TWY F and TWY G) is not depicted in JEPPESEN Chart
Apron/Parking Stands
• Apron is small and usually congested, uneven surface in certain areas.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• Initial turn to the parking stands is under Flight Crew discretion, thereafter the aircraft is guided
by Marshaller to the final position.
• Confirm the current wind does not exceed limitation for operation before cargo compartments
and passenger doors are opened.
LGSR.departure_procedures:
Preferred RWY
• Preferred RWY is RWY 33.
Start-up and Push back
• Request clearance only when the aircraft is ready to comply with the clearance without delay.
• ATC clearance is usually given before push-back.

LGTS.name: Makedonia
LGTS.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Makedonia
Ground Handling: GROUND HANDLING – 118.675
Terminal: N/A
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
8 H24 YES NO Day time only YES YES
WARNING Strong bird activity has been reported. If doubt exists, Flight Crew should request
engineering assistance for bird strike inspection.
Note: Operations with wide-body aircraft require prior approval from the Airport Authorities.
LGTS.company_policy:
• All take-offs and landings shall be performed by Captains or Captains under training only.
• Operating captain shall have minimum (6) months experience on type/variant (after
FLC/Command Release Check).
• EGPWS and reverse thrust on all engines must be operative before departure from Doha.
• Due to landing distance or brake cooling time calculation, if operationally required, the
Commander shall consider the use of full reversers upon landing. Special consideration shall be
given to RWY conditions other than DRY.
• RWY 16 and RWY 10 should be used for landing up to the maximum tailwind component
specified in the aircraft FCOM. Subject to landing performance.
• RWY 34 approach: Aircraft must be fully configured at final approach speed by FAF.
• Training flights – NOT AUTHORIZED.
LGTS.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter and General operations at the aerodrome.
3.Due high terrain to the East and Northeast of the aerodrome. Exercise vigilance
when overflying/vectored over those sectors and always observe the minimum
radar vectoring altitudes.
4.Low transition altitude.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponder may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• ATIS transmission can be received within 60 NM of the aerodrome. Weather and the RWY in
use can be obtained in advance from Athens control.
RWY Preference
• Expect RWY 34 for take-off and landing.
• If RWY 16 is requested for takeoff, expect delay due to inbound traffic.
LGTS.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Do not mistake Sedes (LGSD) Military aerodrome located 2 NM to the
Northeast for Thessaloniki (LGTS/SKG).
2.Higher than normal rate of descent may be expected due to the PAPI angle.
Extra vigilance on tailwind is required. Normal stabilization criteria applies.
3.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWY F
(former RWY with visible marking) for RWY.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Expect radar vectors.
RWY 28
• PAPI and VOR approach descent angle steeper than normal.
RWY 34
• VOR Y, VOR Z and RNP Y approach descent angle is not coincident with PAPI.
• PAPI brightness has been reported as dim.
• PAPI and RNP Z approach descent angle is steeper than normal.
LGTS.ground_maneuvering:
TWYs
• TWYs A3, N and R – NOT AUTHORIZED.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Marshallers are mandatory on entering the apron for guidance to the parking stand.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWYs/apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due to the
proximity of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and
request marshaller/wingwalker.
• Apron is generally congested with vehicles, equipment and staff.
• Slow taxi to the final parking position is required, Marshaller may request to stop suddenly.
LGTS.departure_procedures:
Refuelling with Pax on-board
• According to Local Regulations, Flight Crews must ensure communication with the ground
engineer is established via headset at all times.
• Fire truck must be present at the aircraft.
Planning
• Intersection take-offs are permitted during daytime only and if visibility is above 5,000m.

LHBP.departure_procedures:
Startup and Pushback
• En-route Clearance and CTOT Related Procedures
‐ Contact DELIVERY or GROUND (as indicated in ATIS) 20 minutes prior EOBT or CTOT
(whatever is the latest), providing call sign, aircraft type, destination and stand number.
‐ En Route clearance will be issued with clearance limit, SID or departure route, cleared
altitude and allocated squawk.
‐ If slot is expired (aircraft is not ready for start-up at 10 minutes prior to CTOT) the ATC will not
issue start-up clearance and a new slot is required, coordinate with QR staff.

maneuvering.

correct SID has been selected.

• After landing on RWY 04R and vacating via TWY D, expect to back track on RWY 04L/22R to
enter the apron via TWY F.
• If RWY 04L/22R is used, approach delay may be expected due to traffic flow.
RWY 04L/R
• VOR approaches are offset.
• RWY 04R: ILS may have fluctuations.
RWY 22L/R
WARNING Flight Crew to exercise extra vigilance to not exceed the time on the Downwind
Leg due to mountainous terrain northeast the RWY (as per figure below).
Downwind Leg time is the primary reference for turning base. D4.2 PIS ARC may
be used as reference for obstacle clearance.
• On the instrument approach segment, the aircraft shall not descend below 1,000ft AGL or
the charted circling minima rounded up to the nearest 100ft, whichever is higher. For circling
approach for RWY 22L/R, the MDA (H) should be 1100’ (1000’).
‐ The Downwind Leg is flown for a time period from abeam the threshold defined by MDH on
basis of 3s per 100ft. i.e.: MDH = 1000 ft, time is (3x10) 30s.
Simulation of circle to land in RWY 22L
• RWY is difficult to see through the smog from the city, particularly when landing in the afternoon
towards the Sun.
• RWY 22R: NIL IAP and PAPI angle is steeper than normal.
Local procedure
• RWY 04L/R: If the tail wind component exceeds 10Kt, ATC will assign RWY 22L/R. However,
can request to land on RWY 04L/R up to the maximum tail wind component authorized in the
FCOM. Advise ATC in advance to minimize delays.
• Report to TWR when the aircraft vacate the TWY F or pass the holding point E2, to enter to the
Civil Apron.
LIRP.ground_maneuvering:
RWY
• Broken lines of the RWY edge at the displaced threshold may be confused with the RWY center
line.
Apron/Parking Stands
• The apron is not controlled by ATC.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• Taxi slowly to the parking stand is required since Marshaller may request to stop suddenly.
LIRP.departure_procedures:
General
• Communication with ground staff has been reported as difficult due different English standards.
Use standard and simple phraseology, speak slowly and clearly to avoid any confusion.
Refuelling with Pax Onboard
• Local regulation state that communication with the ground engineer is established via headset
at all times.
• Verify radio contact between cockpit and control TWR is maintained for possible evacuation.
• Verify brakes “ON” during refuelling.
• The presence of the fire-truck is not required.
Startup and Pushback
• Call Tower 10 minutes before start up.
• ATC clearance is usually given during taxi-out.
• Pushback is coordinated by the ground staff.
• Unless pushback has been initiated, arriving aircraft will have priority to enter to apron area.
• Transponder ON prior pushback.
• It is important to monitoring Pisa TWR frequency prior to, and during pushback.
• Departing aircraft are to contact Pisa TWR on the appropriate frequency at holing point E2 or
TWY F.
Planning
CAUTION When taxiing to take off from RWY 22L at TWY D, there is a RWY length sign
with an arrow to the left (towards the beginning of RWY 22L) that indicates the
available RWY distance if the aircraft perform a backtrack at the turn pad.
• Late notification of departure RWY change to RWY 04R may occur due inbound traffic.
• Adherence to SIDs/vectors is required.
• Do not request direct route immediately after departure.

LIRF.name: Fiumicino
LIRF.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Fiumicino
Ground Handling: AVIAPARTNER FIUMICINO - 131.465
Terminal: 3
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
10 H24 YES YES YES NIL YES
CAUTION When orographic clouds are seen over the high terrain in the east, severe low
altitude turbulence may be experienced below FL200. Include this threat in
the joined briefing and advise the Cabin Crew to secure the cabin as early as
possible.
Note: 1.Rome-Fiumicino has capacity restrictions and SLOTS are enforced for
arrival/departures. Comply with the scheduled arrival/departure times to avoid incurring
significant fines or penalties.
2.RWY 16L/34R closed to all operations every Monday between 01:00 and 03:00 (00:00
and 02:00) for ILS ground check.
LIRF.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.RWY 34L/R: ATC may request to descend to 1,500ft on a radar vector just
outside the Glide Slope (GS) intercept point.
4.Low transition altitude.
LIRF.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWYs for
landing RWY.
2.Turbulence and strong crosswinds may be encountered on final approach,
especially between March and November when wind is from Northeast and
above 15Kt on clear sky days or when wind is from Southeast/Southwest in
cloudy days or when TS occur.
3.After heavy rain showers, areas of standing water may exist, particularly on
RWY 16L. Request to tower for the latest surface conditions and braking
actions when applicable to avoid hydroplaning incidents.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Expect RNAV Arrivals.
• Whenever possible, request landing on RWY 16R or RWY 34L to reduce the taxi time.
• ATC vectors to RWY 16L/R approaches may leave the aircraft above the glide slope.
• Monitor speed and configuration. Avoid a rushed approach.
• RWY 16L/R: After crossing ROM, expect to either be vectored to RWY 16L/16R, or cleared
ROM direct CMP and then vectors prior to CMP.
RWY 16L/34R
• After landing ATC may give taxi instructions to use RWY 25 instead of TWY B. Taxiing on RWY
07/25 is only permitted when closed for take-off and landing.
RWY 16R
• Aircraft Landing on RWY 16R may see traffic taking off from RWY 25 and crossing through the
centre line of RWY 16R. Expect late landing clearance.
RWY 07/25
• VOR Approaches are offset.
LIRF.ground_maneuvering:
RWY 07
• Broken lines of the RWY edge at the displaced threshold may be confused with the RWY.
LIRF.departure_procedures:
Refuelling with Pax Onboard
• Local regulation state that communication with the ground engineer is established via headset
at all times.
• Verify radio contact between cockpit and control TWR is maintained for possible evacuation.
• Verify brakes ON during refuelling.
• The presence of the fire-truck is not required.
Startup and Push-back
• When APU is inoperative and/or engine start is required at gate, ‘Follow me’ car must be
requested with pushback clearance.
Planning
• The SID designator in the OFP belongs to the final point of the SID and is not the same that
appears on the FMS as first entry of the departure procedure. To check the SID procedure
between FMS and OFP, please refer to Jeppesen charts 10-3B up to 10-3S for initial climb
procedure and then from 10-3T up to 10-3X3 For SID continuation/transition.

EHBK.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION An Uphill hump is present at RWY 21 TDZ and may not be visible /recognizable
to the crew from the approach end. Flight crew are to apply appropriate flare
techniques to avoid the potential of a hard landing.
RWY 03/21
• RWY 03/21 touchdown zone markings are consistent with that of a RWY with a total length less
than 2,400m. The markings consist of 4 pairs of touchdown zone markings that end at 750m.
Therefore the markings may give the illusion of a long landing.
RWY 21
• Unusual surface profile at the beginning of the RWY (hump), initially upslope followed by a
marked downslope towards the middle, may cause a misleading height perception, especially at
night.
• The location of the upslope at the touchdown zone on RWY 21 significantly increases the
chances of a hard landing.
• Flight crew are reminded to cover this threat and mitigation actions during their arrival briefing.
Below pictorial is for reference only.
RWY profile
RWY 03 Touchdown Zone Markings
RWY 21 Touchdown Zone Markings

Marshaller/Wingwalker.

LWSK.company_policy:
• All take-offs and landings shall be performed by Captains or a Captain under training only.
• Operating Captain shall have minimum six (6) months experience on type/variant (after
FLC/Command Release Check)
• EGPWS must be operative to dispatch from Doha.
• Skopje aerodrome can only be planned as Destination.
LWSK.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Glider and parachute activity is conducted during Summer from a grass airfield
located 4 NM West of Skopje aerodrome, which is not indicated on Jeppesen
Charts.
3.Helicopter operations (civilian and military) are conducted during the day.
4.TCAS alert may occur due transponder equipped tower located 7.5 NM
Southeast of the aerodrome.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponder may not be compatible with civil aircraft TCAS.
LWSK.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION Turbulence and strong crosswinds may be encountered on final approach.
General
• The wind direction and speed changes due to the terrain, generating tail wind in different
segments of approach.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Conventional STARs are published, but radar vector to 12 NM final is used by ATC which may
result in the aircraft being left high in relation to remaining track miles.
RWY 16
• Nil PAPI and IAP.
• As per Flight Crew request, ATC may provide vectors to circling to land to RWY 16.
• If weather conditions permit, circling to land to the West of the aerodrome is recommended, due
to terrain and manmade obstacles located to the East of the aerodrome.
RWY 34
• After landing on RWY 34, ATC expects the aircraft to vacate the RWY via TWY B.
Local Procedure
• Prior to landing, Flight Crew must ensure receipt of the assigned parking stand as Skopje tower
will not be aware of parking stand.
LWSK.ground_maneuvering:
TWYs
• Parallel TWY to TWY A (between TWY G and TWY H) and parallel TWY (between TWY B and
RWY 16 THR) not depicted in Jeppesen chart - NOT AUTHORIZED.
• TWY A5 is NOT AUTHORIZED if parking stand 201 is occupied by Code E aircraft.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Parking stands 15 and 16 – NOT AUTHORIZED.
• No docking system available, aircraft is guided by marshallling.
• Stop bars are not painted for any aircraft type.
LWSK.departure_procedures:
Refuelling with Pax On-board
• Local regulation state that Flight Crew must ensure communication with the ground engineer is
established via headset at all times.
• Fire truck must be present at the aircraft.
Start-up and Pushback
• Request clearance when ready for departure, transponder code is usually given during taxi-out.
Planning
• If RWY 16 is required advise ATC on first contact. Delay up to 10 minutes can be expected.
• Normal clearance is departure following the published SIDs until reaching 4,200ft, then radar
vectors are provided.
• RWY 34:
‐ Only RUGAS 2D and PEP 2E SIDs are AUTHORIZED.
‐ RAXAD 2D, SIN 2D and GOSTI 2D SIDs are NOT AUTHORIZED due to high climb gradient
requirements.

ENEV.company_policy:

FLC/Command Release Check).

given to RWY conditions other than DRY.

to Jeppesen chart 10-9A/).
ENEV.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome .
3.Low transition altitude.
General

• Joint civil and military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponder may not be compatible with civil aircraft TCAS.
ENEV.arrival_procedures:
WARNING Do not commence the approach if the reported winds (including gusts) at 1,400ft
is exceeding 40Kt from any direction.
CAUTION 1.Moderate, occasionally severe turbulence may occur on final RWY 17 with wind
SW-W 20Kt or more.
2.Severe turbulence may occur on final RWY 17 when the wind at 1,400ft is
reported above 40Kt.
3.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWY for
landing RWY.
General
• Ensure all descent clearance provide adequate terrain clearance.
• Due to lack of surrounding lighting in vicinity of the aerodrome, a “black hole” effect may be
experienced during night operations.
• The wind direction and speed changes (maybe different than reported by ATC on the RWY) due
to the terrain, generating tail wind in different segments of approach.
RWY 17/35
• Aircraft must be fully configured no later than 3 NM from the FAF.
• All approaches descent angle not coincident with PAPI and steeper than normal.
ENEV.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION In freezing temperature, the excessive humidity caused by the nearby water
masses may cover the ground surfaces with black ice, making the RWY slippery
with degraded braking action. Monitor the reported weather and exercise vigilance
in all ground maneuverings.
TWYs
• TWYs A4, C1, M1, M2, M3, M4 and M5 – NOT AUTHORIZED.
• TWY A3 has significant upslope towards the apron.
• Follow me vehicle provided for all taxiing.
• Judgmental oversteering required. Flight Crews are reminded to utilize on-board cameras, if
available, to assist during ground maneuvering.
Judgmental oversteering on TWYs
Apron/Parking stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• The stand taxi lane has no guiding lights.
• Arrival parking procedure:
‐ Entry into stand is via TWY Y and adjacent parking stands 26 and 27 must be empty.
‐ The aircraft will be parked with nose facing North.
‐ Flight Crew shall follow the Marshaller instruction for the parking position.
• Expect to be parked on temporary stand located between stands 26B and 27.
Parking stand
ENEV.departure_procedures:
WARNING Operations are NOT AUTHORIZED if the wind at 1,400ft exceeds 60Kt or gusts
above 65Kt from any direction.
Planning
• 5Kt tailwind may be less restrictive for RWY 17 than a departure with head wind on RWY 35.
Taxi-out procedure
• Aircraft to self-maneuver onto TWY Y.
• Parking stands 24 and 25 must be empty.
• Wing Walkers are required during taxi-out.
• If RWY 35 is in use, expected taxi route:
‐ TWY Y (facing North) - TWY A2 – RWY 17/35 – TWY A3 – continue via TWY Y to holding
point of RWY 35.
De-icing/Anti-icing
• To access the de-icing area, expected taxi route:
‐ TWY Y (facing North) - TWY A2 – RWY 17/35 – TWY A3 to enter the de-icing area.
‐ Below pictorial for reference only.
Expected taxi route to access the de-icing area.

Note: Moscow Sheremetyevo has capacity restrictions and slots are enforced for
arrival/departure. Flight Crews are reminded to comply with the scheduled
arrival/departure times to avoid incurring significant fines or penalties.
UUEE.company_policy:
• A330 – Fuel remaining in outer tanks should be transferred to inner tanks to avoid unnecessary
deicing from upper side of wings. Refer to FCOM PRO-SUP-91.
UUEE.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Busy aerodrome and ATC environment due to several nearby aerodromes.
4.QR has received multiple reports of GPS interference and spurious EGPWS
alerts in the vicinity of the aerodrome. Flight Crew must carefully monitor
aircraft navigation and system performance during critical flight phases. Flight
Crew shall refer to the FCOM supplementary procedure following any GPS
outage.
Note: FMS default speed limit is 270Kt below 10,000ft. Flight Crews are required to modify as
necessary to comply with Company Policy.
Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• Expect several frequencies changes from top of descent until landing.
UUEE.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel RWY and
TWYs for landing RWY.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• STARs published but expect radar vector to final approach.
• Entering Moscow FIR, upon request ATC will advise STAR and RWY in use.
• Timely descent should be requested on entering Moscow FIR as short cut routing and ATC
instructions to maintain high speed to final approach is possible.
• ATC may not give the final turn onto the ILS LLZ, expect to make this turn without further
instructions. Clearance to land is given after crews have reported Landing Gear down.
RWYs
• ILS GS fluctuations have been reported.
UUEE.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION RWY, TWYs and aprons may be slippery and the markings may not be visible
during wet and snow/icing conditions.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Expect parking stand 134A. Other parking stands may also be offered.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
UUEE.departure_procedures:
De-icing/Anti-icing
• This aerodrome does not provide a visual positive hold control method (billboards). In case of
two-way communication failure with the de-/anti-icing personnel, Flight Crew shall not move the
aircraft and contact ATC for further instructions.
• The below listed de/anti-icing fluids are the only approved fluids for use in QR aircraft ground
de/anti-icing procedures:
‐ Type I "AVIAFLO EG".
‐ Type IV "AVIAFLIGHT EG".
UUEE.miscellaneous:
Local Border Control Procedure
• Following passenger disembarkation and prior to embarkation of departing passengers,
Local Border Control officers are required to board the aircraft to conduct a mandatory visual
inspection of the passenger cabin and flight deck compartment. Crew members are expected
to provide full access and ensure that the officers' onboard inspections proceed without
interference.

Name of Aerodrome: Barajas

RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
10 H24 YES YES YES NIL LMT (1)
Note: 1.(1) RETI NO on ramps R-4, R-5 and R-7 (Freighter Operations).
2.Madrid has capacity restrictions and SLOTS are enforced for arrival/departure. Flight
Crews are reminded to comply with the scheduled arrival/departure times to avoid
incurring significant fines or penalties.
LEMD.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Multiple reports of GPS interference in the vicinity of the aerodrome. Flight
Crew must monitor aircraft navigation performance. Refer to the relevant
FCOM Supplementary Procedure and FOTs .
3.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
LEMD.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Do not mistake Madrid Torrejon aerodrome (LETO/TOJ) located 6NM to the
East for Madrid Barajas.
2.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake RWYs and
parallel TWY for landing RWY.
General
• Report as soon as possible the intention of missed approach maneuver for RWY 18L/36R. This
is required for ATC to restrict departure from RWY 14R/36L.
• Expect several frequency changes from top of descent until landing.
RWY 18L
• VOR RWY 18L approach descent angle not coincident with PAPI.
Local Procedure
• Although the RWY is temporarily occupied by other traffic, landing clearance may be issued to
an arriving aircraft if the controller is satisfied that at the time the aircraft crosses the threshold
of the RWY in use prescribed separation from the preceding aircraft will exist.
• Southerly RWY configuration can result in regular holding and extensive vectoring at Madrid
Barajas. When TS associated with southerly winds are in the area, they usually can be found in
RWY 18L/R arrival routing area.
Delay Information on Approach
• ATC will provide the Expected Approach Time (EAT), when a landing delay is 10 minutes or
more.
LEMD.ground_maneuvering:
TWYs
• TWYs can be easily confused. In case of any doubt exists, request progressive taxi.
Apron/Parking Stands
• APU can be switched on during ground time, but Aerodrome Authority Operations office has to
be informed via QR Station staff.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWYs/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• Ramp 20 (R-20) section leading to stands 562 thru 580 is upslope: Flight Crew should exercise
proper energy management to prevent aircraft from stopping.
• Visual docking guidance: Position of the sun may blind the video camera used in the docking
system.
LEMD.departure_procedures:
Planning
• Report SID (name and number) on first contact with departure frequency.
• Strict adherence to speed limitations.
• Follow SIDs carefully to meet noise abatement procedures.
Boeing Fleet
• It is recommended to insert Flap-1 maneuver speed as a constraint in the FMC at WPT MD048.
• Be prepared to intervene (SPD, TRK or HDG) if the aircraft does not appear to track the SID
correctly.

• CAT 9 from 05:00 to 23:30 (04:00 to 22:30).
• CAT 7 from 23:31 to 04:59 (22:31 to 03:59) and CAT 9 available O/R.
3.Fuelling operating hours: 04:00 to 22:00 (03:00 to 21:00). Outside these hours O/R.
4.Handling and de-icing operating hours: 04:00 to 23:00 (03:00 to 22:00). Outside these
hours O/R.
5.Arrivals can only be expected to receive an approach clearance if overhead “GVA”
VOR:
• No later than 15 minutes before the night curfew comes into effect.
• No earlier than 5 minutes before the night curfew comes to end.
6.Geneva has capacity restrictions and SLOTS are enforced for arrival/departures. Flight
Crews are reminded to comply with the scheduled arrival/departure times to avoid
incurring significant fines or penalties.
7.A Bird Strikes prevention system is available, activation can be requested by
contacting ATC.
8.Consider the use of TERR mode.
LSGG.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Geneva has a busy TMA with high volume of VFR traffic.
3.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
4.Low transition altitude.
LSGG.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION RWY 04: Carefully review the charted DME distance at the threshold for the
planned approach. If the DME is not collocated with the ILS, consider using
the FMC/FMGC Fix Page and runway end waypoint to determine an accurate
distance to the threshold during the approach.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Ensure all descent clearance provide adequate terrain clearance.
• Radar vectoring may result in aircraft left high due reduced track miles.
RWY 04/22
• RWY width is 50m and can create an illusion of being low during the approach and landing flare.
RWY 04
• Due terrain between 14 NM and 10 NM (GVA VOR), Radio Altimeter is inaccurate with
reference to ‘HAT’.
• ILS LOC false captures have been reported. Aircraft may turn towards high terrain during the
false capture. Monitor position using R-224 GVA.
RWY 22
• Lake Geneva is below RWY elevation, Radio Altimeter is inaccurate with reference to ‘HAT’
during final approach.
LSGG.ground_maneuvering:
TWYs
• All fleets: TWY F – NOT AUTHORIZED.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker .
• Marshalling and Follow me vehicle are available on request.
LSGG.departure_procedures:
Refuelling with Pax On-board
• Flight Crews must ensure through the Ground Staff that Fire Brigade Service has been informed
that aircraft will be refuelled with passengers on-board.
• At least one member of the Flight Crew must be in the cockpit and in contact with the Ground
Staff during the process.

Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 H24 YES YES YES NIL NO
UTTT.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Low Transition Altitude.
UTTT.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Do not mistake former Tashkent Vostochny aerodrome with similar RWY
orientation and not depicted on Jeppesen chart located 6 NM Northeast of
Tashkent Islam Karimov (UTTT/TAS).
2.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel RWY for
landing RWY.
3.Turbulence and strong crosswinds may be encountered on final approach.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• STARs are published, but expect radar vectors to final approach.
• ATC may not give the final turn into ILS, expect to make this turn without further instructions.
Clearance to land is given after Flight Crew have reported Landing Gear down.
UTTT.ground_maneuvering:
General
• RWY and TWYs surfaces are rough and bumpy.
TWYs
• TWY markings and signs are faded, poorly lit and difficult to see especially during hours of
darkness or adverse weather conditions.
Apron/Parking Stands
• All fleets. Parking stands from C15 to C41 – NOT AUTHORIZED.
• Parking stand B19: Identification sign is faded and difficult to see. In case of doubt, stop the
aircraft and request ground assistance.
• When taxiing to the final parking position, ensure the aircraft does not overshoot the parking
positions.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker
Winter Operation
• TWYs/Apron marking may not be visible due to snow/ice. In case any doubt, stop the aircraft
and request ground assistance.
UTTT.departure_procedures:
RWY 08L
• Don not to cross the holding point for RWY 08L located on TWY 1, unless instructed by ATC.
Several incidents have occurred where aircraft had missed the holding point.
RWY 08L Holding Point

2.Multiple reports of GPS interference in the vicinity of the aerodrome. Flight
Crew must monitor aircraft navigation performance. Refer to the relevant
FCOM Supplementary Procedure and FOTs.
3.Low transition altitude.
General
• Joint civil and military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponder may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
OIKB.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel RWY
(30m wide) with landing RWY.
2.RWY centerline, touchdown and TWYs markings are faded and difficult to see,
especially during hours of darkness or adverse weather conditions.
MEA on STARs Limitation

RWYs

TWYs

Apron/Parking Stands
• All parking stands are power-in/out.
• No parking stand markings or guidance available on the apron. Follow Marshaller instructions.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
OIKB.departure_procedures:
General

Startup and Pushback

• Clearance Valid 10 minutes from time of startup clearance.
• A new ATC flight plan is required if aircraft fails to depart after two startup clearances.

aircraft navigation and system performance during critical flight phases. Flight

General

aerodrome.
2.Weather information has been reported as inaccurate. Expect crosswind or tail
wind on approach.
3.Turbulence and wind shear may be encountered on final approach.
4.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel RWY for
landing RWY.

Apron layout

OITT.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Due to the geological configuration in the Tabriz area, considerable differences
in the local atmospheric pressures may occur in Winter, leading to large (up to
1,500ft) variations in terrain clearance with 1013 set.
2.Turbulence and strong crosswinds may be encountered on final approach.
3.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel RWY for
landing RWY.
General
• Non-precision approach, check that approach is set in the FMS and have secondary set to the
opposite RWY. Brief both approaches well in advance.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Expect radar vectoring. Note that no radar minimum altitude chart available in Jeppesen.
• Ensure all descent clearance provide adequate terrain clearance.
MEA on STARs Limitation

marshaller/wingwalker.

Startup and Push-back
• Start-Up Call 5-10 minutes before ready to start.
• Clearance is valid 10 minutes from time of startup clearance. Taxi should be requested within
the validity time.
• A new ATC flight plan is required if aircraft fails to depart after two startup clearances.
• Departure should not be made more than 10 minutes before EOBT.

marshaller/wingwalker.

• Clearance is valid 10 minutes from time of startup clearance.
• A new ATC flight plan is required if aircraft fails to depart after two startup clearances.
• ATC Flight Plan is valid for 30 minutes after filed EOBT.

‐ RWY 18 ILS: Every 1st Wednesday (20:00 to 23:00) of the month.
‐ RWY 36 ILS: Every 2nd Wednesday (20:00 to 23:00) of the month.
‐ DVOR: Every 4th Wednesday (20:00 to 23:00) of the month.
ORER.company_policy:

ORER.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Drones and Unmanned Utility Vehicle (UAV) activity in the vicinity of the
aerodrome.
4.Laser targeting has been reported. Inform ATC when applicable.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponders may not be compatible with civil TCAS.

Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• Radio communication with Baghdad ACC has been reported as poor.
Local Procedure
• Restricted Operation Areas (ROZ). High density military traffic. ATC segregates from ROZ
airspace with vertical and lateral buffer which may require rerouting with additional track miles of
up to 50 NM in exceptional circumstances.
ORER.arrival_procedures:
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Ensure all descent clearances provide adequate terrain clearance.
RWY 18/36

• RWY 18 has a considerable and noticeable uphill slope for landing. RWY end is not visible on

ORER.ground_maneuvering:

assistance.
RWY

RWY 36 Turn Pad
• NIL markings available.
• Use the turn technique as detailed in FCOM/FCTM.
TWYs
• No TWY edge lights, except on the apron and at curved portions of TWYs.
• TWY C towards Cargo Apron have uphill slope. Flight Crew should exercise proper energy
management to prevent aircraft from stopping.
ORER.departure_procedures:
Startup and Pushback
• ATC clearance may be cancelled without notification if Flight Crew fails to pushback or taxi
within 10 minutes.
Deicing
• Deicing is conducted on stand, Flight Crew must advise Erbil Tower and request ground
assistant.

Marshaller/Wingwalker.
OJAI.departure_procedures:
Local Procedure
• Persons on Board (POB) information should be advised to ATC prior to departure normally with
pushback request.
De-Icing/Anti-icing Procedures:
1.When ready for pushback coordinate De-Icing with ATC.
2.De-icing is applied in different locations depending on the Apron where aircraft is parked and/or
the Departure RWY for cargo flights, In exceptional cases ATC may require to apply De-icing at
parking stand.
3.North Apron: De-icing is done at spot located in TWY J.
a. After pushback, taxi to TWY J where aircraft will be marshalled to the DE-ICING spot
(marked on ground).
b. After procedure, taxi on own power to TWY H.
4.South Apron: De-icing is done at spot located in TWY E.
a. After pushback, taxi to TWY E where aircraft will be marshalled to the DE-ICING spot
(marked on ground).
b. After procedure, taxi on own power to TWY A.
5.Cargo Apron:
a. Aircraft will be pushed back and towed to the DE-ICING spot.
b. After procedure, taxi on own power to TWY A.
6.Hotel Apron: Procedure will be applied at parking position.

to the North of the border with Israel.
Note: • RWY 16 – NOT AUTHORIZED for Take-off (local regulation).
• RWY 34 – NOT AUTHORIZED for Landing (local regulation).
OLBA.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Military activity in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
4.Laser targeting has been reported. Inform ATC when applicable.
5.Multiple reports of GPS interference in the vicinity of the aerodrome. Flight
Crew must monitor aircraft navigation performance. Refer to the relevant
FCOM Supplementary Procedure and FOTs.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponders may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
RWY Preference
• RWY 16: Preferred RWY for landing.
• RWY 34: Preferred RWY for take-off.
Note: RWY 03 and RWY 17 are also used for landing when RWY 16 is not available.
OLBA.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.False Localizer captures have been reported.
2.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWY A
for RWY 21.
General
• Missed approaches: Fly accurately due terrain on extended centerlines.
RWY 03
• Avoid tendency to go below nominal approach angle to displaced threshold.
RWY 16
• ILS glide slope has been reported as unreliable below 500ft.
RWY 17
• NIL ALS.
• ILS minima are higher than normal criteria.
RWY 21
• RNP approach is offset.
RWY 35
• Landing is not recommended due to terrain and lack of instrument approach procedures.
• NIL ALS and PAPI.
OLBA.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION RWY 34 and RWY 35 share a common area for threshold.
Apron/Parking Stands
Passenger Operations
• Signs and markings have been reported as faded and difficult to identify, especially during hours
of darkness.
• Parking stands G1 to G23: Segments near the final parking position have upslope. Exercise
proper energy management to prevent the aircraft from stopping.
Cargo Operations
• When allocated to the Cargo apron:
‐ No signs/markings depicted on Jeppesen charts, aircraft will be marshalled to final parking
position.
‐ Parking may be offset from centerline due to fuel pump points, follow marshaller instructions.
‐ Taxi slowly to the final parking position as marshaller may request to stop suddenly.

OTBD.name: Doha International
OTBD.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Doha International
Ground Handling: QAS OPS - 121.050
Terminal: TBA
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 H24 NIL YES YES NIL NO
Note: RFF CAT 10 available O/R with 45 minutes PN.
OTBD.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Military activity in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
3.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
4.Laser strike incident threat. Report to ATC if affected.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponder may not be compatible with civil aircraft TCAS.
Prohibited Area ‘OT (P)-43’
• ATC will only clear direct routings when Prohibited Area ‘OT (P)-43’ is not active and they
anticipate that the aircraft will clear the area’s upper vertical boundary above FL 150. If cleared
direct routing, the Flight Crew is responsible for maintaining vertical separation from Prohibited
Area OT (P)-43.
OTBD.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION Do not mistake Hamad International Airport (OTHH/DOH), located 2.8 NM to the
East for Doha International.
Inbound Doha
• When operating within the Bahrain (OBBB) FIR Flight Crew shall ensure that the cleared level is
set in the FCU/MCP altitude window as soon as the cleared level is received from Bahrain ATC.
This also applies to "WHEN READY DESCENT and MAINTAIN" clearances.
OTBD.ground_maneuvering:
RWYs
• RWY 15 Turn Pad – turning should be anti-clockwise.
TWYs
• TWY N
‐ FOD hazards have been reported in and around the maintenance hangers behind holding
point N. These hazards are propagated by jet blast from aircraft setting breakaway thrust
when taxiing from TWY N onto or crossing RWY 33.
‐ Flight Crew must exercise caution when manoeuvring in this area and when able plan for a
continuous turn from Z1 onto the RWY 33 especially for ‘Heavy’ aircraft.
Apron and Parking Stands
• Eastern Apron 3
‐ TWY between TWY P and parking stands E25 to E28, no center-line taxi lighting available.
Lighting is provided from a floodlight system. Follow me vehicle provided, marshalling service
available at stand.
OTBD.departure_procedures:
General
• Several SIDs have similar designators (ALSEM, ALVEN) Flight Crews shall verify that the
cleared SID is inserted into the FMS and briefed accordingly.
Startup and Pushback
• Request pushback only when ready.

OEAH.name: Al Ahsa
OEAH.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Al Ahsa
Ground Handling: TBA
Terminal: Main Terminal
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
7 03:00 to 18:00 NIL NO NO NIL YES
Note: 1.Outside operating hours, Al Ahsa CTR/ATZ is uncontrolled and TIBA Procedures
apply. Refer to the Jeppesen Airway Manual – Middle east, Section ATC – Saudi
Arabia Rules and Procedures.
2.Non-schedule flights: 3 hours PPR.
3.Fuelling NOT available.
4.No ACARs reception.
5.Customs and Immigration available during hours of scheduled operation.
6.Al Ahsa aerodrome can be planned as destination only.
OEAH.company_policy:
• All take-offs and landings shall be performed by Captains or Captains under training only.
• Operating Captain shall have minimum six (6) months experience on type/variant (after
FLC/Command Release Check).
• Training flights - NOT AUTHORIZED, except for initial qualification of the Captains.
• Full approach procedures must be flown.
OEAH.atc:
CAUTION 1.Al Ahsa aerodrome is located within a restricted airspace.
2.Helicopter and General aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Military activity in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponder may not be compatible with civil aircraft TCAS.
• In case ATC authorizes the aircraft to waypoints A, B, C, D or E (ARAMCO corridor), Flight
Crew needs to advise ATC that those waypoints are not available for QR and request for radar
vectors.
• STARs are NOT published.
Radio Frequency
• En-route radio workload is high due to usage of three (3) frequency, Dammam Radar, Al Ahsa
TWR (AFIS) and TIBA frequency.
Route Planning
• Routing to/from Al Ahsa transit through airspace designated as restricted/ danger which is used
for Military activity. Planned routes maybe altered by ATC subject military activity.
OEAH.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel road lights
located East of the aerodrome for RWY lights and parallel TWY for the active
RWY. Parallel TWY is used as Emergency RWY.
RWY 16/34
• VOR approaches are offset.
OEAH.ground_maneuvering:
Note: Arrester cables installed on both RWY and parallel TWY.
TWYs
• No signage indicating TWY and TWY holding points. If any doubt exists, stop the aircraft and
request ground assistance.
• TWYs located West of RWY16/34 – NOT AUTHORIZED.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Parking stands are self maneuvering. No push-back facilities available.
• "Follow me" vehicle available via AFIS.
• Terminal is located on apron adjacent to “control tower”, unofficially known as apron 1.
• There are no stand numbers or guidance lines marked on the Apron. Careful attention should
be paid to marshaller instructions.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWYs/apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due to the
proximity of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and
request marshaller/wingwalker.
OEAH.departure_procedures:
Take-Off Operating Minima
• Qatar Airways (QR) has GACA approval to use lower than standard take-off minima. Take-off
minimums depicted on Jeppesen Charts do not apply to QR operations and are to be
disregarded. Flight Crew shall refer to and apply Low Visibility Take-off Minima as per OM Part
A.

Terminal: Main
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI

8 Day time only YES YES YES NIL YES

Note: 1.RFF CAT 9 available on request with 72 hours PN required.
2.AFIS available H24.
OEAO.company_policy:
• Al Ula can be planned as Destination only.
• Al Ula is authorized for day time operations only and within the AFIS operating hours.
• All take-offs and landings shall be performed by the operating Captain (PIC).
• Operating Captain shall have minimum six (6) months experience on type/variant (after
FLC/Command Release Check).
• EGPWS and TCAS must be operational prior to dispatch from Doha.
• Full approach procedures must be flown.
• Training flights – NOT AUTHORIZED, except for initial qualification of captains.
• If Al Ula altimeter setting is not available, IAPs are not authorized.
OEAO.atc:
WARNING Al Ula is an uncontrolled aerodrome, located within Class G airspace.
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
General
• Flight Crew to obtain aerodrome altimeter setting on AFIS.
• TIBA Procedures: Refer to the Jeppesen Airway Manual – Middle east, Section ATC – Saudi
Arabia Rules and Procedures.
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponders may not be compatible with civil TCAS.
Language/Radio Transmission (R/T)
• En-route radio workload is high due to usage of three (3) frequencies: Jeddah Radar, Al Ula
TWR (AFIS) and TIBA frequency.
OEAO.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION Turbulence and strong crosswinds may be encountered on final approach.
RWYs
• STARs are not published.
OEAO.ground_maneuvering:
RWYs
• 180° turns must be performed at RWY ends only.
TWYs
• Apron and access TWY located Southeast of TWY B4 and not depicted on Jeppesen chart 10-9
– NOT AUTHORIZED.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan and objects during taxi due to the proximity of other aircraft,
equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request Marshaller/Wingwalker.
OEAO.departure_procedures:
Take-Off Operating Minima
• Qatar Airways (QR) has GACA approval to use lower than standard take-off minima. Take-off
minimums depicted on Jeppesen Charts do not apply to QR operations and are to be
disregarded. Flight Crew shall refer to and apply Low Visibility Take-off Minima as per OM Part
A.
Local Procedure
• Expect to receive IFR departure clearance from AFIS during taxi-out.

especially during hours of darkness or adverse weather conditions.
General
• The aerodrome is hard to identify due surrounding lighting.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• No STARs published. Expect radar vectors.
Local Procedure
• ATC generally clears to 15,000ft, Flight Crew discretion and self position to active RWY.
• After vacating RWY, switch the transponder to OFF.
RWY 15
• VOR approach is offset.
• Flight Crew can request adjustment of approach lighting intensity, if deemed insufficient.
RWY 33
• VOR approach is offset.
• NIL ALS.
OEGS.ground_maneuvering:
TWYs
• All Fleets: Judgemental steering required when turning from TWY A to TWY A1 and vice-versa.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop the aircraft and request
marshaller/wingwalker.
• Apron layout is not depicted in Jeppesen chart. The below graphic is for reference only:
Apron Layout

STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Expect ATC to assign altitude constraints, e.g. FL240 by position EMURI.
• ATC may offer significant shortcuts.
Approaches
• All VOR approaches are offset.
OEMA.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION TWY C crossing the threshold of RWY 18.
RWYs
• 180° turn on RWY - NOT AUTHORIZED.
General
• Clearance is required prior crossing RWY 18/36.
• ATC reported issues with the SMGCS unable to ident aircrafts on ground. Airborne aircraft ident
is operating normally. The issues are not related to aircraft ATC x-ponder system malfunction. If
requested by ATC, confirm aircraft ident and continue taxiing as per ATC instructions.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Parking stand 115 has a pronounced upslope entry and subsequent downslope. Flight crew
should exercise proper energy management to prevent aircraft from stopping.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
marshaller/wingwalker.
GPU Usage
• Flight Crew are to use GPU when available during ground time at Madinah.

• In exceptional circumstances a dispensation may be granted before a flight may
operate during the curfew period. The dispensation MUST be received before the
flight is operated.
• An ATC clearance is not considered to be an approval to operate during curfew.
• Flight Crews shall ensure strict adherence to any curfew dispensation, where they
cannot be met ‘operational staff’ shall make further contact to extend the allocated
time.
• Flight Crews must ensure prior to pushback / taxi that they are certain of
getting airborne prior to the curfew or, if applicable, the curfew extension.

delays. Fuel Optimization team is monitoring fuel burn data for all flights on a daily basis.

window is -5 to +15 minutes. If unable to comply with the COBT contact QR Ground Staff for a
new COBT to avoid further delays.
• The COBT may be obtained from CBR ATC GND on 121.7.
• Take-off RWY 17: enter RWY via TWY G then backtrack.
• Take-off RWY 35: enter RWY via TWY N or TWY P.
• When arrival delays are occurring in SYD, expect enroute holding at position CULIN on AWY
Y59. CULIN holding is a non-standard hold (all turns left).

• To conduct walk around hearing protection must be worn. Ground Staff will not allow access to
apron without hearing protection being sighted.

• Expect significant unforecasted tailwinds below 2,000ft.
• Be sure to select the correct ILS RWY 21 approach transition. WOOFY transition will give the 9
DME ARC.
• Late descent from 2,500ft to 2,000ft after lead in radial can lead to a glide slope interception
from above.
• Monitor speed and configuration. Avoid a rushed approach.
• VOR approach is offset.
RWY 06/24
• VOR approaches are offset.
GROUND MANEUVERING
CAUTION Perth aerodrome has fixed Jet bridges, not moving laterally but vertically
only. Flight Crews should strictly adhere to the VDGS indications to prevent
overshooting the stop mark.
Note: To minimize false alarms during Cargo Hold disinfection (ex-Doha), it is recommended to
delay the starting of engines and try to avoid completely closing the cargo doors for a few
minutes after spraying of the insecticide (to allow the spray dispersing).
Apron/Parking Stands
• Parking stands 150, 151, 162 and 163 have upslope towards final parking position.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
Ground Delays
• Implemented if airborne delay exceeds 10 minutes.
Startup and Pushback
• Engine start authorized during pushback once past clearance line.
• Pushback onto TWY T is only required, when RWY 21 is in use.
• When traffic conflicts or request for guidance, ATC will provide a direction in terms of "pushback
approved tail (direction) "or "expect exit via (TWY).
• Parking stand 151:
‐ Push to disconnect point, then start.
‐ Disconnect point marked as “4D” at the entry to TWY T is not depicted on Jeppesen Charts.
Planning
• Adherence to SIDs/vectors is required. Do not request direct route immediately after departure.

Note: 1.Aerodrome is located in a noise sensitive area.
2.Sydney has capacity restrictions and SLOTS are enforced for arrival/departure. Flight
Crews are reminded to comply with the scheduled arrival/departure times to avoid
incurring significant fines or penalties.
3.Due to curfew restrictions, it is strongly recommended not to plan Sydney as
destination alternate or for diversion during the curfew time.

aerodrome.
Delays and Busy Periods
• Anticipate airborne traffic delays of 20 minutes for arriving aircraft due to terminal area traffic
density and/or single RWY operations.
Note: Flight Crew are advised that the above note is for information only as per Australian
CASA and that there is no requirement to carry fuel specifically to cover such possible
delays. Fuel Optimization team is monitoring fuel burn data for all flights on a daily basis.
Any statistical usage of CONT Fuel, will be added by Flight Dispatch as Dispatch Fuel.

for a few minutes after the spraying of insecticide in order to give the spray time to
disperse.

NZAA.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Do not mistake Ardmore uncontrolled aerodrome located 8 NM to the East for
Auckland International.
2.RWY 23L. Expect high sink rate on final approach before touchdown zone due
to buildings within vicinity of aerodrome.
3.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWY for
landing RWY.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Do not accept visual approach clearance until RWY is correctly identified.
NZAA.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION Do not cross the RWY holding points without ATC clearance, even when stop
bars are not lit. Readback of RWY holding instruction is required.
Apron/Parking stands
• Expect to taxi to/from International terminal apron via TWY J for the apron Eastern side and via
TWY K for the apron Western side.
• Expect to be assigned to a contact stand.
• For extended ground time, the aircraft will be towed off to a remote stand 60 minutes after
arrival. The aircraft will be towed back to a contact stand 90 minutes before departure.
NZAA.departure_procedures:
DCL procedure:
• DCL to be initiated within 25 minutes of filed EOBT.
• ATC will respond upon receipt of request.
• The ATC clearance will normally be received within 5 minutes of the DCL request.
• Flight Crew should respond (accept/reject) to the DCL clearance within 5 minutes.
• If the 5 minutes window is exceeded, revert to voice clearance with ATC.
RWY 05R – Take-off Restrictions
• Normal RWY Operations
‐ Takeoff shall be performed from intersection A10.
‐ The use of RWY 05 western extension for full RWY length take-off is only authorized when
RWY is wet. Flight Crew must contact Auckland Delivery on 128.2 prior to start, requesting a
backtrack. TORA = 3535m.
• Work in Progress (YELLOW Pages), WIP EAST
‐ Take-off shall be performed from intersection A10.
‐ If western extension (portion of RWY west of A10) is required for takeoff, request shall
be made via AUCKLAND delivery when requesting route clearance. Refer to NOTAM
information for activation of WORK IN PROGRESS EAST (YELLOW) Jeppesen pages for
further details.
• Work in Progress (YELLOW Pages), WIP WEST
‐ Take-off shall be performed from the displaced threshold between A9 and A8.
‐ If intersections A10 or A9 are required for takeoff, request shall be made via AUCKLAND
delivery when requesting route clearance. Refer to NOTAM information for activation of
WORK IN PROGRESS WEST (YELLOW) Jeppesen pages for further details.

Note: Ohakea is authorized as DALT for Auckland operations. The following requirements shall
be considered:
1.IOCC shall be contacted via SATCOM or ACARS in the event of a potential/actual
diversion to Ohakea.
2.In the event of a diversion to Ohakea, Flight Crew shall notify Air New Zealand
operations control (Auckland) on frequency 131.9 advising ETA at Ohakea.
3.Ohakea RNZAF Operations should be notified on 135.1 or 134.5 advising ETA and
fuel uplift required.
4.Passengers must remain onboard the aircraft for the duration of the transit.
Disembarkation would only occur if the aircraft is unable to depart or in case of a
medical requirement. IOC must be contacted for instructions if disembarkation is
required.
5.Cargo holds must remain closed for the duration of the transit. There is no suitable
loading equipment in Ohakea to support the removal, shift or addition of any
baggage/cargo/mail containers.
6.Diversion to OHA is prohibited if a potential AOG upon landing (except emergencies)
is known prior to top of descent. IOC must be contacted for instructions.

Marshaller/Wingwalker.

attention should be paid to Marshaller instructions.
FZAA.departure_procedures:
Startup and Pushback
• Request ATC clearance 20 minutes prior to taxi.
• Startup clearance should include requested flight level, endurance, fuel and persons on board.
• ATC clearance is usually given during taxi-out.
