HCMM.name: Aden Adde International
HCMM.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Aden Adde International
Ground Handling: FAVORI LLC
Terminal: Main Apron/New Terminal
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
7 03:00 to 15:00 NIL NO YES YES NO
CAUTION 1.Birds in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.VIP movement(s) are not announced through NOTAM, delays can be expected.
Note: There is no maintenance engineer in MGQ. An engineer will travel on-board the aircraft
on sector JIB-MGQ-JIB and will be included on the GD.
HCMM.company_policy:
• Mogadishu can be planned as Destination only.
• Mogadishu is authorized for day time operations only.
• All take-off and landing by Captain or Captain under training only.
• TCAS and EGPWS must be operative prior to dispatch from Doha.
• Do not dispatch with APU INOP. If APU becomes INOP after dispatch, contact IOC as soon as
practicable.
• Uplift of potable water and catering items are not permitted from Mogadishu.
HCMM.atc:
WARNING Traffic separation between VFR and IFR flight is not provided. Not all traffic may
be displayed on TCAS.
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.VFR traffic and Military aircraft operating without transponder or an
incompatible TCAS system in the vicinity of aerodrome.
3.Helicopter and Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (Drones) activity in the vicinity of the
aerodrome.
4.Low transition altitude.
General
• Joint Civil and Military Aerodrome.
• Class D airspace up to FL145.
• Class G airspace above FL145.
• Non-radar environment. Procedural separation with the use of DME and radials to separate
arriving and departing traffic.
• ATC standard has been reported as poor. Flight Crew to maintain the highest levels of vigilance
and monitor possible conflicting traffic through all available means.
• IATA IFBP (In Flight Broadcast Procedure) apply in Mogadishu FIR.
• NAV Database has only 5 characters and the AWYs in Class G airspace have 6 characters.
Local Procedures
• ATC requires the POB and Endurance during the first contact.
• Holding: If required, ATC will request aircraft to hold over the water.
Peak Periods
• From 06:00 to 09:00.
HCMM.arrival_procedures:
General
• Do not descent below FL100 within 65 NM of the aerodrome until over the sea.
• RWY lights are solar powered and operated from dusk till dawn.
RWY 05
• NIL ALS and PAPI.
• RWY 05 is preferred for landing up to the maximum tailwind limit.
RWY 23
• NIL ALS, IAP and PAPI.
• Guided Visual available.
HCMM.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION 1.Degraded braking action when RWY wet, due to considerable rubber deposits.
2.Animals observed on the ground maneuvering areas.
3.FOD and ground vehicles on the RWY, aprons and TWYs.
General
• TWY and Apron located at threshold RWY 23 – NOT AUTHORIZED.
Turn Pads
• Intermediate turn pads located along RWY 05/23 – NOT AUTHORIZED.
• After landing on RWY 05 perform 180 degrees turn over RWY surface.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Expect to be parked on Stands 8, 9 or 5 (in preferential order) on the Eastern side of the apron.
Other stands may be offered.
• The main apron is congested. Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects
during taxi due to the proximity of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists,
stop aircraft and request Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• If any aircraft is parked on stand 1, one Wing Walker will be positioned at the edge of stand 1 to
ensure wing tip clearance for taxing from TWY A towards the apron and vice versa.
HCMM.departure_procedures:
General
• If a Take-off alternate is required, ensure the flight is dispatched under ETOPS.
• ATC flight plan is filed locally by the handling agent.
Refuelling with Pax onboard
• Fire truck must be present.
Startup and Push-back
• Engine startup is to be performed after completion of push-back.
Preferred RWY
• Expect RWY 23 for departure. If an SID is not issued by ATC, request to fly a SID appropriate to
the route. In case of a VMC departure, immediate left turn should be commenced in accordance
with the guidance in OM-A.
• In case of RWY 05 departure, immediate right turn should be commenced in accordance with
the guidance in OM-A.
SIDs
• Boat patrol in place for departures from RWY 23 to ensure no boats, ships cross the
take-off/approach flight path.
HCMM.miscellaneous:
General
• Local security teams with dogs may inspect the aircraft during the turnaround.
• If no maintenance engineer available, Commanders are required to conduct the transit check.

ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWYs and
intersecting RWY for landing RWY.
Note: For CAT II/III operations, the maximum crosswind component is 15Kt.
Simultaneous Parallel RWY Operations – RWY35L and RWY35R
• Aircraft must be level prior to turning base leg.
• 1,000ft vertical separation is maintained with aircraft on the opposite RWY until both aircraft are
established on the final approach (localizer).
• When given a descend clearance ATC expect to comply immediately, as the aircraft on
the “lower side” cannot be turned base until they are level. If slow in descend expect to be
re-sequenced or left to fly downwind.
GROUND MANEUVERING
Apron/Parking Stands

Marshaller/Wingwalker.
TWYs

DEPARTURE PROCEDURES

aircraft call sign, assigned SID name and transponder code.

NIL

‐ Parallel TWYs A, B, E and F towards Northwest.

‐ Marshaller is always in front of the aircraft holding his position until everyone is clear.
‐ Before taxiing follow the instructions of the Marshaller.

COMPANY POLICY

AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL
CAUTION 1.TCAS alert may be triggered due to extensive VFR traffic operation around the
aerodrome.
2.Busy ATC environment due to several nearby aerodromes.
3.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
4.Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (drones) have been reported.
RWY Assignment
• ATC can authorize simultaneous approaches:
‐ for easterly arrivals on RWY 06L/R and RWY 07L/R.
‐ for westerly arrivals on RWY 24L/R, RWY 25L/R and Hawthorne aerodrome RWY 25.
• Simultaneous departures can be authorized for RWY 24L/R and RWY 25L/R with course
divergence beginning within 2 NM from the departure end of parallel RWYs.
• If RWY assigned is not acceptable, coordinate with ATC well in advance, as late notification
may result in arrival or departure delay.
Noise Abatement Procedures (Weather permitting)
• Westerly Operations (Daytime):
‐ Westerly operations is the normal traffic pattern during daytime for arrival and departures due
to prevailing Westerly wind.
‐ Arrivals are usually assigned “Outer” RWY 24R and RWY 25L.
‐ Departures are assigned “Inner” RWY 24L and RWY 25R.
• Over-Ocean Operations (00:00 to 06:30 local time):
‐ All landing and take-offs usually occur on the “Inner” RWYs.
‐ Departures are usually assigned to depart over the ocean to the West (RWY 24L and RWY
25R) with no turns authorized before crossing shoreline.
‐ Arrivals are assigned over the ocean to the East (RWY 06R and RWY 07L).
• Easterly Operations:
‐ Easterly operations are used when the wind is Easterly in direction.
‐ Arrivals are usually assigned to two “Outer” RWYs (RWY 06L and RWY 07R).
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION 1.Do not mistake Hawthorne (KHHR/HHR) aerodrome, located 4 NM to the
East of aerodrome with similar RWY orientation, for Los Angeles (KLAX/LAX)
International.
2.Carefully review the charted DME distance at the threshold for the planned
approach. If the DME is not collocated with the ILS, consider using the
FMC/FMGC Fix Page and RWY end waypoint to determine an accurate
distance to the threshold during the approach.
3.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWYs
and parallel RWY for landing RWY.
4.All RWYs have the same QDM, but designated differently to avoid confusion.
Note: For CAT II/III operations, the maximum crosswind component is 15Kt.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Expect radar vectoring.
Simultaneous Parallel RWY operations
• Do not accept visual approach until landing RWY is correctly identified.
Separation
• Reduced separation of 2.5 NM between arrivals is authorized for approaches on RWY 24R and
RWY 25L.
CODE E FLEET

required.

Ground Control.
• Follow-me vehicle available on request.
TWYs

• TWY C between TWY C5 and TWY C9 – NOT AUTHORIZED, portion of TWY C between
TWYs C9 and C10 is authorized for pushback purposes only.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft, TWY/Apron restrictions, and objects during taxi due to the proximity of other
aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request Marshaller/Wing
Walker.
Passenger Operations
• QR operates from the Tom Bradley International Terminal (TBIT).
• Flight Crew may expect gate assignment delays and/or remote parking when a gate is
unavailable.
• Access to TBIT gates via taxi lane C-10 is controlled by American Airlines Ramp Tower on
frequency 129.325.
Cargo Operations
• QR Cargo operates from Imperial Cargo Complex, located South of RWY 25L threshold.
• Parking on Imperial Cargo Complex is performed with engine shut down on TWY A1 then tow
onto the stand.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
WARNING High Terrain located northeast and east from aerodrome. Departing Los Angeles
on LAXX , OSHNN or SEBBY SIDS, the following diversion strategy should be
followed in case of engine failure:
• After the first turn and below 10,000ft: return to KLAX.
CAUTION When departing RWY 25L/R on SEBBY Departure, Flight Crew are to ensure that
the turn is initiated immediately when passing SMO-R160 (Santa Monica VOR).
Note: Flight Crew are required to cross-check that all SID altitude constraints are met in the
FMS. If unable to comply, request another SID.
Startup and Pushback
Operations on Imperial Cargo Complex
‐ Pushback clearance should be requested from Ground.
Planning
• Intersection departures may be authorized on RWY 24L TWY E8 and RWY 25R TWY F
intersections.
• Simultaneous departure from RWY 25L/25R: When published departure procedure include a left
turn, expeditious compliance with turn is required.
SIDs
• Adherence to SIDs/Vectors is required.
• Do not request direct route immediately after departure.
• Expect further clearance to filed altitude at least 3 minutes after departure.

KEWR.name: Newark Liberty International
KEWR.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Newark Liberty International
Ground Handling: TBA
Terminal: TBA
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 (E) H24 NIL NO YES NIL NO
Note: Aerodrome is located in a noise sensitive area.
CODE E FLEET
Note: RWY 11/29 — NOT AUTHORIZED for landing and take-off.
COMPANY POLICY
NIL
AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Busy ATC environment due to several nearby aerodromes.
3.TCAS alert may be triggered due to extensive VFR traffic operation around the
aerodrome.
4.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
General
• Newark is located within the New York Terminal Control Area, sharing airspace with both New
York - Kennedy Intl and La Guardia.
• When cloud base drops below 500ft, traffic holding builds up quickly. Holdings of 1h or greater
may affect both arriving and departing flights.
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel RWY or
TWYs for landing RWY.
Note: When conducting CAT II/CAT III operations, the maximum crosswind component is
limited to 15Kt.
General
• In a nonstandard phraseology, the descent clearance is issued in such wordings or manner as
“reach XYZ at 13,000ft”, even though the word “Descent” is not mentioned.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• There is limited use of STARs.
• Expect radar vectors for ILS approach RWY 04R or RWY 22L (ATC preferential RWYs for
landing).
• Considerably low level vectoring is normal.
GROUND MANEUVERING
RWYs
• RWY 04R/22L
‐ After landing RWY 04R or RWY 22L, TWR will instruct arrivals to hold short of RWY 04L or
RWY 22R for departing traffic.
‐ Arriving aircraft are expected to clear arrival RWY, continue pass center TWY P and read
back the “Hold Short” instructions.
• RWY Crossing
‐ After RWY crossing instructions are received, the aircraft is authorized to taxi beyond first
TWY to ensure the entire aircraft is clear of active RWY.
• Do not necessarily expect hand-offs between Ground and Ramp Controls and vice versa. After
landing, if not instructed, initiate call to Ramp Control PRIOR to ENTERING the ramp area.
TWYs
• Judgmental oversteering required.
Apron/Parking stands
• Exercise vigilance when the ramp is wet or when snow/ice is present. If determined by PIC that
aircraft cannot be parked safely under own power, request towing.
• Parking stands. If no parking stands available on the International Terminal, expect parking in
the remote area.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
Startup and Pushback
• Request clearance up to 25 minutes prior to departure, except when ATIS indicates departure
delays are in effect.
• Flow control procedures can often cause extensive ground delays.
• Monitor and maintain listening watch with “flow control” (GATE HOLD frequency) at all times.
• Failure to respond to 3 successive calls or inability to taxi at the issued taxi time will result in
further delay.
• Initiate call to Ground Control PRIOR to EXITING the ramp area if not instructed to do so.
De-icing
• Completed on stand.
• “Snow-desk” will coordinate deicing times. Ensure ATC is advised of the times.
RWY Preference
• Expect RWY 22R or RWY 04L for departure.
MISCELLANEOUS
NIL

KJFK.name: Kennedy International
KJFK.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Kennedy International.
Ground Handling:
• Pax: SWISSPORT RAMP SERVICES – NIL (For ground services request, liaise with ground
staff).
• Terminal Handling: AMERICAN AIRLINES T8 TOWER – 129.2 (For terminal entry/exit
clearances and for gate assignment).
Terminal:
• Pax: Terminal 8
• Cargo: ‘China Airlines Cargo Facility’ (Building 76)
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 (E) H24 NIL NO YES NIL YES
Note: 1.Aerodrome is located in a noise sensitive area.
2.Preferred Noise Abatement Departure Procedure is NADP 2.
COMPANY POLICY
• The following approach procedures shall only to be conducted by the Captain or Captain under
training:
‐ VOR or GPS RWY 13L/R.
‐ RNAV (RNP) RWY 13L/R (Authorized fleets only).
CODE E FLEET - A350/B777
RNP AR
• This aerodrome is authorized for RNP AR procedures. Flight Crews are encouraged to request
the applicable RNP AR approach if conditions permit. The Journey Log Approach Flown - RNP
AR form must be completed accordingly.
AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL
CAUTION 1.TCAS alert may be triggered due to extensive VFR traffic operation around the
aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, General Aviation including Para-sailing and Banner towing activity in
the vicinity of the aerodrome.
3.Radio interference and false instructions on TWR frequencies have been
reported.
Note: Peak Arrival Times 15:00 to 17:00 Local Time.
Holding
• When cloud base drops below 500ft, traffic holding builds up quickly. Holdings of one hour or
greater are common, affecting both arriving and departing flights.
• Holding speed/altitude within New York area.
Altitude Airspeed (KIAS)
Up to 6,000ft 200Kt
6,001 - 14,000ft 210Kt
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
CAUTION 1.Avoid rushed approaches. Consider requesting a 10 NM final approach.
2.Immediately advise ATC in the event of a missed approach.
3.Carefully review the charted DME distance at the threshold for the planned
approach. If the DME is not collocated with the ILS, consider using the
FMC/FMGC Fix Page and RWY end waypoint to determine an accurate
distance to the threshold during the approach.
4.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWYs for
landing RWY.
5.Use caution for possible VHF radio interference and false instructions on tower
frequencies.
Note: For CAT II/III operations, the maximum crosswind component is 15Kt.
Operational Flight Plan (OFP) — DESCENT PRESENTATION
• Arrival procedures are shown as “DCT” segment on OFP, to cater for flight level profiling. The
“DCT” routing is as per published STARs. Distance bias has been added where required. The
abbreviation “TOD” appears within the “Arrival procedure” should be disregarded.
Descent/STAR Overview
• Early descent with Boston Control is standard, 200 - 300 NM away. Fuel consumption, for the
expected early descent profiles are included in the OFP.
• Be familiar with full place names of approach aids as these are frequently referred to by ATC.
• Non-standard phraseology. Descent clearance is given as “reach XYZ at 12,000ft”, even though
the word descent is not mentioned.
• For all STARs, plan descent in order to comply with all published “Expect to cross at
FL/Altitudes” instructions.
• STARs from North Atlantic/North East can expect routing via “Kennebunk (ENE)” or “Plymouth
(PLYMM)”. STAR maybe suspended by radar vectoring. Traffic from East-Northeast and
“PLYMM” is routed via waypoint “ROBER”, after which radar vectors are provided.
• “Kingston (IGN)” STAR, expect Enroute holdings as published. Approximately 175 NM from
“IGN” to RWY 31L/R .
• Expect extensive low level radar vectoring.
• Expect ATC to assign speed constraints.
Approach Overview
• RNAV (GPS) and VOR approaches are regularly assigned in order to improve traffic flow and
for noise abatement purposes.
• Simultaneous approaches used to intersecting RWYs.
• Be prepared for late RWY changes.
RWY 04L
• NIL ALS.
RWY 04R
• VOR approach is offset.
RWY 13L
• ILS approach is rarely used due to conflict with La Guardia approach, but when used, arrival
rates drop to around 28 per hour (half of normal rate). VASI is turned off during RWY 13L ILS.
RWY 22L
• RNAV (GPS) X is offset and require wings level below 400ft AAL after passing DA/MDA.
RWY 22R
• Combination of offset ILS approach and displaced threshold may give optical illusions at DA.
RWY 13L/R Approaches (VOR or GPS Approach)
• ATC may offer either RWY late during the arrival.
• RWY 13L/R Visual Cues;
‐ RWY Lead-In Light System (RLLS) provides visual guidance to the RWY environment.
‐ When visual references are established, fly visually to the aerodrome via the RLLS to the
RWY.
‐ Exercise vigilance when visually acquiring the RWY lights as misidentification can occur.
‐ RLLS RWY 13L uses the 1,000 ft lights section of the ALS when VOR or GPS approach is
in use and is angled toward the AQUEDUCT, also 5 SFL from 1,200 ft to 2,000 ft and a 5
SFL grouping approximately 1 mile from the RWY with 1 adjacent light forming the approach
lights. The approach gate is angled 35° south of RWY 13L centerline designed to provide
earlier identification of the RWY environment.
RWY 31L
• VOR approach is offset.
CODE E FLEET
A350/B777 – RNAV (RNP) APPROACHES
• RWY 13L/R RNAV (RNP) approaches require wings level below 400 ft AAL.
• Aircraft should be wings level by waypoints JONAT (RWY 13R) and JEVNI (RWY 13L).
GROUND MANEUVERING
RWYs
• Due to lack of space between RWYs and outer TWY B, it is important that aircraft do not stop
on RWY exits, continue moving away from RWY following taxi instructions from TWR.
• Landing RWY 04R or RWY 22L and crossing RWY 04L/22R:
‐ TWR will instruct aircraft to hold short of RWY 04L or RWY 22R and remain on TWR
frequency.
‐ RWY crossing instructions is issued by TWR. Do not call ground control.
‐ Depending on traffic, considerable holding time may be experienced during RWY crossing.
TWYS
• Kennedy has a complex network of TWYs, which may be confusing during nighttime operation.
Apron/Parking Stands
CAUTION Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to
the proximity of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop
aircraft and request Marshaller/Wingwalker.
Passenger operations
Note: Prior to landing, ensure receipt of the assigned parking stand and the apron entry point,
as Kennedy Ground will not be aware of either.
• Apron entry points to Terminal 8 are via TWY NC or TWY TA.
• Terminal 8 is controlled by American Airlines (AA) Ramp Control on frequency 129.2.
• After landing:
‐ Advise Kennedy Ground that the aircraft is proceeding to Terminal 8 and the assigned apron
entry point.
‐ Establish contact with AA Ramp Control on 129.2 and remain on Ground frequency.
‐ Prior to entering the Terminal 8 apron, request clearance from AA Ramp Control and further
instructions.
• Parking stands 8, 12, 46 and 47 are MARS stands for different aircraft types:
‐ Code E/F: Follow the yellow dashed lines to the position designated with alphanumeric code
8X, 12X, 46X, 47X.
Cargo Operations
QR operates from ‘China Airlines Cargo Facility’ (Building 76).
• Access to the apron is via TWYs C6 or W. Judgemental steering is required.
• Advise ground control on frequency 121.9 MHz that you are parking at ‘Building 76 – Cargo
Area C’.
• Do not enter the apron until marshaller and wingwalkers are present to guide and assist the
parking maneuver.
• If there is an aircraft parked in the apron south of Building 76, the aircraft will be towed to the
final parking position.
Cargo Apron
Cargo Apron
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
Startup and Pushback
Note: On several parking stands, Flight Crew may observe interference on one or more VHF
radios. This interference ceases after pushback from the stand.
• ATC Clearance: Call Kennedy Clearance 25 minutes prior to departure.
• Ramp Area Clearance – Terminal 8
‐ Pushback Clearance. Contact AA Ramp Control 129.2.
‐ Do not enter main TWYs system without clearance from Ground control.
De-icing
• A snow desk run by Port Authority is in operation at Kennedy aerodrome in which airline agents
and ATC work together to minimize departure delays and ensure that holdover times will not be
impacted during delays.
• Contact SWISSPORT on 131.950 prior to pushback when ready.
• Contact Kennedy Ground for taxi instructions to Romeo de-icing pads.
• Contact SWISSPORT on 131.950 prior to entry onto TWY S for taxi and Pad instructions.
Note: Left turn from TWY S to TWY R is not allowed. If the aircraft missed the TWY SD, towing
is required to turn around the aircraft.
Planning
• Take-off RWY 04L:
‐ Expect to fly RWY heading to 1.5 DME, then turn right to heading 100, providing separation
from possible missed approach on RWY 04R, who are also as required to turn right to
heading 100 per the map. At Flight Crew discretion, ATC may instruct aircraft to turn right to
heading 100 prior to 1.5 DME when arrival aircraft is not a factor.
• Take-off RWY 31L
‐ Expect climb to 5,000ft until about 20 NM East of Kennedy aerodrome, then expect clearance
to continue climb to 11,000ft after clearing arrival traffic. Be aware that CANARSIE climb
requires an early turn.
‐ When landing RWY 04R and departing RWY 31L, all aircraft receive the BREEZY POINT
Climb.
• SIDs:
‐ Radar departures are sometimes issued instead of SID.
‐ Call New York Departure when instructed by TWR.
‐ Expect step climbs.
• Speed Control: ATC expects 250KT to 10,000ft.

NIL

considerations.

been reported as having soft spots. Additional thrust may be required when approaching the

and Ground Engineer to request pushback to TWYs G or D to avoid the soft spots.

• Expect radar vectoring.
• Ensure all descent clearance provide adequate terrain clearance.
• ATC may not give the final turn onto the ILS LLZ. Expect to make this turn without further
instructions. Clearance to land is given after Flight Crew has reported Landing Gear down.
RWY 08
• ILS IDENT signal may be intermittent.
• Due to lack of surrounding lighting in vicinity of the aerodrome, a “black hole” effect may be
experienced during night operations.
• Approach Lightning System and PAPI lights are dim and difficult to identify.
• Surface wind report does not reflect the actual wind on final approach.
• RWY has a considerable and noticeable uphill slope for landing. RWY end is not visible on
landing roll.
RWY 26
• NIL ALS.

request marshaller/wingwalker.

EGPH.name: Edinburgh
EGPH.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Edinburgh
Ground Handling: SWISSPORT EDINBURG – 131.55
Terminal: TBA
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
H24
9 Curfew: Refer NIL YES YES NIL YES
to note 1
Note: 1.Night Noise Level Restrictions between 23:30 to 06:00 (22:30 to 05:00):
• A330/B777 – NOT AUTHORIZED.
• A35K– Only Authorized when the take-off weight is limited at or below 296,000kg.
• B789 – Only Authorized when the take-off weight is limited at or below 231,000kg.
2.Fuel operating hours: 05:30 to 23:00 (04:30 to 22:00). Outside these hours by prior
arrangement with handling agent.
3.Edinburgh has capacity restrictions and SLOTS are enforced for arrival/departure.
Flight Crews are reminded to comply with the scheduled arrival/departure times to
avoid incurring significant fines or penalties.
EGPH.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Military operations in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
4.Glider activity in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
5.Low transition altitude.
EGPH.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Carefully review the charted DME distance at the threshold for the planned
approach. If the DME is not collocated with the ILS, consider using the
FMC/FMGC Fix Page and runway end waypoint to determine an accurate
distance to the threshold during the approach.
2.Railway tracks are located approximately 340m from the end of RWY 24.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Expect radar vectors to 9 NM on final.
RWY Preference
• RWY 24 is the preferred RWY for landing.
• When RWY 06 is in use, expect significant tail wind during the initial westerly approach leg
bringing the aircraft high on profile.
EGPH.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION TWY INCURSION THREAT. Aerodrome has a complex TWY network. Flight
Crew must familiarize themselves with the Jeppesen Airport charts and Airport
Briefing pages (taxi procedures) before operating to Edinburgh (EGPH/EDI).
Do not initiate or continue taxi unless both Flight Crew members have received,
understood and agreed on the ATC taxi clearance.
Note: Intersection of TWY A and TWYs L and M has history of TWY incursions. Occurrence of
missing either TWY L or M when taxing to the designated parking stand. ATC clears via
L1 or M1. Area is depicted as HS1 in the Jeppesen 10-9 chart.
Intersection TWYs L and M
TWYs
• Simultaneous taxi operations on TWYs L and M is authorized with no restrictions. Strict
adherence to TWY L centerline is required due to wingtip clearance.
• Judgmental oversteering required.
Apron/Parking Stands
CAUTION 1.Pilots shall not turn off from the taxiway center line unless the stand entry
guidance is illuminated with the aircraft identification displayed or a marshaller
has signaled clearance to proceed.
2.Do not exceed 5 kt during the turn.
• Expect to be parked on stands 15, 16 or 18.
• Main apron. In case parking stand 4A is assigned, follow the below procedure:
• Stand 4A:
1.Entry/exit TWY E via holding position E1 only.
2.Parking is power-in and pushback, assisted by Marshaller on request.
3.The service road at the right wingtip should be blocked during parking due to wingtip
clearance.
4.Pushback procedure:
‐ Aircraft is pushed back to TWY F abeam parking stands 6–7, to taxi out via TWY E.
‐ Pushback clearance from stand 4A onto TWY F will be issued by ATC only when it is
confirmed that the adjacent service roads have been closed by Airside Operations.
‐ Wingtip clearance will be monitored by Airfield Operations during aircraft maneuvering
from TWY F until E-1 holding point.
EGPH.departure_procedures:
General
• To avoid pushback delays, advice ATC 5 minutes before pushback, to coordinate the ramp
operations.
De-icing/Anti-icing
• De/anti-icing is performed at the gate by SWISSPORT staff.
• Inform ground staff of the need for de/anti-icing. SWISSPORT staff will contact the Crew and
coordinate the procedure.
SIDs – BOEING FLEET
SIDs out of UK aerodromes are Noise Preferential Routes (NPRs).

‐ Using the applicable Flap setting do not exceed 190Kt.
‐ On completion of the initial turn accelerate and clean up on schedule.
• Departures with an initial turn less than 30°.
‐ Accelerate and clean up on schedule as per NOP.

General
• At 2 NM from touchdown, if the aircraft is off track by 1 NM, Go-around instruction will be issued
by ATC.
Local Procedures
• After landing RWY 08R/26L, clearance is not required to cross RWY 08L/26R, unless otherwise
instructed.
Continuous Descent Approach Operations
• Due to noise abatement requirements this aerodrome requires Continuous Descent Approach
(CDA) operations. All operators CDA vertical tracks are monitored for compliance. For further
details refer to the Jeppesen AIRPORT BRIEFING pages ARRIVAL, NOISE ABATEMENT
section.
EGKK.ground_maneuvering:
RWYs

EGKK.departure_procedures:
Planning
• Flight Crew should plan for an on-time departure despite being notified of an ATC slot
restriction. This includes on-time boarding of passengers. When a departure slot has been
issued, Flight Crew should advise ATC to send a ready message when pre-departure formalities
are complete.
• When allocated parking stand 573, Flight Crew may request non-standard pushback procedure
if parking stand 64 is unoccupied. If approved, aircraft will be pushed back onto parking stand
64 then pulled forward towards TWY Q for engine start-up.
RWY 26L
• Starter extension of 150m is available.
SIDs – BOEING FLEET

CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (Drones) activity has been reported, especially to
the Northwest of the Aerodrome (RWY 09L threshold). Exercise vigilance and
report to ATC when spotted as soon as possible.
3.Busy ATC environment due to several nearby aerodromes.
4.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
5.Low transition altitude.
6.Multiple reports of GPS interference in the vicinity of the aerodrome. Flight
Crew must carefully monitor aircraft navigation and system performance during
critical flight phases. Flight Crew shall refer to the FCOM supplementary
procedure following any GPS outage.
EGLL.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION Ensure that RWY and STAR selected in the FMS are in accordance with ATC
clearance.
RWYs
• RNP approaches descent angle is not coincident with PAPI.
Continuous Descent Approach Operations
• Due to noise abatement requirements this aerodrome requires Continuous Descent Approach
(CDA) operations. All operators CDA vertical tracks are monitored for compliance. For further
details refer to the Jeppesen AIRPORT BRIEFING pages ARRIVAL, NOISE ABATEMENT
section.
EGLL.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION Do not confuse the broken RWY edge lines (markings) at the displaced threshold
of RWYs 09L/R with the RWY centre line markings.
TWYs
• Taxi instructions is normally to “follow the greens” to assigned stand. Note lighting configuration
may lead past assigned stand, resulting in overshooting assigned parking stand.
• A significant upslope has been reported on TWY S3 towards crossing RWY 09R/27L. Exercise
proper energy management to enable continuous taxi while crossing the RWY.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Terminal 4. Parking stands 401 to 407 have an upslope towards the final parking position. Flight
Crews should exercise proper energy management during RETI operations.
EGLL.departure_procedures:
De-Icing
• Service provider is AéroMag De-Ice.
• De-icing/anti-icing takes place on the gate, prior to engine start.
• Confirm with BA ground engineer that they are ready to start de-icing, so that the ground
engineer and other ground staff are aware that de-icing will start.
• De-icing (Type-1) and Anti-Icing (Type-4) are available.
• 30 minutes prior to STD, contact AéroMag De-Ice on 121.730 and provide the following
information:
‐ Call sign and flight number.
‐ Stand number.
‐ Estimated departure time.
‐ De-icing treatment required.
• Remain on the frequency until de-icing process is complete.
• Once the Jet Bridge and ground equipment are clear from the aircraft, Iceman will confirm with
the Flight Crew that the aircraft is configured prior to start of de-icing.
• Once de-icing/anti-icing is complete the trucks will move safely away from the aircraft and the
Aeromag ICEMAN will provide the Flight Crew with the post de-icing information.
• AéroMag can also be contacted on AéroMag Operations: +44 020 875 04991.
• For ATC coordination refer Jeppesen briefing pages.
SIDs - BOEING FLEET
SIDs out of UK aerodromes are Noise Preferential Routes (NPRs).
• Departures with an initial turn greater than 30°.
‐ Using the applicable Flap setting do not exceed 190Kt.
‐ On completion of the initial turn accelerate and clean up on schedule.
• Departures with an initial turn less than 30°.
‐ Accelerate and clean up on schedule as per NOP.

station information.

• Diversion procedures:
‐ Any flight planned with Al Udeid Air Base as alternate but carrying additional fuel for a further
alternate must contact IOC for preferred diversion aerodrome.
‐ Immediately after landing and vacating the RWY, Flight Crews shall instruct passengers to
close all window sunshades for security reasons. Window sunshades may be re-opened only
after the aircraft has taxied out to the RWY and is ready for departure.
‐ Passengers must remain onboard the aircraft for the duration of the transit. Disembarkation
would only occur if the aircraft is unable to depart or in case of a medical requirement. IOC
must be contacted for instructions if disembarkation is required.
OTBH.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Military activity and Helicopter operations at the aerodrome.
General
• Al Udeid is a Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponder may not be compatible with civil aircraft TCAS.
• SIDs and STARs are not published.
OTBH.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel RWY and
TWYs for landing RWY.
RWY 16R/34L
• VOR Approaches are offset.
OTBH.ground_maneuvering:
Note: Aircraft arresting gears installed before all RWY thresholds.
TWYs
• Use minimum power setting during movement on TWYs.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Expect to be parked on a TWY. Follow-me vehicle may be provided.
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
OTBH.departure_procedures:
Planning
• All RWYs: Takeoff roll commences 220m from RWY threshold.

OTHH.name: Hamad International
OTHH.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Hamad International.
Ground Handling and Equipment: QAS OPS - 121.050
Operations and Maintenance: IOCC/MOOC - 125.15
IOC Ops Tel: +974 4018 4500
IOC Flight Watch Tel: +974 4018 4514
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
10 H24 NIL YES YES NIL YES (1)
Note: (1) LMT-RETI for Apron 6 (600 stands) and Cargo Apron.
OTHH.company_policy:
Cabin Door Operation
• Cabin doors will be opened by ground staff on arrival in Doha. Normally, the crew are to use
the aft left hand door to disembark. The door will be opened by the ground staff after the last
passenger has disembarked. In the event the door has not been opened on time, cabin crew will
inform the PIC who will contact the TRC (Turn Around Coordinator) or QAS Ops for the same.
OTHH.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Military operations in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
3.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
4.Laser strike incident threat. Report to ATC if affected.
Prohibited Area ‘OT (P)-43’
• ATC will only clear direct routings over Prohibited Area ‘OT (P)-43’ when the area is not in use
and ATC anticipates that the aircraft will clear the area’s upper vertical boundary above FL 150.
If cleared direct routing, the Flight Crew is responsible for maintaining vertical separation from
Prohibited Area OT (P)-43.
OTHH.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Do not mistake Doha International (OTBD/DIA), located 2.8 NM to the West, for
Hamad International.
2.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWY for
landing RWY.
Note: Guided Visual Approach (GVA) not applicable for the A320 (A7-LAA onwards) and A380.
Inbound Doha
• When operating within the Bahrain (OBBB) FIR Flight Crew shall ensure that the cleared level is
set in the FCU/MCP altitude window as soon as the cleared level is received from Bahrain ATC.
This also applies to "WHEN READY DESCENT and MAINTAIN" clearances.
RWY 34L
• ILS glide slope fluctuations have been reported.
OTHH.ground_maneuvering:
Apron/Parking Stands
CAUTION Exercise vigilance while docking to stand 122 as the design of the lead in line
from TWY E4 to stand 122 is a potential threat due to the bend in TWY E4.
• Solid yellow lead-in lines with (“lead in lights”) into stands are only valid for the Center parking
position. Parking stands with dashed lines for narrow body aircraft marked with Left ‘L’ or Right
‘R’ markings do not have lead in line or lights to turn into stands and a ‘Follow me’ vehicle /
Marshalled assistance is required.
• Parking stands:
‐ The segment towards the final parking position have upslope. Exercise proper energy
management to prevent the aircraft from stopping.
‐ When parked at Apron 6 (600 stands), the parking brake must remain set ON at all times,
unless required to be released for safety reasons.
• Visual Guidance Docking System (VGDS):
‐ Exercise caution at all gates as sensitivity may differ from gate to gate.
‐ The position of the sun may blind the video camera used in the VDGS.
OTHH.departure_procedures:
General
• Several SIDs have similar designators (ALSEM, ALVEN) Flight Crews shall verify that the
cleared SID is inserted into the FMS and briefed accordingly.
• For ATC traffic sequencing. Before takeoff, on first contact with Hamad Tower, advise if a speed
higher or lower than 250 KIAS will be required on departure.
• Aircrafts departing Hamad Int, if routing via Bahrain FIR shall state the following on first contact:
QTRXXXX;
‐ ROUTING VIA.. (ALVEN/ VAXIN/LUBET OR TULUB)
‐ PASSING FL..
‐ CLIMBING TO FL..
‐ REQUESTING FINAL FL..
• Maximum available FL when routing via LUBET L934 DAVUS is FL320. Avoid requesting higher
Flight Levels on this route.
Target Startup Time TSAT
• Flights parked on stands not equipped with VDGS will receive TSAT via ACARS functionality
at TOBT – 30 min.
In the event that ACARS is not available, the Flight Crew will inform the Turnaround Coordinator
(TRC), and verbal communication of the TSAT will be provided.
ACARS message will be sent. If the TSAT has been canceled, the Flight Crew shall coordinate
with the TRC or QR IOC to generate a new TOBT in the system, and the new TSAT message
will be sent to the aircraft accordingly.
Startup and Pushback
• Request pushback only when ready.
• For engine start on stand, contact ATC at TSAT - 5 minutes.
R/T Phraseology used for issuing of Departure Clearance
• The following phraseology will be used by the ATC controllers when issuing a departure
clearance:
‐ Climb via the (SID name) to altitude (xxxx) thousand feet (Final SID altitude).
‐ Example: “QTR001 cleared to Heathrow, climb via the LUBET1E departure, 6,000 ft, Squawk
7762”.
• Follow the SID vertical profile restrictions unless instructed otherwise by ATC.
OTHH.miscellaneous:
Medical/Security Assistance
• When the requirement for assistance is identified:
‐ If the flight is more than 30 minutes from landing, contact IOC for the request.
‐ If the flight is less than 30 minutes from landing, may request directly to ATC.
Ground Equipment and APU utilization
• Aircraft parked at PLB (Passenger Loading Bridge) stands:
‐ GPU and PCA will be immediately connected upon engine shutdown.
‐ APU must be shut down on arrival provided that GPU is available.
‐ In case of inoperative GPU and/or PCA/ACU, the APU must be used.
• Aircraft parked at remote stands:
‐ The GPU and an ACU will be connected upon engine shutdown if the ground time is more
than 90 minutes.
‐ The APU must be used for flights with less than 90 minutes ground time.
‐ In case of inoperative APU, the GPU and/or PCA/ACU must be used.
Passenger Cabin Temperature Management
• If the average cabin temperature exceeds 29°C prior to passenger boarding:
‐ Contact IOC at the earliest opportunity to determine the appropriate course of action.
‐ Inform the TRC that boarding is on hold due to high cabin temperature.
• If the average cabin temperature exceeds 29°C while passengers are onboard:
‐ Actively monitor the cabin temperatures and, in coordination with the CSD/CS, take proactive
measures to ensure that passenger comfort is maintained at acceptable levels throughout
boarding process.
‐ The PIC shall make the corresponding PA anytime the cabin temperature exceeds 29°C.
Refer to the Pilot Public Address Guide.

Name of Aerodrome: Abha
Ground Handling: SAUDI GROUND SERVICES (SGS) - 131.425
Terminal: TBA
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 H24 YES NO YES NIL NO
CAUTION 1.High Density Altitude Aerodrome Operations.
2.Dust Devils (small wind/sand tornadoes) in vicinity of the aerodrome.
3.Birds in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
Note: Customs inspects the aircraft after the last passenger disembarks.
OEAB.company_policy:
• All take-offs and landings shall be performed by Captains or a Captain under training only.
• Operating Captain shall have minimum six (6) months experience on type/variant (after
FLC/Command Release Check).
• Abha is authorized for day time operations only.
• Extra fuel and minimum ground time is recommended.
OEAB.atc:
WARNING Abha aerodrome is located within the Emergency Security Control of Air Traffic
(ESCAT) area. Monitor ATC frequencies and strictly adhere to follow all ATC
instructions. ESCAT rules may activated by the Saudi authorities at any time.
CAUTION 1.Yemen border is located less than 60 NM South of Abha aerodrome.
2.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
3.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
4.Low transition altitude.
ATC Procedures
• Expect transition from JEDDAH CTA to KHAMIS MUSHAIT CTA over waypoint KATIX.
• Expect to cross waypoint KATIX at FL240 inbound to Abha and FL230 when outbound (subject
to ATC clearance).
• Contact Abha approach at least 5 mins before entering CTA/TMA and maintain listening watch
on the frequency within the CTA/TMA.
Radar Vectoring
• Expect radar vectoring for arrivals and departures.
OEAB.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Do not mistake King Khaled Airbase located 10 NM Northeast with similar RWY
alignment for Abha.
2.Turbulence and strong crosswinds may be encountered in final approach.
3.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWY for
landing RWY. Parallel TWY is also used as Emergency RWY.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• STARs not published. Expect radar vectoring.
• Ensure that all ATC clearances provide adequate terrain clearance.
RWY 13
• ILS approach:
‐ If approach is in use, expect ATC clearance to ABH VOR over waypoint DUMSI.
‐ ATC clearance may be provided for the ILS RWY 13 approach and to descend as published
without specifying from which waypoint to join the ARC. (BAREK is the IAF to start the APP).
‐ To avoid LOC false captures, it is recommended to intercept the final approach within 30º
from the final course.
RWY 31
• VOR approach is offset.
OEAB.departure_procedures:
General
• Summer: Due to a combination of aerodrome elevation and temperature, take-off performance
is impacted.
Refuelling with Pax On-board
• Fire truck must be present at the aircraft.
Startup and Pushback
• ATC clearance is usually given during taxi-out.
• Person on Board (POB) information should be advised to ATC prior to departure normally with
pushback request.
Take-Off Operating Minima
• Qatar Airways (QR) has GACA approval to use lower than standard take-off minima. Take-off
minimums depicted on Jeppesen Charts do not apply to QR operations and are to be
disregarded. Flight Crew shall refer to and apply Low Visibility Take-off Minima as per OM Part
A.

OSDI.company_policy:

• Other aerodromes in Syria - NOT AUTHORIZED.
• Use all available resources to identify and remain clear of prohibited/restricted airspace.
• All take-offs and landings shall be performed by Captain or Captain under training only.
• Operating Captain shall have minimum six (6) months experience on type/variant (after
FLC/Command Release Check).
• EGPWS, APU and TCAS must be operational prior to departure to Damascus.
• Crew should remain onboard the aircraft unless operationally required.
• Uplift of potable water and catering is not permitted.
• Fuel uplift at Damascus aerodrome is not authorized.
• Route deviations:
‐ Coordination with Damascus ATC is required prior to deviating from ATS route.
‐ IOC shall be informed.
OSDI.atc:
WARNING 1.Uncontrolled traffic activity around the aerodrome may exist. Exercise vigilance
and inform ATC.
2.TEL AVIV FIR (LLLL) border is located 40 NM Southwest of Damascus
aerodrome.
CAUTION 1.Monitor NOTAMs for NAV AIDS operational status.
2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
3.Military and Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (Drones) activity in the vicinity of the
aerodrome.
General
• Joint Civil and Military aerodrome.
• Military aircraft transponders may not be compatible with civil aircraft TCAS.
• In case of diversion using airway L513, Flight Crew to ensure not to enter the restricted airspace
located 20 NM to the West. The pictorial below is for reference only.
Approved Routes
Radio frequency
• En-route radio workload is high due to usage of multiple frequencies at the same time.
• Contact DAM ACC 5 mins before entering DAM FIR. In case of failure, contact DAM tower,
Latakia (OSLK/LTK) or Aleppo (OSAP/ALP) Tower.
OSDI.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel TWYs for
landing RWY.
2.RWY may be difficult to identify due lack of contrast with surrounding area.
General
• Expect tailwind on final approach.
STARs/Radar Vectoring
• Delayed descent may result in reduced track miles and aircraft being left high on profile.
Preferred Approach Criteria
1.RNAV (GNSS) RWY 23R.
2.OVERLAY - RNAV VISUAL RWY 23R.
3.Guided Visual RWY 05L/05R.
4.Visual approaches - All RWYs.
OSDI.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION 1.RWYs have rubber deposits on the touchdown zone.
2.FOD have been reported on ground maneuvering areas.
3.Vehicles, personnel and animals may be present in the aprons and TWYs.
General
• RWY, TWYs, apron and parking stands markings and signs are either missing or faded and
difficult to see.
• Below pictorials are for reference only.
RWY 05L/23R Overview
RWY 05R/23L Overview
RWYs
• RWY surface is rough and bumpy.
TWYs
• Adhere strictly to the center-line marking and apply judgmental steering for all turns.
Apron/Parking Stands
• Consider aircraft wingspan, TWY/apron restrictions and objects during taxi due to the proximity
of light poles, other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and
request Marshaller/Wingwalker.
• Refer below graphic depicting the location of lights poles on the apron and adjacent to the
TWYs.
Apron Overview
Passenger Operations
• Expect to be parked on Apron 2, nose-out parking.
• There are no parking stand surface markings on the apron. Follow marshaller instructions.
• Expect taxi in-via TWY B1 and taxi-out via TWY B.
Cargo Operations
• When parking on Apron 6, entry/exit is preferred via TWY E due wingtip clearance (from light
poles).
OSDI.departure_procedures:
General
• Call ATC 5 minutes before ready to start.
• Departure clearance will be received during taxi out.
Planning
• RWY 23L is the preferred RWY for departure.
De-icing
• Not available.
• If icing, frost or cold soak above limits are observed, departure must be delayed.
• Use of hot water/air are not authorized.

2.RFF CAT 10 available O/R.
3.RWY 12/30 will be closed every Monday from 11:00 to 14:00 for routine maintenance.
OMDW.atc:

2.Busy ATC environment.
3.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operation at the aerodrome .
Language/ Radio Transmission (R/T)
• High volume of R/T which demands paying close attention to ATC transmissions at all times, as
the controllers tend to speak rapidly.
• Communication with ATC should be short and precise.
OMDW.arrival_procedures:

landing RWY.
Note: Inbound traffic shall advise Dubai Arrivals on first contact: aircraft call sign, passing level,
aircraft type and if full RWY length is required.
OMDW.departure_procedures:
Local Procedures
• Departing traffic must advise Dubai Departures on first contact: call-sign and passing level.
OMDW.miscellaneous: • This is an AFP sector and the OFP will be generated automatically.

OMDB.name: Dubai International
OMDB.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Dubai International
Ground Handling: DNATA OPS: 131.600
Terminal: N/A
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
10 H24 NIL NO YES NIL YES (1)
Note: 1.(1) RETI - NOT AUTHORIZED for Code E/F Fleets.
2.Dubai International cannot be planned as DALT for Cargo operations.
3.Dubai International may be used as Destination Alternate only for PAX aircraft
scheduled to arrive:
‐ from 03:00 to 08:00,
‐ from 10:00 to 15:00,
‐ from 23:00 to 01:00.
4.In accordance with the security procedures at Dubai International aerodrome, transit
Crew members can leave and re-enter the aircraft only once.
5.Dubai International has capacity restrictions and SLOTS are enforced for
arrival/departure. Flight Crews are to comply with the scheduled arrival/departure
times to avoid incurring significant fines or penalties.
OMDB.atc:
CAUTION 1.Restricted airspace in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
2.Busy ATC environment.
3.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
General
• Arrivals peak period: 19:00 to 23:00.
ATC Slot
• ATC Slot (if issued) tolerance is +/- 2.5 minutes after/before slot time allocated.
OMDB.arrival_procedures:
CAUTION 1.Do not mistake Sharjah International (OMSJ/SHJ) located 9 NM to the East for
Dubai International aerodrome.
2.Ensure correct RWY identification for landing. Do not mistake parallel RWY and
TWYs for landing RWY.
Note: Inbound traffic must advise Dubai Arrivals Radar on first contact of their aircraft type
along with call-sign, passing level and if full RWY length is required.
Enroute Holding Details
• ATC may assign enroute holding instructions not depicted in the STAR charts. To display the
enroute holding details in the Jeppesen FD Pro X enroute map you must enable Waypoints ON
in the enroute map filters list.
RWYs
• All RNP approaches descent angle not coincident with PAPI.
Diversions
• Traffic diverting to Sharjah International, expect radar vectoring for finals to RWY in use.
OMDB.ground_maneuvering:
CAUTION TWY INCURSION THREAT. Aerodrome has a complex TWY network. Flight
Crew must familiarize themselves with the Jeppesen Airport charts and Airport
Briefing pages (taxi procedures) before operating to Dubai-International
(OMDB/DXB). Do not initiate or continue taxi unless both Flight Crew members
have received, understood and agreed on the ATC taxi clearance.
Note: 1.Parallel TWY K and taxilane Z. When taxiing to the designated parking stand after
arrival, the final turn onto the parking stand must be made from TWY K. Flight Crew
shall not crossover to taxilane Z unless specifically authorized by ATC.
2.TWY Intermediate Holding Point (IHP) signs are displayed with RED and BLACK
backgrounds. Flight Crew must cross check the location of TWY IHP points in the 10-9
charts, if in doubt stop aircraft and confirm with ATC. Below graphic for reference only.
TWY Intermediate Holding Point (IHP) signs
OMDB.departure_procedures:
Note: Flight Crews are required to contact DNATA Load Control on 131.600 to provide the
following OFP figures: Taxi fuel, Trip and Ramp fuel, RTOW, water tank level and fuel
density.
Start Up and Push-back
• DCL Procedure
‐ Service available from 30 minutes before until 30 minutes after EOBT.
‐ Flight Crew shall advise in receipt of clearance when requesting pushback.
‐ Voice message shall be used if not datalink equipped.
• “Dubai Ground” is responsible for issuing start and push-back approval, along with taxi
clearance.
• Approval to start on stand does not imply approval to push-back.
Planning
• For the purpose of performance calculations, the standard departure points are:
‐ RWY 12R: K5.
‐ RWY 12L: M1C/N1C.
‐ RWY 30R: M13/N8A.
‐ RWY 30L: K15A/M18A.
• Flight Crew should base their performance calculations on departure from standard departure
points from the notified RWY in use if there is no restriction to payload.
• ATC may use a different departure point nearer the RWY end based on the traffic situation.
• If the standard departure point is insufficient, Flight Crew must advise ATC of the required
departure point on first contact. Delays for departure may expected, based on RWY
dependency requirements.
• Requests for departures from other than the notified RWY in use may not be available. Subject
to ATC approval on a case by case basis and may subject to delays.
OMDB.miscellaneous:
• According to Local Regulations, only Captains are allowed to exit the aircraft for the external
inspection. The walk around and transit check shall be performed by the Captains only if
required.
• This is an AFP sector and the OFP will be generated automatically.

2.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
General
• Aerodrome lighting must be activated by the pilot via the CTAF frequency. The Pilot Activated
Lights (PAL) require three one-second pulses for activation (transmission to be completed within
five seconds). If PAL activation fails, contact aerodrome operations by phone on +61 3 5227
9150 for manual aerodrome lighting activation.
YMAV.arrival_procedures:
RWY 18/36
• VOR approaches are offset.
• Nil ALS.
RWY 18

YMAV.ground_maneuvering:
Apron/Parking Stand

of other aircraft, equipment and personnel. If any doubt exists, stop aircraft and request
Marshaller/Wingwalker.
YMAV.departure_procedures:
Planning
• Flight Crew should comply with all speed and/or altitude restrictions when following a SID. If
unable to meet the requirements for performance reasons, advise ATC at the time of accepting
the clearance but no later than before being cleared for takeoff.

YMML.name: Melbourne International
YMML.general:
Name of Aerodrome: Melbourne International.
Ground Handling:
• Pax: MENZIES - 131.8
• Cargo: MENZIES - 128.45
Terminal: International Terminal, Pier D.
Operating Significant Circling Visual Steep Descent
RFF RETI
Hours Terrain Approach Approach Angle
9 H24 NIL YES YES NIL YES
Note: In the event of a Death/Illness onboard in flight, Refer to 6.7.2.1.9.1 Medical Events.
COMPANY POLICY
• RWY 09/27 is authorized for landing and take-off provided:
‐ All landings shall be performed by Captains or a Captain under training only.
AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL
CAUTION 1.Helicopter, VFR and General Aviation operations at the aerodrome.
2.Model aircraft activity in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
General
• Expect speed control between 180 - 200 NM before approach.
• Violation of altitudes or ATC instructions, will likely result in ATC logging an “Incident Report”.
Delays and Busy Periods
• Anticipate airborne traffic delays of 15 minutes for arriving aircraft due to terminal area traffic
density and/or single RWY operations.
• An aircraft that has diverted and returning to Melbourne must carry “Traffic holding Fuel” during
following times:
‐ Daily 21:00 to 23:00: 15 minutes additional fuel.
‐ Daily 23:00 to 14:00: 10 minutes additional fuel.
Note: The above is for information only as per Australian CASA and that there is no
requirement to carry fuel specifically to cover such possible delays. Fuel Optimization
team is monitoring fuel burn data for all flights on a daily basis. Any statistical usage of
CONT Fuel, will be added by Flight Dispatch as Dispatch Fuel.
ARRIVAL PROCEDURES
RWY 34
• VOR approach is offset.
GROUND MANEUVERING
CAUTION Readback in full all taxi instructions issued by ATC.
Note: To minimize false alarms during Cargo hold disinfection (ex-Doha), it is recommended to
delay the starting of engines and try to avoid completely closing the cargo doors for a few
minutes after the spraying of insecticide in order to give the spray time to disperse.
TWYs
• Surface has been reported as rough, especially near RWY 34 threshold (TWY K and TWY A).
Run-up Bays
• Turn direction at run-up bay on TWY B is counterclockwise and run-up bay on TWY K is
clockwise.
Apron/Parking Stands
Cargo operations
• Southern Freight Apron:
‐ TWY J has upslope from TWY S until Southern Freight Apron.
‐ TWY J has tight turns into the Apron.
‐ RETI – NOT AUTHORIZED for taxing on this apron.
‐ Exercise proper energy management to prevent aircraft from stopping.
APU and GPU usage
• APU usage allowed on parking bays.
DEPARTURE PROCEDURES
General
• Fuel figures to be informed via ACARS to GHA.
Startup and Pushback
• Confirm Pre Departure Clearance (PDC) clearance to the controller via voice communication.
• Advise ATC when ready for pushback. Pushback is coordinated by ATC directly with tow-tug
driver.
• Provide parked position number to ATC on acknowledgement of AWY clearance.
• Readback of SID and transition is required.
• ATC advise(s) Flight Crew of a pushback disconnect point and tail direction (North, South, East
or West) when pushing back onto a TWY.
• Tugs (pushback tractors): Will maintain listening watch 121.7, but will not be issued a
disconnect point via ATC, the only direct ATC contact tugs will have during a pushback is after
disconnecting from aircraft and proceeding to cross a TWY.
RWY Preference
• If RWY 09/27 is required for use, coordinate with dispatch due performance restriction.
• Turn from TWY T to TWY A has significant upslope.
SID
• Comply with all Speed and/or Altitude restrictions when following a SID. If unable to meet the
requirements for performance reasons, ATC should be informed at the time of accepting the
clearance but no later than before being cleared for take-off.
MISCELLANEOUS
NIL
